| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, HOPPER] |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| NumberOfAllowedProducts | int | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  |  | 1 |  | The number of different products that can be loaded into this compartment simultaneously. | No | No | [Integer] | [HOPPER] |  |
| AllowedProductName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | Products |  | [HOPPER] |  |  |  |  |  | The products that can be loaded in this compartment. Blank means that all products are allowed. Single product name, groups and named filters can be used. |  |  | [NaN] | [] | ProductName |

### シート: CustomerDemand

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| CustomerName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Customers | Yes | [NEO, TRIAD, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The name of the Customer. Customer Groups are supported; the Group behavior will be Enumerate. | [NaN] | [TRIAD, NEO] | CustomerName |
| ProductName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Products | Yes | [NEO, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The name of the Product requested by the Customer. Product Groups are supported; the Group behavior will be Enumerate. | [NaN] | [NEO] | ProductName |
| ProductNameGroupBehavior | [Enumerate, Aggregate] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Enumerate |  | If  product group is entered in the Product Name field, the Product Name Group Behavior field allows you to specify if demand records should be generated for every individual product in the group (Enumerate) or if any product contained in the group would be able to satisfy the demand record (Aggregate). In the case that Aggregate is used, the flows into the customer location for all products contained in the product group will be summed, and this total quantity is used to evaluate the demand record. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| PeriodName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Periods | Yes | [NEO, CYCLO] |  |  | Model Horizon |  | The Period in which the demand record occurs. Period Groups are supported; the Group behavior will be enumerate. | [NaN] | [NEO] | PeriodName |
| Quantity | double | PositiveNumeric | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO, TRIAD, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The amount of the Product requested by the Customer in the specified Period. | [Quantity] | [TRIAD, NEO] |  |
| MinQuantity | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The minimum amount of Product required to satisfy the Customer's demand in the specified Period. This value is used to turn demand satisfaction into a soft constraint; if no value is entered, it is assumed that Min Quantity is equal to Quantity. | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| MaxQuantity | double | PositiveNumeric |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The maximum amount of Product that the Customer can receive in the specified Period. This value is used to turn demand satisfaction into a soft constraint; if no value is entered, it is assumed that Max Quantity is equal to Quantity. | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| QuantityUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | Quantity |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that measures Quantity, Min Quantity, and Max Quantity. Quantity, Volume, and Weight units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO, TRIAD, CYCLO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the Demand record exists in the model. If Status is set to Exclude, this  record will be ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [TRIAD, NEO] |  |
| DemandStatus | [Include, Exclude, Consider] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Include |  | Demand Status dictates whether or not the Demand record must be considered in the optimization run. If the Demand Status is set to Consider, the demand record can be serviced if it is optimal to do so, and if it is chosen not to be served then any associated penalty costs will be applied. If the Demand Status is set to Exclude then the demand record will be forced into a Not Served status and any associated Penalty Cost will be applied. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ServiceMode | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  | Yes | TransportationModes |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | Optionally, a required Transportation Mode can be specified here. If multiple Modes are acceptable, a Mode Group may be used; the Group behavior will be Aggregate. | [NaN] | [] | ModeName |
| DeliveryFrequency | int | PositiveNumeric |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The Delivery Frequency can be used as a way to generate shipment sizes for inbound arcs to customers. If the Shipment Size field is left empty in the Transportation Policies table for an inbound lane to a Customer and Delivery Frequency is populated, the Shipment Size will be applied as Demand Quantity / Delivery Frequency. This is especially useful when your costs are using rate cards entered in the Transportation Band Costing table. | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| UnitPrice | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The price that the Customer will pay for each unit of the Product. This price overrides the Unit Price given in the Products table. | [Cost] | [] |  |
| UnitPriceUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | UnitPrice |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines Unit Price. Quantity, Volume, and Weight units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| PenaltyCost | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The penalty cost associated with not fulfilling a unit of demand | [Cost] | [] |  |
| PenaltyCostUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | PenaltyCost |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines Penalty Cost. Quantity, Volume, and Weight units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, TRIAD, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [TRIAD, NEO] |  |

| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| CustomerName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Customers | Yes | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The name of the Customer. Customer Groups are supported; the Group behavior will be Enumerate. | [NaN] | [] | CustomerName |
| ProductName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Products | Yes | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The name of the Product requested by the Customer. Product Groups are supported; the Group behavior will be Enumerate. | [NaN] | [] | ProductName |
| PeriodName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Periods | Yes | [NEO] |  |  | Model Horizon |  | The Period in which the demand record occurs. Period Groups are supported; the Group behavior will be enumerate. | [NaN] | [] | PeriodName |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the Origin Policy record exists in the model. If Status is set to Exclude, this  record will be ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ProductOriginLocation | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  | Yes | Facilities, Suppliers, Sources |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | An origin location for the product can be specified here. Groups are supported in this field, with the Group Behavior being treated as Aggregate. | [NaN] | [] | [Facilities.FacilityName, Suppliers.SupplierName] |
| ProductOriginLocationGroupBehavior | [Enumerate, Aggregate] |  |  |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Aggregate |  | If ProductOriginLocation Name is a Group, use this field to specify the Group's behavior. If Enumerate is selected, an instance of this constraint will be created for each member of the Group. If Aggregate is selected, flow in the constraint will be summed over the members of the Group to satisfy demand. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Quantity | double | NonNegative | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The amount of the Product requested by the Customer in the specified Period that this Origin Location policy will apply for. If you want to prevent an origin location from being used to service a demand record, you can enter a quantity of 0. | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| QuantityUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | Quantity |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that measures Quantity, Min Quantity, and Max Quantity. Quantity, Volume, and Weight units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |

### シート: CustomerFulfillmentPolicies

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| CustomerName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Customers | Yes | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The Customer that the policy record applies to. | [NaN] | [THROG] | CustomerName |
| ProductName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Products | Yes | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The Product (or Product group) that the policy record applies to. | [NaN] | [THROG] | ProductName |
| SourceName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Facilities, Suppliers, Sources | Yes | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The source Facility or Supplier (Groups are also supported) that the policy record applies to. | [NaN] | [THROG] | [Facilities.FacilityName, Suppliers.SupplierName] |
| OptimizationPolicy | [To Optimize, By Ratio (Auto Scale), By Ratio (No Scale), Single Source] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO, CYCLO] |  |  | To Optimize |  | The logic that order fulfillments for the Customer, Product, and Source combination(s) must follow. To Optimize selects the lowest cost option. By Ratio (Auto Scale) will enforce a flow split between the specified sources, the defined ratios will apply to flow into the customer from the listed sources only. By Ratio (No Scale) will enforce a flow split between the specified sources and the defined ratios will be in terms of the total inbound flow into the Customer. Single Source policies force the Customer to receive the Product from one Source. Note that if a group is used in the Customer Name, Product Name, or Source Name field that the groups will be enumerated and the policy logic will be applied over the enumerated entries. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| OptimizationPolicyValue | double | PositiveNumeric |  | OptimizationPolicy = By Ratio (Auto Scale) OR By Ratio (No Scale) |  |  |  | [NEO, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | By Ratio (Auto Scale): The Policy Value will define the percentage of flow that must be satisfied from the listed source. The percentage will be calculated based on the total inbound flow for the customer/product combination and enforced proportionally across the sources with a policy of By Ratio (Auto Scale).  By Ratio (No Scale): The Policy Value will define the percentage of flow that must be satisfied from the listed source. The percentage will be calculated based on the total inbound flow for the customer/product combination  and enforced over the listed sources. If the Policy Values add up to less than 100, the remainder of unaccounted flow can be sourced from any other viable lane. If the Policy Values add up to more than 100, they will be scaled to 100 and enforced the same as Auto Scale. | [Percentage] | [] |  |
| SimulationPolicy | [By Preference, Single Source, Allocation, By Distance] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | By Preference |  | The logic that order assignment flows for the Customer, Product, and Source combination(s) must follow. By Preference will pick the sources pending product availability in the specified order of preference. Single Source policies force the Customer to receive the Product from one Source. Allocation will source as much as possible from the sources based on availability in the specified order of preference - this is similar to By Preference but the Allocation policy will not attempt to keep orders together from a single source. By Distance will select the closest source using straight-line distances. | [NaN] | [THROG] |  |
| SimulationPolicyValue | double | PositiveNumeric |  | SimulationPolicy = By Preference |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | By Preference: Policy Value defines the order of preference in which a source will be selected pending product availability, with the lower numbers representing a higher selection preference. Allocation: Policy value defines the order in which sources will be selected. | [Percentage] | [THROG] |  |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the policy exists in the model. If Status is set to Exclude, the policy record is ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [THROG] |  |
| UnitCost | Cost Time Expression | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE StepCosts.StepCostBehavior = [All Item, Incremental, Stepwise] |  |  |  | StepCosts |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | 0 | Optimization: Currently supports doubles and named step costs that are defined in the StepCosts table where StepCostBehavior = All Item, Incremental or Stepwise or a combination of Stepwise and All Item   Simulation: Currently supports doubles | The cost incurred per unit of flow for the specified Source/Customer/Product combination. | [Cost] | [] | StepCostName |
| UnitCostUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | UnitCost |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines flow for the Source/Customer/Product combination. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| MinimumOrderQuantity | double | PositiveNumeric |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The minimum quantity that must move between source and destination in a period for a flow to occur | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| MinimumOrderQuantityUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | MinimumOrderQuantity |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines the MOQ for the Source/Customer/Product combination. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| LotSize | double | PositiveNumeric |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | Orders placed from the Customer to the specified sources must be filled in multiples of the specified lot size. | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| LotSizeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | LotSize |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines the Lot Size for the Source/Customer/Product combination. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| MaxSourcingRange | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The maximum allowable distance between Customer and Source for the Product(s) specified. This field is intended for use in models with group policies to eliminate excessively long lanes from consideration. If all sources exceed the max sourcing range for a Customer-Product pairing, the closest source will be kept by default - this behavior can be overwritten through the Keep Closest Lane For Max Sourcing Distance run option. | [Distance] | [] |  |
| MaxSourcingRangeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Distance] |  | MaxSourcingRange |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Distance] |  | {PrimaryDistanceUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Distance) that defines Max Sourcing Range. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| OnlySourceFromSurplus | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | 0 |  | If True, when selecting a source for the Customer / Product combination only sources that have available surplus can be selected to fulfill the order. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [THROG] |  |

Single Source policies force the Customer to receive the Product from one Source. Note that if a group is used in the Period Name, Customer Name, Product Name, or Source Name field that the groups will be enumerated and the policy logic will be applied over the enumerated entries. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| OptimizationPolicyValue | double | PositiveNumeric |  | OptimizationPolicy = By Ratio (Auto Scale) OR By Ratio (No Scale) |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | By Ratio (Auto Scale): The Policy Value will define the percentage of flow that must be satisfied from the listed source. ### シート: CustomerOrderProfiles

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| OrderID | String |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The unique identifier for each Order profile, as orders are generated based on their recurring properties they will be indexed off of the entered order number. All Orders will be placed as single line item orders, however multiple recurring orders can be set at the same Customer | [NaN] | [] |  |
| CustomerName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  |  | Customers |  | [THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The name of the Customer placing the Order | [NaN] | [] | CustomerName |
| ProductName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  |  | Products |  | [THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The Product that the Order will be placed for | [NaN] | [] | ProductName |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the Order profile exists in the model. If Status is set to Exclude, this  record will be ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Quantity | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The amount of Product being ordered by the Customer. Both doubles, as well as distributions are accepted as entries. | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| QuantityUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  | Quantity |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines Quantity. Quantity, Volume, and Weight units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| NumberOfOrders | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  | 1 |  | Based on the information entered in the Start Date, Time Between Orders and End Date fields, the Order Profile will generate orders based at different times throughout the simulation. When an order is generated, the Number Of Orders will be evaluated to determine how many orders are going to be placed at that specific time. Once the Number Of Orders is determined, all of the other information specified in the Product Name, Quantity, Number of Lines will be used to generate the details for each of the orders at that time. | [Integer] | [] |  |
| NumberOfLines | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  | 1 |  | If the Product Name is left blank for the Order Profile, recurring orders can be generated with a varying number of line items in each order. An integer value, or a distribution can be entered in this field. In the event that the number of lines in an order is 2 or greater, the Order Profile Product Selection and Order Profile Product Affinity tables will be used to determine which products are to be ordered. | [Integer] | [] |  |
| ServiceLevel | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | Service Level will be used to calculate the Due Date for all of the generated recurring orders. Due Date will be set to the Order Date + Service Level. | [Time] | [] |  |
| ServiceLevelUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  | ServiceLevel |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) used to define Service Level. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| StartDate | Date/Time |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The Start Date will defined the date that the first order will drop. Orders will drop exactly at the date/time specified in Start Date, and then each following order's drop date will be determined by the Time Between Orders information. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| TimeBetweenOrders | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | Time Between Orders will specify how much time goes by from the previous order until the next order drops. Both doubles, as well as distribution entries are supported. | [Time] | [] |  |
| TimeBetweenOrdersUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  | TimeBetweenOrders |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) used to define Time Between Orders. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| EndDate | Date/Time |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The End Date will specify the last possible time that an order can drop. If the Time Between Orders causes the next order drop date to fall after the specified End Date, that last order will not be generated. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |

### シート: CustomerOrders

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| OrderID | String - Fixed |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The unique identifier for each Order; an Order consists of one or more Line Items. | [NaN] | [THROG] |  |
| LineItemID | String - Fixed |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The unique identifier for the single Line Item being defined; Orders with multiple Line Items will require multiple table records. | [NaN] | [THROG] |  |
| CustomerName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  |  | Customers |  | [THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The name of the Customer placing the Order. Customer Groups are supported; the Group behavior will be Enumerate. | [NaN] | [THROG] | CustomerName |
| ProductName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  |  | Products |  | [THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The Product in the Line Item. | [NaN] | [THROG] | ProductName |
| Quantity | double | NonNegative | Yes |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The amount of Product requested by the Customer. | [Quantity] | [THROG] |  |
| QuantityUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | Quantity |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines Quantity. Quantity, Volume, and Weight units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| OrderDate | Date/Time |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The date and time at which the Customer places the Order. | [NaN] | [THROG] |  |
| DueDate | Date/Time |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The date and time at which the Order is due at the Customer. | [NaN] | [THROG] |  |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the Line Item exists in the model. If Status is set to Exclude, this  record will be ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [THROG] |  |
| UnitPrice | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The price that the Customer will pay for each unit of the Product. This price overrides the Unit Price given in the Products table. | [Cost] | [] |  |
| UnitPriceUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | UnitPrice |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines Unit Price. Quantity, Volume, and Weight units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| ServiceMode | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | TransportationModes |  | [] |  |  |  |  | Optionally, a required Transportation Mode can be specified here. If multiple Modes are acceptable, a Mode Group may be used; the Group behavior will be Aggregate. If NULL, any Mode is assumed to be acceptable. | [NaN] | [] | ModeName |
| SourceName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | Facilities |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | Optionally, a required Source for the Line Item can be specified here. If multiple Sources are acceptable, a Source (Facility or Supplier) Group may be used; the Group behavior will be Aggregate. If NULL, any Source is assumed to be acceptable. | [NaN] | [] | FacilityName |
| SingleSourceOrder | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | If True, all Line Items in the Order must be sourced from a single site (Facility or Supplier). If False, each Line Item within the Order may be sourced from a different site. A value of True in this field overrides Single Source Line Items = False, enforcing that all Line Items in the order come from a single source. This entry will override the properties set in the Customers table | [NaN] | [] |  |
| SingleSourceLineItems | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | If True, the entire Line Item must be sourced from a single site (Facility or Supplier). If False, the Line Item may be sourced from different sites. This entry will override the properties set in the Customers table | [NaN] | [] |  |
| AllowPartialFillOrders | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | If True, the Order may be partially filled. Otherwise, it must be filled in full. If backordering is allowed, the remaining quantity may be satisfied in the future with additional shipments; if the Backorder Time Limit is reached on a partially filled order, the remaining quantity will be cancelled. If Partial Fill Orders is False, Partial Fill Line Items will also be forced to False. This entry will override the properties set in the Customers table | [NaN] | [] |  |
| AllowPartialFillLineItems | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | If True, the specified Line Item may be partially filled. Otherwise, the Line Item must be filled in full. If backordering is allowed, the remaining quantity of partially filled Line Items may be satisfied in the future with additional shipments; if the Backorder Time Limit is reached on a partially filled Line Item, the remaining quantity will be cancelled. This entry will override the properties set in the Customers table | [NaN] | [] |  |
| AllowDropShip | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | If True, orders placed by the Customer that cannot be filled directly by its fulfillment source(s) can be directly filled by an alternative source (Supplier or Facility). | [NaN] | [] |  |
| AllowBackorders | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | If True, the Customer will accept a backorder in the event that the Order cannot be fulfilled before its Due Date. If False and if the Order is not fulfilled by its Due Date, it will be cancelled on that date. This entry will override the properties set in the Customers table | [NaN] | [] |  |
| BackorderTimeLimit | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | If Allow Backorders = True, this value is the amount of time after which the backorder will be cancelled by the Customer. A value of NULL will be interpreted as an infinite time limit, while a value of zero is equivalent to setting Allow Backorders = False. This entry will override the properties set in the Customers table | [Time] | [] |  |
| BackorderTimeLimitUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | BackorderTimeLimit |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) used to define Backorder Time Limit. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [THROG] |  |

| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| RiskProfileName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | RiskRatingConfigurations |  | [DART, NEO, TRIAD, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The name of the Risk Profile that the risk configuration is saved for. Risk Profiles are configured to run using specified source data in the Risk Rating Configurations table, and will take the risk weightings and bands associated with the Risk Profile from each of the configuration tables. | [NaN] | [] | RiskProfileName |
| GeographicRiskWeight | double | NonNegative | Yes |  |  |  |  | [DART, NEO, TRIAD, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The weight applied to the Geographic Risk when calculating the Customer Risk Score. Detailed Geographic Risks configurations on geographic risk components are entered in the Geographic Risk Configurations table. | [Risk] | [] |  |
| UserDefinedRiskWeight | double | NonNegative | Yes |  |  |  |  | [DART, NEO, TRIAD, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The weight applied to the User Defined Risk when calculating the Customer Risk Score. | [Risk] | [] |  |
| ConcentrationRiskWeight | double | NonNegative | Yes |  |  |  |  | [DART, NEO, TRIAD, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The weight applied to the Concentration Risk when calculating the Customer Risk Score. | [Risk] | [] |  |

Risk Bands are defined in the Risk Band Definitions table. | [NaN] | [] | BandName |
| SourceCountRiskWeight | double | NonNegative | Yes |  |  |  |  | [DART, NEO, TRIAD, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The weight applied to the Source Count Risk when calculating the Customer Risk Score. | [Risk] | [] |  |
| SourceCountRiskBand | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  |  | RiskBandDefinitions |  | [DART, NEO, TRIAD, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The Risk Band that is used to evaluate the Source Count Risk at the Customer. Risk Bands are defined in the Risk Band Definitions table. | [NaN] | [] | BandName |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [DART, NEO, TRIAD, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |

If False, each Line Item within the Orders may be sourced from a different site. A value of True in this field overrides Single Source Line Items = False, enforcing that all Line Items in the orders come from a single source. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| SingleSourceLineItems | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | False |  | If True, the entire Line Item in orders placed from this Customer must be sourced from a single site (Facility or Supplier). If False, the Line Items may be sourced from different sites. | [NaN] | [] |  |

| BackorderTimeLimit | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | This value is the amount of time after which unsatisfied backorders will be cancelled by the Customer. A value of NULL will be interpreted as an infinite time limit, while a value of 0 is equivalent to setting Allow Backorders = False. | [Time] | [] |  |

| AllowPartialFillOrders | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | If True, orders placed by the Customer may be partially filled. Otherwise, orders must be filled in full. If backordering is allowed, the remaining quantity of partially filled orders may be satisfied in the future with additional shipments; if the Backorder Time Limit is reached on a partially filled order, the remaining quantity will be cancelled. If Partial Fill Orders is False, Partial Fill Line Items will also be forced to False. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| AllowPartialFillLineItems | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | If True, line items within orders placed by the Customer may be partially filled. Otherwise, line items must be filled in full. If backordering is allowed, the remaining quantity of partially filled line items may be satisfied in the future with additional shipments; if the Backorder Time Limit is reached on a partially filled line item, the remaining quantity will be cancelled. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| AllowDirectShip | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  | False |  | If True, orders placed by the Customer that cannot be filled directly by its fulfillment source(s) can be shipped directly to the customer from an alternative location (Supplier or Facility) | [NaN] | [] |  |
| QueuePriority | int |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | For any Queues that are evaluated with a Queueing Priority Logic, there can be a By Location component to the priority logic used. If By Location is used, The Queue Priority fields in the Customers, Facilities and Suppliers table will be used to determine how the queues will be ordered | [Integer] | [] |  |

If no schedule is specified, the Customer is assumed to be open 24 hours per day. | [NaN] | [] | ScheduleName |

| FixedDeliveryTime | Cost Time Expression | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | The fixed time required at the delivery locations for this Customer. This will be additive to the FixedDeliveryTime specified in the Shipments, Facilities and Transportation Assets tables. | [Time] | [] |  |
| FixedDeliveryTimeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | FixedDeliveryTime |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [HOPPER] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines Fixed Delivery Time | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| UnitPickupTime | Cost Time Expression | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | The time required per unit to be loaded at this Customer. This will be additive to any UnitPickupTime that is specified in the Shipments, Facilities and Transportation Assets tables. | [Time] | [] |  |
| UnitPickupTimeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | UnitPickupTime |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [HOPPER] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines Unit Pickup Time. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| UnitDeliveryTime | Cost Time Expression | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | The time required per unit to be delivered at this location. This will be additive to any UnitDeliveryTime that is specified in the Shipments, Facilities and Transportation Assets tables. | [Time] | [] |  |

Quantity, Volume, and Weight units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |

For more detailed information on Geographic Risk, reference the Geographic Risk Metrics output table. | [Risk] | [] |  |
| UserDefinedRiskScore | double |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, TRIAD] |  |  |  |  | An optional input that can be leveraged by DART when calculating Customer Risk. If left empty for some records, the User Defined Risk will not be considered for those specific Customers. The weighting for this component will be defined in the Customer Risk Configurations table, and any outputs will be reported alongside other geographic risk scores in the Geographic Risk Metrics table. | [Risk] | [] |  |

| OperatingCalendar | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | BusinessCalendars |  | [HOPPER] |  |  | None |  | The calendar (defined in the Business Calendars table) dictating which days of the year the Customer will shut down. If no calendar is specified, the Customer is assumed not to shut down. | [NaN] | [] | CalendarName |

### シート: CustomersMultiTimePeriod

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

Changes that are made in the Multi Time Period table will be applied as overrides for the records of the base table for the specified periods only. Additionally, only the fields that contain information needing to be updated are required to be entered, in the absence of data in the Multi Time Period table the data from the standard table will be persisted | [NaN] | [] | PeriodName |
| CustomerName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Customers | Yes | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The name of the Customer | [NaN] | [] | CustomerName |

| CustomerStatus | [Include, Exclude] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | Customer Status dictates whether or not the Customer exists in the model during the specified period(s).If the Customer is included in the model, all of its demand records are enforced based on the Min QTY/Max QTY specified in the Customer Demand table. If the Customer Status is set to Exclude, then any demand records for this customer in that period are ignored and there are no penalty costs applied for lost demand. If penalty costs need to be enforced, the Customer Demand Status can be leveraged in the Customer Demand table. | [NaN] | [] |  |

| SingleSourceLineItems | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | If True, each line item in an order must come from a single source. Otherwise, a line item may be satisfied by multiple sources. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| AllowBackorders | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | If True, the Customer accepts backorders in the event that an order cannot be fulfilled before its Due Date (defined in the Customer Orders table). | [NaN] | [] |  |
| BackorderTimeLimit | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | This value is the amount of time after which unsatisfied backorders will be cancelled by the Customer. A value of NULL will be interpreted as an infinite time limit, while a value of 0 is equivalent to setting Allow Backorders = False. | [Time] | [] |  |

| AllowPartialFillOrders | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | If True, orders placed by the Customer may be partially filled. Otherwise, orders must be filled in full. The remaining quantity of partially filled orders may be satisfied in the future with additional shipments; if the Backorder Time Limit is reached on a partially filled order, the remaining quantity will be cancelled. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| AllowPartialFillLineItems | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | If True, line items within orders placed by the Customer may be partially filled. Otherwise, line items must be filled in full. The remaining quantity of partially filled line items may be satisfied in the future with additional shipments; if the Backorder Time Limit is reached on a partially filled line item, the remaining quantity will be cancelled. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| AllowDirectShip | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | If True, orders placed by the Customer that cannot be filled directly by its fulfillment source(s) will be filled by an alternative source (Supplier or Facility) | [NaN] | [] |  |
| QueuePriority | int |  |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | For any Queues that are evaluated with a Queueing Priority Logic, there can be a By Location component to the priority logic used. If By Location is used, The Queue Priority fields in the Customers, Facilities and Suppliers table will be used to determine how the queues will be ordered | [Integer] | [] |  |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |

| CustomerName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Customers | Yes | [NEO] |  |  | ALL |  | The Customer(s) which we are constraining service. Groups are supported. | [NaN] | [] | CustomerName |
| CustomerNameGroupBehavior | [Enumerate, Aggregate] |  |  |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Aggregate |  | If Customer Name is a Group, use this field to specify the Group's behavior. If Enumerate is selected, an instance of this constraint will be created for each member of the Group. If Aggregate is selected, the flow in the constraint will be summed over the members of the Group. | [NaN] | [] |  |

If Enumerate is selected, an instance of this constraint will be created for each member of the Group. If Aggregate is selected, the flow in the constraint will be summed over the members of the Group. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| SourceName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  | Yes | Facilities, Suppliers, Sources | Yes | [NEO] |  |  | ALL |  | The Source(s) which we are constraining service from. Groups are supported. | [NaN] | [] | [Facilities.FacilityName, Suppliers.SupplierName] |
| SourceNameGroupBehavior | [Enumerate, Aggregate] |  |  |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Aggregate |  | If Source Name is a Group, use this field to specify the Group's behavior. If Enumerate is selected, an instance of this constraint will be created for each member of the Group. If Aggregate is selected, the flow in the constraint will be summed over the members of the Group. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ModeName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  | Yes | TransportationModes | Yes | [NEO] |  |  | ALL |  | The Mode(s) which we are constraining service over. Groups are supported. | [NaN] | [] | ModeName |
| ModeNameGroupBehavior | [Enumerate, Aggregate] |  |  |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Aggregate |  | If Mode Name is a Group, use this field to specify the Group's behavior. If Enumerate is selected, an instance of this constraint will be created for each member of the Group. If Aggregate is selected, the flow in the constraint will be summed over the members of the Group. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| PeriodName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  | Yes | Periods | Yes | [NEO] |  |  | ALL |  | The Period(s) in which we are constraining service. Groups are supported. | [NaN] | [] | PeriodName |
| PeriodNameGroupBehavior | [Enumerate, Aggregate] |  |  |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Aggregate |  | If Period Name is a Group, use this field to specify the Group's behavior. If Enumerate is selected, an instance of this constraint will be created for each member of the Group. If Aggregate is selected, the flow in the constraint will be summed over the members of the Group. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| TransitBand | double | NonNegative | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | Transit Bands can be defined for Customer/Product pairings on the basis of either time or distance. Transit Bands force orders to be fulfilled by Facilities within a particular travel time or distance from the Customer. | [Distance] | [] |  |
| TransitBandUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Distance, Time] |  | TransitBand | Yes | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Distance, Time] |  | {PrimaryDistanceUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines Transit Band. Time and Distance units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| BandPercentage | double | Percentage | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The percentage of service that must occur within the Service Band for the specified Customer/Product combination. | [Percentage] | [] |  |
| BandPercentageUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | BandPercentage | Yes | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines Band Percentage. Quantity, Volume, and Weight units of measure are supported. The default UOM will be the primary Quantity unit of measure. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the constraint exists in the model. If Status is set to Exclude, this constraint (or constraints in the case of Enumerated Groups) will be ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |

### シート: CustomSimulationScripts

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| CustomScriptName | Name |  | Yes |  | Yes |  | Yes | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The name of the custom script. Depending on the Script Policy Type, this name can be entered into the SIM Policy field of the Transportation, Production, Inventory, Customer Fulfillment, Replenishment or Procurement Policy tables. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| CustomScriptPolicyType | [Inventory, Sourcing, Transportation, Production, OnModelStart, RecurringEvent] |  | Yes |  |  |  | Yes | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The type of policy the script has been built to enforce. If the policy is set to Transportation, Production, Inventory or Sourcing then the Script Name must be referenced in the corresponding Policy table. If the Policy Type is set to be On Model Start, it will execute at the beginning of the model. If the Policy Type is set to Recurring Event it will begin on the entered Start Date, will be repeatedly executed based on the Time Between Events, and then will stop on the End Date. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ExecutablePath | String |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The path to where the python file is saved. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| StartDate | Date/Time* |  |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | For a Policy Type of Recurring Event, the Start Date will specify the first point in the simulation at which the script will execute. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| TimeBetweenEvents | double |  |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | For a Policy Type of Recurring Event, the Time Between Events will specify how the interval at which the policy will be evaluated. | [Time] | [] |  |
| TimeBetweenEventsUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The unit of measure that time is evaluated in. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| EndDate | Date/Time* |  |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | For a Policy Type of Recurring Event, the End Date will specify the last point in the simulation at which the script will execute. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |
