### シート: Facilities

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| FacilityName | Name |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, TRIAD, HOPPER, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The name of the Facility | [NaN] | [TRIAD, THROG, DENDRO, NEO, HOPPER] |  |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, TRIAD, HOPPER, CYCLO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the Facility will be included in the scenario run. If the Status is set to Exclude, all elements of the Facility will be ignored and any mentions of the Facility in other input tables will be ignored as well | [NaN] | [TRIAD, THROG, DENDRO, NEO, HOPPER] |  |
| FacilityStatus | [Open, Closed, Consider] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, TRIAD] |  |  | Open |  | Facility Status dictates whether or not the Facility is to be actively included in the model. If the Facility Status is set to Consider, the Facility may either be opened (Initial State = Potential) or closed (Initial State = Existing) depending on what is optimal. If the Facility Status is set to be  excluded from the model, any associated closing costs will still be applied and any records for this Facility in other tables will be ignored. | [NaN] | [TRIAD] |  |
| InitialState | [Existing, Potential] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Existing |  | If Initial State = Existing, the Facility currently exists in the network. If Initial State = Potential, the Facility will be considered for inclusion in the network during the model run. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Organization | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | Organizations |  | [CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The Organization which the Facility belongs to. Organizations are defined in the Organizations table. | [NaN] | [] | OrganizationName |
| Address | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, TRIAD, HOPPER, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The street address of the Facility. | [NaN] | [TRIAD] |  |
| City | String - Fixed |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, TRIAD, HOPPER, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The city of the Facility. | [NaN] | [TRIAD, THROG, DENDRO, NEO, HOPPER] |  |
| Region | String - Fixed |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, TRIAD, HOPPER, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The state/region of the Facility. | [NaN] | [TRIAD, THROG, DENDRO, NEO, HOPPER] |  |
| PostalCode | String - Fixed |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, TRIAD, HOPPER, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The ZIP/postal Code of the Facility. | [NaN] | [TRIAD, THROG, DENDRO, NEO, HOPPER] |  |
| Country | String - Fixed |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, TRIAD, HOPPER, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The country of the Facility. | [NaN] | [TRIAD, THROG, DENDRO, NEO, HOPPER] |  |
| Latitude | double | LatitudeValue |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, TRIAD, HOPPER, CYCLO] |  |  | 0 |  | The latitude coordinate of the Facility. | [GeoCoordinate] | [TRIAD, THROG, DENDRO, NEO, HOPPER] |  |
| Longitude | double | LongitudeValue |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, TRIAD, HOPPER, CYCLO] |  |  | 0 |  | The longitude coordinate of the Facility. | [GeoCoordinate] | [TRIAD, THROG, DENDRO, NEO, HOPPER] |  |
| FixedStartupCost | Cost Time Expression | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE StepCosts.StepCostBehavior = [Stepwise] |  |  |  | StepCosts |  | [NEO] |  |  | 0 | Optimization: Currently supports doubles and named step costs that are defined in the StepCosts table where StepCostBehavior = Stepwise | If Status = Consider and Initial State = Potential, the Fixed Startup Cost will be incurred if the Facility is opened during the model solve. No startup cost will be incurred if the facility already exists. Fixed Startup Cost may be a single value or a step function of the Facility's throughput. If a step function is used, this cost will be determined based on the largest throughput recorded in any of the periods for which the Facility will be operating once opened. | [Cost] | [] | StepCostName |
| FixedOperatingCost | Cost Time Expression | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE StepCosts.StepCostBehavior = [Stepwise] |  |  |  | StepCosts |  | [NEO, TRIAD] |  |  | 0 | Optimization: Currently supports doubles and named step costs that are defined in the StepCosts table where StepCostBehavior = Stepwise | The fixed cost associated with operating the Facility; Fixed Operating Cost may be a single value or a step function of the Facility's throughput. If a step function is used, this cost will be determined using the total throughput over each period. Step costs can be defined in the Step Costs table and referenced by name here. | [Cost] | [NEO] | StepCostName |
| FixedClosingCost | Cost Time Expression | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE StepCosts.StepCostBehavior = [Stepwise] |  |  |  | StepCosts |  | [NEO] |  |  | 0 | Optimization: Currently supports doubles and named step costs that are defined in the StepCosts table where StepCostBehavior = Stepwise | If Status = Consider and Initial State = Existing, the Fixed Closing Cost will be incurred if the Facility is closed during the model solve. Fixed Closing Cost may be a single value or a step function of the Facility's throughput. If a step function is used, this cost will be determined based on the largest throughput recorded in any of the periods for which the Facility was operating. Step costs can be defined in the Step Costs table and referenced by name here. | [Cost] | [] | StepCostName |
| StorageCapacity | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO,THROG] |  |  |  |  | This value indicates the total storage capacity (across products) of the Facility over each period in the horizon. Total Storage is measured as the Ending Inventory at the Facility, calculated as Max Turns Estimated Inventory + Ending Prebuild Inventory. Period-specific capacities can be entered in the Facilities Multi Time Period table and product-specific capacities can be modeled using inventory constraints. | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| StorageCapacityUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | StorageCapacity |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO,THROG] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure used to define the Storage Capacity of the Facility. Quantity, weight, and volume units of measure defined in the Units Of Measure table are accepted. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| ThroughputCapacity | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, TRIAD] |  |  |  |  | This value indicates the total allowable throughput (across products) of the Facility over each period in the horizon. Period-specific capacities can be entered in the Facilities Multi Time Period table and product-specific limits can be modeled using flow constraints. | [Quantity] | [TRIAD, NEO] |  |
| ThroughputCapacityUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | ThroughputCapacity |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure used to define the Throughput Capacity of the Facility. Quantity, weight, and volume units of measure defined in the Units Of Measure table are accepted. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| ThroughputCapacityPeriod | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | For Simulation runs, this field will specify the length of time over which the throughput capacity will be applied. For example, the Throughput Capacity might be set to 500 EA with Capacity Period = 1 and Capacity Period UOM = DAY - this would mean that the Facility could process no more than 500 units in any day. The first period will be evaluated at the Simulation Start Time, and throughput capacity will be evaluated over the specified period length. Capacity is evaluated over individual capacity periods, a rolling capacity is not considered. | [Time] | [] |  |
| ThroughputCapacityPeriodUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | ThroughputCapacityPeriod |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines Throughput Capacity Period. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| SingleSource | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  | False |  | If True, the Facility can only receive inbound product from a single source. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| SingleSourceOrders | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | False |  | If True, all Line Items in the Orders placed from this Facility must be sourced from a single site (Facility or Supplier). If False, each Line Item within the Orders may be sourced from a different site. A value of True in this field overrides Single Source Line Items = False, enforcing that all Line Items in the orders come from a single source. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| SingleSourceLineItems | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | False |  | If True, the entire Line Item in orders placed from this Facility must be sourced from a single site (Facility or Supplier). If False, the Line Items may be sourced from different sites. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| AllowBackorders | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | If True, the Facility accepts backorders in the event that an order cannot be fulfilled before the Order Due Date (defined in the Replenishment Orders and Procurement Orders tables). | [NaN] | [] |  |
| BackorderTimeLimit | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | This value is the amount of time after which unsatisfied backorders will be cancelled by the Facility. A value of NULL will be interpreted as an infinite time limit, while a value of 0 is equivalent to setting Allow Backorders = False. | [Time] | [] |  |
| BackorderTimeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | BackorderTimeLimit |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure used to define the Backorder Time Limit. Any of the time units of measure defined in the Units Of Measure table can be used here. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| AllowPartialFillOrders | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | If True, orders placed by the Facility may be partially filled. Otherwise, orders must be filled in full. The remaining quantity of partially filled orders may be satisfied in the future with additional shipments; if the Backorder Time Limit is reached on a partially filled order, the remaining quantity will be cancelled. If Partial Fill Orders is False, Partial Fill Line Items will also be forced to False. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| AllowPartialFillLineItems | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | If True, line items within orders placed by the Facility may be partially filled. Otherwise, line items must be filled in full. The remaining quantity of partially filled line items may be satisfied in the future with additional shipments; if the Backorder Time Limit is reached on a partially filled line item, the remaining quantity will be cancelled. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| OperatingSchedule | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | BusinessHours |  | [HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | The schedule (defined in the Business Hours table) dictating when the Facility will be open for business. If no schedule is specified, the Facility is assumed to be open 24 hours per day. | [NaN] | [] | ScheduleName |
| OperatingCalendar | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | BusinessCalendars |  | [HOPPER] |  |  | None |  | The calendar (defined in the Business Calendars table) dictating which days of the year the Facility will shut down. If no calendar is specified, the Facility is assumed not to shut down. | [NaN] | [] | CalendarName |
| QueuePriority | int |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | For any Queues that are evaluated with a Queueing Priority Logic, there can be a By Location component to the priority logic used. If By Location is used, The Queue Priority fields in the Customers, Facilities and Suppliers table will be used to determine how the queues will be ordered | [Integer] | [] |  |
| FixedCO2Emissions | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | 0 |  | The fixed amount of CO2 Emissions at this Facility. This can be thought of as the amount of CO2 that needs to be offset for the facility to exist in the network. Variable CO2 Emissions can be defined for specific production or transportation activities in the Production Policy and Transportation Policy tables. | [Cost] | [] |  |
| FixedPickupTime | Cost Time Expression | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | The fixed time required at this Facility. This will be additive to the FixedPickupTime specified in the Shipments, Customers and Transportation Assets tables. | [Time] | [] |  |
| FixedPickupTimeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | FixedPickupTime |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [HOPPER] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines Fixed Pickup Time. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| FixedDeliveryTime | Cost Time Expression | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | The fixed time required for deliveries at this Facility. This will be additive to the Shipments, Customers and Transportation Assets tables. | [Time] | [] |  |
| FixedDeliveryTimeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | FixedDeliveryTime |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [HOPPER] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines Fixed Delivery Time | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| UnitPickupTime | Cost Time Expression | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | The time required per unit to be loaded at this Facility. This will be additive to any UnitPickupTime that is specified in the Shipments, Customers and Transportation Assets tables. | [Time] | [] |  |
| UnitPickupTimeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | UnitPickupTime |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [HOPPER] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines Unit Pickup Time. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| UnitDeliveryTime | Cost Time Expression | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | The time required per unit to be delivered at this Facility. This will be additive to any UnitDeliveryTime that is specified in the Shipments, Customers and Transportation Assets tables. | [Time] | [] |  |
| UnitUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | UnitPickupTime OR UnitDeliveryTime |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [HOPPER] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines the basis on which UnitPickupTime and UnitDeliveryTime will be applied at this Facility. Quantity, Volume, and Weight units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| UnitDeliveryTimeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | UnitDeliveryTime |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [HOPPER] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines Unit Delivery Time. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| GeographicRiskScore | double |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, TRIAD] |  |  |  |  | When geocoding a location, or following the most recent DART run, the Geographic Risk will be populated using the Primary Risk Rating defined in Model Settings. For more detailed information on Geographic Risk, reference the Geographic Risk Metrics output table. | [Risk] | [] |  |
| UserDefinedRiskScore | double |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, TRIAD] |  |  |  |  | An optional input that can be leveraged by DART when calculating Facility Risk. If left empty for some records, the User Defined Risk will not be considered for those specific Facilities. The weighting for this component will be defined in the Facility Risk Configurations table, and any outputs will be reported alongside other geographic risk scores in the Geographic Risk Metrics table. | [Risk] | [] |  |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, TRIAD, HOPPER, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [TRIAD, THROG, DENDRO, NEO, HOPPER] |  |

### シート: FacilitiesMultiTimePeriod

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| PeriodName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Periods | Yes | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The period name in which a change is being made. Groups of periods are also supported. Changes that are made in the Multi Time Period table will be applied as overrides for the records of the base table for the specified periods only. Additionally, only the fields that contain information needing to be updated are required to be entered, in the absence of data in the Multi Time Period table the data from the standard table will be persisted | [NaN] | [] | PeriodName |
| FacilityName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Facilities | Yes | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The name of the Facility | [NaN] | [] | FacilityName |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the multi-time period record exists in the model. If Status is set to Exclude, the record is ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| FacilityStatus | [Open, Closed, Consider] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | Facility Status dictates whether or not the Facility is included in the model for the specified period(s). If Status is set to Consider, the Facility may either be opened (Initial State = Potential) or closed (Initial State = Existing) depending on what is optimal. If the Facility is excluded from the model, any records for this Facility in other tables will also be ignored. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| FixedStartupCost | Cost Time Expression | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE StepCosts.StepCostBehavior = [Stepwise] |  |  |  | StepCosts |  | [NEO] |  |  |  | Currently supports doubles and named step costs that are defined in the StepCosts table where StepCostBehavior = Stepwise | If Status = Consider and Initial State = Potential, the Fixed Startup Cost will be incurred if the Facility is opened during the model solve. No startup cost will be incurred if the facility already exists. Fixed Startup Cost may be a single value or a step function of the Facility's throughput. If a step function is used, this cost will be determined based on the largest throughput recorded in any of the periods for which the Facility will be operating once opened. | [Cost] | [] | StepCostName |
| FixedOperatingCost | Cost Time Expression | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE StepCosts.StepCostBehavior = [Stepwise] |  |  |  | StepCosts |  | [NEO] |  |  |  | Currently supports doubles and named step costs that are defined in the StepCosts table where StepCostBehavior = Stepwise | The fixed cost associated with operating the Facility; Fixed Operating Cost may be a single value or a step function of the Facility's throughput. If a step function is used, this cost will be determined using the total throughput over each period. Step costs can be defined in the Step Costs table and referenced by name here. | [Cost] | [] | StepCostName |
| FixedClosingCost | Cost Time Expression | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE StepCosts.StepCostBehavior = [Stepwise] |  |  |  | StepCosts |  | [NEO] |  |  |  | Currently supports doubles and named step costs that are defined in the StepCosts table where StepCostBehavior = Stepwise | If Status = Consider and Initial State = Existing, the Fixed Closing Cost will be incurred if the Facility is closed during the model solve. Fixed Closing Cost may be a single value or a step function of the Facility's throughput. If a step function is used, this cost will be determined based on the largest throughput recorded in any of the periods for which the Facility was operating. Step costs can be defined in the Step Costs table and referenced by name here. | [Cost] | [] | StepCostName |
| StorageCapacity | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | This value indicates the total storage capacity (across products) of the Facility for the specified periods. Total Storage is measured as the Ending Inventory Product-specific capacities can be modeled using inventory constraints. | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| StorageCapacityUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | StorageCapacity |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure used to define the Storage Capacity of the Facility. Quantity, weight, and volume units of measure defined in the Units Of Measure table are accepted. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| ThroughputCapacity | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | This value indicates the total allowable throughput (across products) of the Facility over the specified period(s). Product-specific limits can be modeled using flow constraints. | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| ThroughputCapacityUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | ThroughputCapacity |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure used to define the Throughput Capacity of the Facility. Quantity, weight, and volume units of measure defined in the Units Of Measure table are accepted. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| ThroughputCapacityPeriod | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | For Simulation runs, this field will specify the length of time over which the throughput capacity will be applied. For example, the Throughput Capacity might be set to 500 EA with Capacity Period = 1 and Capacity Period UOM = DAY - this would mean that the Facility could process no more than 500 units in any day. The first period will be evaluated at the Simulation Start Time, and throughput capacity will be evaluated over the specified period length. Capacity is evaluated over individual capacity periods, a rolling capacity is not considered. | [Time] | [] |  |
| ThroughputCapacityPeriodUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | ThroughputCapacityPeriod |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines Throughput Capacity Period. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| SingleSource | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | If True, the Facility can only receive inbound product from a single source. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| SingleSourceOrders | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | If True, every order placed at the Facility must be satisfied from a single source. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| SingleSourceLineItems | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | If True, each line item in an order must come from a single source. Otherwise, a line item may be satisfied by multiple sources. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| AllowBackorders | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | If True, the Facility accepts backorders in the event that an order cannot be fulfilled before the Order Due Date (defined in the Replenishment Orders and Procurement Orders tables). | [NaN] | [] |  |
| BackorderTimeLimit | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | This value is the amount of time after which unsatisfied backorders will be cancelled by the Facility. A value of NULL will be interpreted as an infinite time limit, while a value of 0 is equivalent to setting Allow Backorders = False. | [Time] | [] |  |
| BackorderTimeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | BackorderTimeLimit |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure used to define the Backorder Time Limit. Any of the time units of measure defined in the Units Of Measure table can be used here. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| AllowPartialFillOrders | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | If True, orders placed by the Facility may be partially filled. Otherwise, orders must be filled in full. The remaining quantity of partially filled orders may be satisfied in the future with additional shipments; if the Backorder Time Limit is reached on a partially filled order, the remaining quantity will be cancelled. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| AllowPartialFillLineItems | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | If True, line items within orders placed by the Facility may be partially filled. Otherwise, line items must be filled in full. The remaining quantity of partially filled line items may be satisfied in the future with additional shipments; if the Backorder Time Limit is reached on a partially filled line item, the remaining quantity will be cancelled. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| OperatingSchedule | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | BusinessHours |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The schedule (defined in the Business Hours table) dictating when the Facility will be open for business. If no schedule is specified, the Facility is assumed to be open 24 hours per day. | [NaN] | [] | ScheduleName |
| OperatingCalendar | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | BusinessCalendars |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The calendar (defined in the Business Calendars table) dictating which days of the year the Facility will shut down. If no calendar is specified, the Facility is assumed not to shut down. | [NaN] | [] | CalendarName |
| QueuePriority | int |  |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | For any Queues that are evaluated with a Queueing Priority Logic, there can be a By Location component to the priority logic used. If By Location is used, The Queue Priority fields in the Customers, Facilities and Suppliers table will be used to determine how the queues will be ordered | [Integer] | [] |  |
| FixedCO2Emissions | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The fixed amount of CO2 Emissions at this Facility for the given period. This can be thought of as the amount of CO2 that needs to be offset for the facility to exist in the network. Variable CO2 Emissions can be defined for specific production or transportation activities in the Production Policy and Transportation Policy tables. | [Cost] | [] |  |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |

### シート: FacilityCountConstraints

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| FacilityName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Facilities | Yes | [NEO] |  |  | ALL |  | The Facility or Facilities for which we are counting open sites. Groups are supported. | [NaN] | [] | FacilityName |
| FacilityNameGroupBehavior | [Enumerate, Aggregate] |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Aggregate |  | If Facility Name is a Group, use this field to specify the Group's behavior. If Enumerate is selected, an instance of this constraint will be created for each member of the Group. If Aggregate is selected, the flow in the constraint will be summed over the members of the Group. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| PeriodName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Periods | Yes | [NEO] |  |  | ALL |  | The Period(s) in which we are counting open Facilities. Groups are supported. | [NaN] | [] | PeriodName |
| PeriodNameGroupBehavior | [Enumerate, Aggregate] |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Aggregate |  | If Period Name is a Group, use this field to specify the Group's behavior. If Enumerate is selected, an instance of this constraint will be created for each member of the Group. If Aggregate is selected, the flow in the constraint will be summed over the members of the Group. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ConstraintType | [Min, Max, Fixed, Fixed With Tolerance, Conditional Min] |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The type of constraint imposed on the count for the specified Facility/Period combination.   Min: the count must be greater than or equal to the Constraint Value. Max: the count must be less than or equal to the Constraint Value. Fixed: the count must be equal to the Constraint Value. Fixed With Tolerance: The count must be equal to the Constraint Value * (1 +/- Relative Constraint Tolerance). Relative Constraint Tolerance is specified in the Model Run Options. Conditional Min: if the count is nonzero, it must be greater than or equal to the Constraint Value. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ConstraintValue | int | NonNegative | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The numeric value that the specified count will be evaluated against. | [Integer] | [] |  |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the constraint exists in the model. If Status is set to Exclude, this constraint (or constraints in the case of Enumerated Groups) will be ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| CountingRule | [Facilities, Periods] |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | Facilities: Count the number of unique Facilities which are open during the specified Period(s). Periods: Count the unique number of Periods in which the specified Facility or Facilities are open. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |

### シート: FacilityDemand

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| FacilityName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Facilities | Yes | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The name of the Facility. Facility Groups are supported; the Group behavior will be Enumerate. | [NaN] | [] | FacilityName |
| ProductName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Products | Yes | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The name of the Product requested by the Facility. Product Groups are supported; the Group behavior will be Enumerate. | [NaN] | [] | ProductName |
| ProductNameGroupBehavior | [Enumerate, Aggregate] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  | Enumerate |  | When set to aggregate, this field allows for product groups that are entered for a demand record to be evaluated together as all group members being able to satisfy the single demand record. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| PeriodName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Periods | Yes | [NEO] |  |  | Model Horizon |  | The Period in which the demand record occurs. Period Groups are supported; the Group behavior will be enumerate. | [NaN] | [] | PeriodName |
| Quantity | double | PositiveNumeric | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The amount of the Product requested by the Facility in the specified Period. | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| MinQuantity | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The minimum amount of Product required to satisfy the Facility's demand in the specified Period. This value is used to turn demand satisfaction into a soft constraint; if no value is entered, it is assumed that Min Quantity is equal to Quantity. | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| MaxQuantity | double | PositiveNumeric |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The maximum amount of Product that the Facility can receive in the specified Period. This value is used to turn demand satisfaction into a soft constraint; if no value is entered, it is assumed that Max Quantity is equal to Quantity. | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| QuantityUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | Quantity |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that measures Quantity, Min Quantity, and Max Quantity. Quantity, Volume, and Weight units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the Demand record exists in the model. If Status is set to Exclude, this  record will be ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| DemandStatus | [Include, Exclude, Consider] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Include |  | Demand Status dictates whether or not the Demand record must be considered in the optimization run. If the Demand Status is set to Consider, the demand record can be serviced if it is optimal to do so, and if it is chosen not to be served then any associated penalty costs will be applied. If the Demand Status is set to Exclude then the demand record will be forced into a Not Served status and any associated Penalty Cost will be applied. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ServiceMode | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | TransportationModes |  | [] |  |  |  |  | Optionally, a required Transportation Mode can be specified here. If multiple Modes are acceptable, a Mode Group may be used; the Group behavior will be Aggregate. | [NaN] | [] | ModeName |
| UnitPrice | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The price that the Facility will pay for each unit of the Product. This price overrides the Unit Price given in the Products table. | [Cost] | [] |  |
| UnitPriceUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | UnitPrice |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines Unit Price. Quantity, Volume, and Weight units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| PenaltyCost | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The penalty cost associated with not fulfilling a unit of demand | [Cost] | [] |  |
| PenaltyCostUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | PenaltyCost |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines Penalty Cost. Quantity, Volume, and Weight units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |

| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| RiskProfileName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | RiskRatingConfigurations |  | [DART, NEO, TRIAD, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The name of the Risk Profile that the risk configuration is saved for. Risk Profiles are configured to run using specified source data in the Risk Rating Configurations table, and will take the risk weightings and bands associated with the Risk Profile from each of the configuration tables | [NaN] | [] | RiskProfileName |
| GeographicRiskWeight | double | NonNegative | Yes |  |  |  |  | [DART, NEO, TRIAD, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The weight applied to the Geographic Risk when calculating the Facility Risk Score. Detailed Geographic Risks configurations on geographic risk components are entered in the Geographic Risk Configurations table. | [Risk] | [] |  |
| UserDefinedRiskWeight | double | NonNegative | Yes |  |  |  |  | [DART, NEO, TRIAD, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The weight applied to the User Defined Risk when calculating the Facility Risk Score. | [Risk] | [] |  |
| UtilizationRiskWeight | double | NonNegative | Yes |  |  |  |  | [DART, NEO, TRIAD, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The weight applied to the Utilization Risk when calculating the Facility Risk Score. Detailed Utilization Risks configurations on the utilization risk components are entered in the Utilization Risk Configurations table. | [Risk] | [] |  |
| ConcentrationRiskWeight | double | NonNegative | Yes |  |  |  |  | [DART, NEO, TRIAD, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The weight applied to the Concentration Risk when calculating the Facility Risk Score. | [Risk] | [] |  |
| ConcentrationRiskBand | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  |  | RiskBandDefinitions |  | [DART, NEO, TRIAD, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The Risk Band that is used to evaluate the Concentration Risk at the Facility. Risk Bands are defined in the Risk Band Definitions table. | [NaN] | [] | BandName |
| SourceCountRiskWeight | double | NonNegative | Yes |  |  |  |  | [DART, NEO, TRIAD, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The weight applied to the Source Count Risk when calculating the Facility Risk Score. | [Risk] | [] |  |
| SourceCountRiskBand | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  |  | RiskBandDefinitions |  | [DART, NEO, TRIAD, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The Risk Band that is used to evaluate the Source Count Risk at the Facility. Risk Bands are defined in the Risk Band Definitions table. | [NaN] | [] | BandName |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [DART, NEO, TRIAD, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |

### シート: FixedRoutes

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn | UOMConversion |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| FixedRouteName | Name |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The name of the fixed route | [NaN] | [] |  |  |
| RoutePlannerName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | TransportationRoutePlanners | Yes | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The route planner that will use the fixed route | [NaN] | [] | RoutePlannerName |  |
| ReviewPeriodFirstTime | Date/Time |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The first Date/Time at which to review the queue for the specified fixed route. After this Date/Time, the review period length will dictate when fixed route reviews occur. If NULL, Review Period First Time will be the start of the simulation. | [NaN] | [] |  |  |
| ReviewPeriod | Cost Time Expression | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | ReviewSchedules |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The time between fixed route executions. After Review Period First Time, a fixed route execution will occur each time Review Period has elapsed. For example, if Review Period First Time is 1/1/2022 12:00:00 AM and Review Period is six hours, we will conduct reviews on 1/1/2022 at 12:00:00 AM, 6:00:00 AM, 12:00:00 PM, and 6:00:00 PM, continuing similarly until the simulation ends. A Review Schedule defined in the Review Schedules table can be entered by Review Schedule Name in this field as well. If NULL, continuous review is used. | [Time] | [] | ReviewScheduleName |  |
| ReviewPeriodUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines Review Period length. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |  |
| RouteCost | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG] |  |  | 0 |  | The cost associated with a shipment made under the specified Origin/Destination/Product/Mode combination. The Average Shipment Size will be used to estimate the number of shipments that occurred; the Fixed Cost will be applied to the estimated number of shipments according to the Fixed Cost Rule. If a step cost is used, the steps will be evaluated in terms of the number of shipments that are sent over the Origin/Destination/Product/Mode combination. | [Cost] | [] |  |  |
| RouteCostRule | [Prorate, Treat As Full] |  |  | RouteCost |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG] |  |  | Prorate |  | The rule by which Fixed Cost is applied to the estimated number of shipments. The cost can be prorated in the event that the estimated number of shipments is not an integer. If the selected rule is Treat as Full, the estimated number of shipments will be rounded up and the fixed cost will be applied accordingly. | [NaN] | [] |  |  |
| RouteCapacity | double | PositiveNumeric |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | 1 |  | The average shipment size associated with this Origin/Destination/Product/Mode combination. This figure will be used to estimate the fixed cost of shipments and will also be used in combination with any Time or Distance Unit Costs. If NULL, for an inbound lane to a Customer and Delivery Frequency is populated under CustomerDemand table, the Shipment Size will be applied as Demand Quantity / Delivery Frequency. For other Cases where the field is NULL, a default value of one unit will be assumed. | [Quantity] | [] |  |  |
| RouteCapacityUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  | RouteCapacity |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines Average Shipment Size. Quantity, Volume, and Weight units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol | Inter |
| AssetName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | TransportationAssets |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The name of the Transportation Asset assigned to this fixed route.  If none is provided then the planner will dynamically select from those available | [NaN] | [] | AssetName |  |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the fixed route exists in the model. If Status is set to Exclude, the fixed route record is ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [] |  |  |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |  |

If Enumerate is selected, an instance of this constraint will be created for each member of the Group. If Aggregate is selected, the flow in the constraint will be summed over the members of the Group. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ProductName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Products | Yes | [NEO] |  |  | ALL |  | The Product(s) in the flow we are constraining. Groups are supported. | [NaN] | [] | ProductName |
| ProductNameGroupBehavior | [Enumerate, Aggregate] |  |  |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Aggregate |  | If Product Name is a Group, use this field to specify the Group's behavior. If Enumerate is selected, an instance of this constraint will be created for each member of the Group. If Aggregate is selected, the flow in the constraint will be summed over the members of the Group. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ModeName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  | Yes | TransportationModes | Yes | [NEO] |  |  | ALL |  | The Transportation Mode(s) of the flow we are constraining (optional). Groups are supported. If left empty, all flows will be considered regardless of if a Mode is used. To specifically constrain against flows that do not use a Mode, enter a value of 'None' | [NaN] | [] | ModeName |
| ModeNameGroupBehavior | [Enumerate, Aggregate] |  |  |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Aggregate |  | If Mode Name is a Group, use this field to specify the Group's behavior. If Enumerate is selected, an instance of this constraint will be created for each member of the Group. If Aggregate is selected, the flow in the constraint will be summed over the members of the Group. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| PeriodName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Periods | Yes | [NEO] |  |  | ALL |  | The Period(s) in which we are constraining flow. Groups are supported. | [NaN] | [] | PeriodName |
| PeriodNameGroupBehavior | [Enumerate, Aggregate] |  |  |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Aggregate |  | If Period Name is a Group, use this field to specify the Group's behavior. If Enumerate is selected, an instance of this constraint will be created for each member of the Group. If Aggregate is selected, the flow in the constraint will be summed over the members of the Group. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ConstraintType | [Min, Max, Fixed, Fixed With Tolerance, Conditional Min] |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The type of constraint imposed on the flow for the specified Source/Destination/Product/Mode/Period combination.   Min: the flow must be greater than or equal to the Constraint Value. Max: the flow must be less than or equal to the Constraint Value. Fixed: the flow must be equal to the Constraint Value. Fixed With Tolerance: The flow must be equal to the Constraint Value * (1 +/- Relative Constraint Tolerance). Relative Constraint Tolerance is specified in the Model Run Options. Conditional Min: if the flow is nonzero, it must be greater than or equal to the Constraint Value. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ConstraintValue | double | NonNegative | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The numeric value that the specified flow will be evaluated against. | [Constraint] | [] |  |
| ConstraintValueUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight, Shipment, Quantity-Distance, Volume-Distance, Weight-Distance, Quantity-Time, Volume-Time, Weight-Time] |  | ConstraintValue | Yes | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight, Shipment, Quantity-Distance, Volume-Distance, Weight-Distance, Quantity-Time, Volume-Time, Weight-Time] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines Constraint Value. Quantity, Volume, Weight and Shipment units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the constraint exists in the model. If Status is set to Exclude, this constraint (or constraints in the case of Enumerated Groups) will be ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |

Destinations: Count the number of unique Destinations in the flows for the specified Origin/Product/Mode/Period combination. Products: Count the number of unique Products in the flows for the specified Origin/Destination/Mode/Period combination. Modes: Count the number of unique Modes used in the flows for the specified Origin/Destination/Product/Period combination. Periods: Count the number of unique Periods in which flow occurs for the specified Origin/Destination/Product/Mode combination. Origins-Destinations: Count the number of unique Origin-Destination combinations on which flow occurs for the specified Mode/Product/Period combination. Destinations-Modes: Count the number of unique Destination-Mode combinations on which flow occurs for the specified Origin/Product/Period combination. Products-Modes: Count the number of unique Product-Mode combinations on which flow occurs for the specified Origin/Destination/Period combination. | [NaN] | [] |  |

### シート: GreenfieldSettings

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| MinNumberOfNewFacilities | int | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [TRIAD] |  |  |  |  | The minimum number of new facility locations to be opened during solve. This will include any Candidate Facilities that are selected as well as any Greenfield Facilities. This value is ignored if MinimizeTotalFacilities is set to True. | [Integer] | [] |  |
| MaxNumberOfNewFacilities | int | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [TRIAD] |  |  |  |  | The maximum number of new facility locations to be opened during solve. This will include any Candidate Facilities that are selected as well as any Greenfield Facilities. This value is ignored if MinimizeTotalFacilities is set to True. | [Integer] | [TRIAD] |  |
| MinimizeTotalFacilities | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [TRIAD] |  |  | False |  | If True, the number of new facility locations to be opened will be minimized (Greenfield Facilities and Candidate Facilities). If False, the total cost (Facility Fixed Cost and Transportation Cost) will be minimized. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ExcludeFacilities | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [TRIAD] |  |  | True |  | If True, all Facilities in the model will be ignored during the Greenfield run. This value is set to False if OnlyUseFacilitiesAsCandidateLocations is set to True. | [NaN] | [TRIAD] |  |
| ExcludeSuppliers | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [TRIAD] |  |  | True |  | If True, all Suppliers in the model will be ignored during the Greenfield run. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| OnlyUseFacilitiesAsCandidateLocations | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [TRIAD] |  |  | False |  | If True, the selection pool for Greenfield locations will be restricted to Facilities that are Included. Facilities with a Facility Status of 'Open' will be forced to open and have their fixed operating cost charged in the Greenfield solve. Facilities with a Facility Status of 'Consider' will have the option to be opened as a Candidate Facility location and if selected, their fixed operating cost will be charged. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| CustomerMaxSourcingDistance | double | PositiveNumeric |  |  |  |  |  | [TRIAD] |  |  |  |  | The maximum distance that any Customer can source a flow from a Greenfield Facility, a Candidate Facility, or an Existing Facility. | [Distance] | [TRIAD] |  |
| GreenfieldFacilityMinThroughput | double | PositiveNumeric |  |  |  |  |  | [TRIAD] |  |  |  |  | The minimum quantity of demand that a Greenfield Facility must service to be selected.This will apply to all Facilities, both new and existing, for the Greenfield solve. | [Quantity] | [TRIAD] |  |
| GreenfieldFacilityMaxThroughput | double | PositiveNumeric |  |  |  |  |  | [TRIAD] |  |  |  |  | The maximum quantity of demand that a single Greenfield Facility can service. For existing or candidate Facilities the throughput capacity will be specified in the Facilities table. | [Quantity] | [TRIAD] |  |
| GreenfieldFacilityFixedCost | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [TRIAD] |  |  | 0 |  | The fixed cost applied to each Greenfield Facility used in the solve. | [Cost] | [] |  |
| CustomerFulfillmentCost | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [TRIAD] |  |  | 1 |  | The cost charged for all flows from Facilities to Customers on a quantity-distance basis. | [Cost] | [] |  |
| ProcurementCost | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [TRIAD] |  |  | 0.75 |  | The cost charged for all flows from Suppliers to Facilities on a quantity-distance basis. | [Cost] | [] |  |
| RunLocationRefinementHeuristic | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [TRIAD] |  |  | True |  | If True, the Greenfield Facilities selected by the solver can be improved beyond their initial location that would be co-located with a Customer / Cluster location. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| CustomerClusterRadius | double | PositiveNumeric |  |  |  |  |  | [TRIAD] |  |  |  |  | The distance that Customers should be clustered over. The UOM of this distance measure is the primary distance UOM specified in the Model Settings table. | [Distance] | [] |  |
| AutoDetectClusterRadius | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [TRIAD] |  |  | True |  | If True, large models with more than 3000 customers will be automatically clustered to have less than 3000 nodes during the optimization phase. Clustomers are then disaggregated for outputs. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ClusteringApproach | [utm, fcluster] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [TRIAD] |  |  | utm |  | The method used to identify customer clusters. The 'fcluster' method requires a computation of the full distance matrix between all customers pairs which can be a time consuming process. The 'utm' method is based on the Universal Transverse Mercator (UTM) coordinate system looks to identify clusters in each UTM zone. While UTM based method may in some cases be less accurate it does not to precompute the full distance matrix between all customer pairs. | [NaN] | [] |  |

### シート: Groups

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| GroupName | Name |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, TRIAD, HOPPER, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The name of the Group; this value can be referenced in other tables to apply records to members of the Group. Depending on which table the Group is referenced in, treatment of the Group members will either be implied or taken as input in that table. Group behavior types are Aggregate and Enumerate. Items in an Enumerated Group are evaluated individually while items in an Aggregated Group will be evaluated collectively. | [NaN] | [THROG, DENDRO, NEO] |  |
| GroupType | [Products, Customers, Facilities, Suppliers, Sources, Destinations, Periods, Modes, WorkCenters, WorkResources, Processes, BillsOfMaterials, Shipments, TransportationAssets] |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, TRIAD, HOPPER, CYCLO] |  |  |  | Optimization: Currently supports GroupType = [Products, Customers, Facilities, Suppliers, Sources, Destinations, Periods, Modes, WorkCenters]  Simulation: Currently supports GroupType = [Products, Customers, Facilities, Suppliers, Sources, Destinations] | The type of elements the Group consists of. If Group Type = Source, the Group can contain both Facilities and Suppliers. If Group Type = Destination, the Group can contain both Facilities and Customers. Otherwise, Group Type denotes the table that members may come from.  NEO does not currently support GroupType = WorkResources  THROG does not currently support GroupType = Periods | [NaN] | [THROG, DENDRO, NEO] |  |
| MemberName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Products, Customers, Facilities, Suppliers, Periods, TransportationModes, WorkCenters, WorkResources, Processes, BillsOfMaterials, Shipments, TransportationAssets |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, TRIAD, HOPPER, CYCLO] |  |  |  | Optimization: Currently supports GroupType = [Products, Customers, Facilities, Suppliers, Sources, Destinations, Periods, Modes, WorkCenters]  Simulation: Currently supports GroupType = [Products, Customers, Facilities, Suppliers, Sources, Destinations] | The entity that is being assigned to the Group. The Member Name must have an associated record in the relevant master table. | [NaN] | [THROG, DENDRO, NEO] | [Products.ProductName, Customers.CustomerName, Facilities.FacilityName, Suppliers.SupplierName, Periods.PeriodName, TransportationModes.ModeName, WorkCenters.WorkCenterName, WorkResources.WorkResourceName, Processes.ProcessName, BillsOfMaterials.BOMName, Shipments.ShipmentID, TransportationAssets.AssetName] |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, TRIAD, HOPPER, CYCLO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether the specified Member is included in or excluded from the Group during the model run. If Status = Exclude, the specified Member is ignored wherever the Group is referenced. | [NaN] | [THROG, DENDRO, NEO] |  |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, TRIAD, HOPPER, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [THROG, DENDRO, NEO] |  |

| HistogramName | Name |  |  |  | Yes |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The name of the histogram. If this Histogram Name is used as an input in another table, the histogram's data as described by the Bin Edges and Bin Heights will be used to form a distribution and draw a value. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| BinEdge | double |  |  |  | Yes |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The lower edge of the bin, a bin's width will be determined by the next defined bin edge. The last edge will serve as the upper edge for the last bin and the height will be ignored. | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| BinHeight | double |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The height of each bin. | [Quantity] | [] |  |

| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

| PeriodName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Periods | Yes | [NEO] |  |  | ALL |  | The Period(s) in which we are constraining the changes. Groups are supported. | [NaN] | [NEO] | PeriodName |
| PeriodNameGroupBehavior | [Enumerate, Aggregate] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Enumerate |  | If Period Name is a Group, use this field to specify the Group's behavior. If Enumerate is selected, an instance of this constraint will be created for each member of the Group. If Aggregate is selected, the limit in the constraint will be applied over the members of the Group. | [NaN] | [NEO] |  |
| Budget | double | NonNegative | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | 0 |  | The limit for the amount of change on the change type's subject in the given Period. | [NaN] | [NEO] |  |
| BudgetBasis | [Percentage, Quantity] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Quantity |  | The basis for the change limit. | [NaN] | [NEO] |  |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the constraint exists in the model. If Status is set to Exclude, this constraint (or constraints in the case of Enumerated Groups) will be ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [NEO] |  |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [NEO] |  |

### シート: InputFactors

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| InputFactorName | Name |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The name of the Input Factor (SIM-specific); Input Factors correspond to values ("genes") that are allowed to be adjusted in the genetic algorithm (GA). The GA will iteratively run the simulation with modified genes to improve the model outcomes according to the Utility Level field of the Output Factors table. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| TableName | Name | Valid Input Table | Yes |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The name of the table that contains the data which the Input Factor can adjust. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ColumnName | Name | Valid Column Name | Yes |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The name of the column (within the specified table) that the Input Factor will adjust. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Filter | String | Valid Filter |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The filter to apply to the specified column and table (optional). | [NaN] | [] |  |
| BaseValue | double |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The value used to initialize the Input Factor. If left empty, the value from the cell in the Input Table being referenced will be used as the base value. | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| MinValue | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The minimum value that the Input Factor is allowed to take in any iteration. | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| MaxValue | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The maximum value that the Input Factor is allowed to take in any iteration. | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| StepSize | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The increments by which the Input Factor is allowed to be adjusted. Min Value and Max Value will always be allowed even if they cannot be reached from Base Value in increments of this size. | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| RelativeValues | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | If True, the values specified for Min Value, Max Value and Step Size will be relative percentages of the Base Value. For example, if Base Value = 200, Min Value = 50 and RelativeValues = TRUE then the Minimum Value that van be used would be 100. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Enumerate | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | Optionally, a list of values which the Input Factor is allowed to take. These values should be entered as [ Value 1 ｜ Value 2 ｜ Value 3 ｜ … ]. If both the Base Value and Enumerate fields are populated, the values specified in Enumerate will be used. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | Status dictates whether or not the Input Factor is included when running the genetic algorithm. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |

| FacilityName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Facilities | Yes | [NEO] |  |  | ALL |  | The Facility or Facilities at which we are constraining inventory. Groups are supported. | [NaN] | [] | FacilityName |
| FacilityNameGroupBehavior | [Enumerate, Aggregate] |  |  |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Aggregate |  | If Facility Name is a Group, use this field to specify the Group's behavior. If Enumerate is selected, an instance of this constraint will be created for each member of the Group. If Aggregate is selected, the flow in the constraint will be summed over the members of the Group. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ProductName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Products | Yes | [NEO] |  |  | ALL |  | The Product(s) for which we are constraining inventory. Groups are supported. | [NaN] | [] | ProductName |
| ProductNameGroupBehavior | [Enumerate, Aggregate] |  |  |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Aggregate |  | If Product Name is a Group, use this field to specify the Group's behavior. If Enumerate is selected, an instance of this constraint will be created for each member of the Group. If Aggregate is selected, the flow in the constraint will be summed over the members of the Group. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| PeriodName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Periods | Yes | [NEO] |  |  | ALL |  | The Period(s) in which we are constraining inventory. Groups are supported. | [NaN] | [] | PeriodName |
| PeriodNameGroupBehavior | [Enumerate, Aggregate] |  |  |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Aggregate |  | If Period Name is a Group, use this field to specify the Group's behavior. If Enumerate is selected, an instance of this constraint will be created for each member of the Group. If Aggregate is selected, the flow in the constraint will be summed over the members of the Group. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ConstraintType | [Min, Max, Fixed, Fixed With Tolerance, Conditional Min] |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The type of constraint imposed on the inventory for the specified Facility/Product combination.   Min: the inventory level must be greater than or equal to the Constraint Value. Max: the inventory level must be less than or equal to the Constraint Value. Fixed: the inventory level must be equal to the Constraint Value. Fixed With Tolerance: The inventory level must be equal to the Constraint Value * (1 +/- Relative Constraint Tolerance). Relative Constraint Tolerance is specified in the Model Run Options. Conditional Min: if the inventory level is nonzero, it must be greater than or equal to the Constraint Value. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ConstraintValue | double | NonNegative | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The numeric value that the specified inventory level will be evaluated against. | [Constraint] | [] |  |
| ConstraintValueUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight, DaysOfSupply] |  | ConstraintValue | Yes | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight, DaysOfSupply] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines Constraint Value. Quantity, Volume, and Weight units of measure are supported. DOS is also supported where Constraint Type = Min. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| ConsideredInventory | [Prebuild Only, Cycle Only, Prebuild and Cycle] |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Prebuild Only |  | The type of inventory that is to be considered when applying the constraint. Prebuild inventory is restricted based on the Ending Prebuild Inventory, and Cycle Inventory is restricted based on the total of Average Turn Estimated Inventory, Average Transportation Estimated Inventory and Average Production Estimated Inventory. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the constraint exists in the model. If Status is set to Exclude, this constraint (or constraints in the case of Enumerated Groups) will be ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |

If Aggregate is selected, the flow in the constraint will be summed over the members of the Group. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ConstraintType | [Min, Max, Fixed, Fixed With Tolerance, Conditional Min] |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The type of constraint imposed on the inventory count for the specified Facility/Product/Period combination.   Min: the count must be greater than or equal to the Constraint Value. Max: the count must be less than or equal to the Constraint Value. Fixed: the count must be equal to the Constraint Value. Fixed With Tolerance: The count must be equal to the Constraint Value * (1 +/- Relative Constraint Tolerance). Relative Constraint Tolerance is specified in the Model Run Options. Conditional Min: if the count is nonzero, it must be greater than or equal to the Constraint Value. | [NaN] | [] |  |

| CountingRule | [Facilities, Products, Periods] |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | Facilities: Count the number of unique Facilities at which inventory is held for the specified Product/Period combination. Products: Count the number of unique Products that are held in inventory for the specified Facility/Period combination. Periods: Count the number of unique Periods in which inventory is held for the specified Facility/Product combination. | [NaN] | [] |  |
