### シート: InventoryPolicies

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| FacilityName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Facilities | Yes | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The Facility that the policy record applies to. | [NaN] | [THROG, DENDRO, NEO] | FacilityName |
| ProductName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Products | Yes | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The Product that the policy record applies to. | [NaN] | [THROG, DENDRO, NEO] | ProductName |
| SimulationPolicy | [(s,S), (Min,Max), (R,Q), (T,S), Do Nothing] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  | Simulation: Currently supports policies of [R,Q] [s,S] | The inventory management logic applied to the specified Facility/Product combination. | [NaN] | [THROG] |  |
| SimulationPolicyValue1 | double | NonNegative | Yes | SimulationPolicy = (s,S) OR (Min,Max) OR (R,Q) |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The first argument of the Policy. To Optimize policies do not require parameters. (T,S) will not use this parameter, but the periodic review information will be taken from the Review Period First Time / Duration fields directly | [Quantity] | [THROG] |  |
| SimulationPolicyValue1UOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight, DaysOfSupply] |  | SimulationPolicyValue1 |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight, DaysOfSupply] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure by which the reorder points specified will be evaluated by | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| SimulationPolicyValue2 | double | NonNegative | Yes | SimulationPolicy = (s,S) OR (Min,Max) OR (R,Q) OR (T,S) |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The second argument of the Policy. To Optimize policies do not require parameters. | [Quantity] | [THROG] |  |
| SimulationPolicyValue2UOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight, DaysOfSupply] |  | SimulationPolicyValue2 |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight, DaysOfSupply] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure by which the order up to / order quantities will be evaluated by | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| StockingSite | [True, False] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO, CYCLO] |  |  | True |  | If True, the Facility can hold inventory of the specified Product. Otherwise, no units of the Product can be held in inventory at the Facility. Non-inventory handling costs can be defined in the Warehousing Policies table. | [NaN] | [NEO] |  |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the policy exists in the model. If Status is set to Exclude, the policy record is ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [THROG, DENDRO, NEO] |  |
| ReviewPeriodFirstTime | Date/Time |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The first Date/Time at which to review inventory of the Product at the specified Facility. Inventory levels will be evaluated using the criteria defined by the Policy and Policy Parameter fields. After this Date/Time, the review period length will dictate when inventory reviews occur. If NULL, Review Period First Time will be the start of the simulation. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ReviewPeriod | Cost Time Expression | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | ReviewSchedules |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The time between inventory reviews. After Review Period First Time, an inventory review will occur each time Review Period has elapsed. For example, if Review Period First Time is 1/1/2022 12:00:00 AM and Review Period is six hours, we will conduct reviews on 1/1/2022 at 12:00:00 AM, 6:00:00 AM, 12:00:00 PM, and 6:00:00 PM, continuing similarly until the simulation ends. A Review Schedule defined in the Review Schedules table can be entered by Review Schedule Name in this field as well. If NULL, continuous review is used. | [Time] | [THROG] | ReviewScheduleName |
| ReviewPeriodUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | ReviewPeriod |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines Review Period. | [NaN] | [THROG] | Symbol |
| UnitValue | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | Unit Value represents the value of one unit of the Product being held at the Facility. If NULL, this field will be equal to the Unit Value specified in the Products table. The holding cost for this Facility/Product combination will be calculated as Unit Value * Carrying Cost Percentage. | [Cost] | [] |  |
| UnitValueUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | UnitValue |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO, CYCLO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines the Unit Value. Quantity, Volume, and Weight units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| CarryingCostPercentage | double | Percentage |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  | {InventoryCarryingCostPercentage} |  | The percentage of the Product's value (Unit Value) that will be incurred as a per unit inventory holding cost. This percentage is to be entered as an annual percentage of the value, and inventory holding costs will be calculated as Average Inventory Value * Carrying Cost Percentage * (Period Length in days) / 365). This field will read entries in as the percentages which they represent, for example an entry of 12 will correspond to a carrying cost of 12%. | [Percentage] | [] |  |
| InitialInventory | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The inventory levels for the Facility/Product combination at the beginning of the model run. | [Quantity] | [THROG, DENDRO, NEO] |  |
| InitialInventoryUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | InitialInventory |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines initial inventory levels. Quantity, Volume, and Weight units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| UnitStorageCost | Cost Time Expression | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE StepCosts.StepCostBehavior = [All Item, Incremental, Stepwise] |  |  |  | StepCosts |  | [NEO] |  |  | 0 | Currently supports doubles and named step costs that are defined in the StepCosts table where StepCostBehavior = All Item, Incremental or Stepwise or a combination of Stepwise and All Item | The cost associated with storing one unit of the Product in inventory at the Facility. This cost is incurred in addition to inventory holding costs and is applied against the calculated Average Inventory for the Facility / Product combination. This field can support a single numeric value, a step cost lookup (defined in the Step Costs table), or a custom cost function. | [Cost] | [] | StepCostName |
| UnitStorageCostUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | UnitStorageCost |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines Unit Storage Cost. Quantity, Volume, and Weight units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| MinDwellTime | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The Min Dwell Time will determine the length of time that a product must remain at a Facility before it can flow out or be consumed. When being used in Optimization, Dwell Time will be evaluated against the period lengths to determine which period a product will become available. For example, if the Dwell Time is 45 Days and a model has monthly periods, the product will become available in Period 2. | [Time] | [] |  |
| MinDwellTimeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | MinDwellTime |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines the length of the Min Dwell Time | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| MaxDwellTime | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The Max Dwell Time will determine the maximum length of time that a product can remain at a Facility before it must flow out or be consumed. When being used in Optimization, Dwell Time will be evaluated against the period lengths to determine which period a product will become available. For example, if the Dwell Time is 45 Days and a model has monthly periods, the product must leave the Facility by Period 2. | [Time] | [] |  |
| MaxDwellTimeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | MaxDwellTime |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines the length of the Max Dwell Time | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| MinProductAge | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The minimum product age will determine the minimum age a product can have before it has to leave the Facility or be consumed. | [Time] | [] |  |
| MinProductAgeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | MinProductAge |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines the length of the Product's Max Age | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| MaxProductAge | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The maximum product age will determine the maximum age a product can have before it has to leave the Facility or be consumed. | [Time] | [] |  |
| MaxProductAgeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | MaxProductAge |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines the length of the Product's Max Age | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| InitialInventoryAge | double | NonNegative |  |  | Yes |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The initial inventory age will determine the age of initial inventory. | [Time] | [] |  |
| InitialInventoryAgeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | InitialInventoryAge |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines the length of the Product's Max Age | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| SurplusLevel | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The inventory level above which surplus inventory will be calculated. For any inventory that is identified as surplus, any push policies will be evaluated from the Replenishment Policies table to identify which destinations the surplus inventory might be sent out to. In the event that ALL inventory should be pushed, set the Surplus Level to 0. | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| SurplusLevelUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight, DaysOfSupply] |  | SurplusLevel |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight, DaysOfSupply] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines Surplus Level.  Quantity, Volume, and Weight and DOS units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| SurplusReviewPeriodFirstTime | Date/Time |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The first Date/Time at which to evaluate whether surplus inventory is present for the Facility/Product combination. If NULL, Surplus Review Period First Time will be the start of the simulation. NOTE this review period will only apply to Push policies where identified surplus is pushed out of the facility. In instances where Pull policies are evaluating surplus, a surplus review will happen at the time the pull request lands at the Facility. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| SurplusReviewPeriod | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | 30 |  | The time between Surplus reviews. After Surplus Review Period First Time, a Surplus review will occur each time Surplus Review Period has elapsed. If NULL, Surplus Review Period Duration will be 30 days. NOTE this review period will only apply to Push policies where identified surplus is pushed out of the facility. In instances where Pull policies are evaluating surplus, a surplus review will happen at the time the pull request lands at the Facility. | [Time] | [] |  |
| SurplusReviewPeriodUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | SurplusReviewPeriod |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines Surplus Review Period. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| DOSWindow | double | PositiveNumeric |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The number of days' worth of throughput that will be tracked for the Facility/Product combination when determining the Days of Supply (DOS) level. Throughput will be summed over this window then divided by the length of the window to obtain a single-day DOS estimate. This DOS estimate can be used as the unit of measure in inventory constraints. | [Time] | [] |  |
| DOSWindowUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | DOSWindow |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines the length of the DOS Window. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| DOSLeadTime | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The length of time to offset the DOS Window by when determining the DOS value for the Facility/Product combination. | [Time] | [] |  |
| DOSLeadTimeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | DOSLeadTime |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines DOS Lead Time. If DOS Lead Time = 5 and DOS Lead Time UOM Symbol = Day, the end point of the DOS Window will be five days prior to the current simulation time. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| DOSReviewPeriodFirstTime | Date/Time |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The first Date/Time at which to calculate DOS for the Facility/Product combination. If NULL, DOS Review Period First Time will be the start of the simulation. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| DOSReviewPeriod | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | 30 |  | The time between DOS reviews. After DOS Review Period First Time, a DOS review will occur each time DOS Review Period has elapsed. If NULL, DOS Review Period will be 30 days. | [Time] | [] |  |
| DOSReviewPeriodUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | SafetyStockReviewPeriod |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines DOS Review Period Duration. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| ForecastType | [Historical, User-Defined] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | If Days Of Supply is used as a UOM for SIM Policy Value 1 or 2, or in the Surplus Level definition, we must specify whether a Historical or User-Defined forecast will be used when calculating the DOS value. If User-Defined is selected, the Forecast Name specified in the DOS User Defined Forecast Name will be used for all DOS calculations. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| DOSUserDefinedForecastName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | UserDefinedForecasts |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | If DOS Forecast Type is set to User-Defined, the User Defined Forecast Name entered here will be used for all DOS calculations. User Defined Forecasts are defined in the User Defined Forecasts table where the aggregation and disaggregation settings are entered, and all forecast data will be entered in the User Defined Forecasts Data table | [NaN] | [] | ForecastName |
| QuotedTimeToFill | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG] |  |  |  |  | The Quoted Time To Fill will be used to calculate Order and Line Service Percentages for the Facility and Facility Product Service Summary output tables. Orders that are able to be filled within the quoted fill time from the time that they enter the order fulfillment queue at the facility will be marked as successfully serviced. | [Time] | [] |  |
| QuotedTimeToFillUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  | QuotedTimeToFill |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines Quoted Time To Fill. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| TimeBetweenInventoryTurns | double | PositiveNumeric |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The amount of time that it takes for Inventory of the specified Facility/Product combination to turn over. Given the time between inventory turns, the number of turns will be calculated for the length of the period. Then, total outbound flow of the specified Product from the Facility will be divided by the number of turns to determine the Max Turn Estimated Inventory. In the event that the Time Between Inventory Turns is longer than the length of the Period, the Turn Estimated Inventory will be calculated based off of the proportion of the turn window that falls within the period. | [Time] | [] |  |
| TimeBetweenInventoryTurnsUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  | TimeBetweenInventoryTurns |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines the Time Between Inventory Turns. Time units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [THROG, DENDRO, NEO] |  |

### シート: InventoryPoliciesMultiTimePeri0

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| PeriodName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Periods | Yes | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The period name in which a change is being made. Groups of periods are also supported. Changes that are made in the Multi Time Period table will be applied as overrides for the records of the base table for the specified periods only. Additionally, only the fields that contain information needing to be updated are required to be entered, in the absence of data in the Multi Time Period table the data from the standard table will be persisted | [NaN] | [] | PeriodName |
| FacilityName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Facilities | Yes | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The Facility that the policy record applies to. | [NaN] | [] | FacilityName |
| ProductName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Products | Yes | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The Product that the policy record applies to. | [NaN] | [] | ProductName |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the multi-time period record exists in the model. If Status is set to Exclude, the record is ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| PolicyStatus | [Include, Exclude] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | Policy Status dictates whether or not the policy exists in the model for the specified period(s). If Policy Status is set to Exclude, the policy record is ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| SimulationPolicy | [(s,S), (Min,Max), (R,Q), (T,S), Do Nothing] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The inventory management logic applied to the specified Facility/Product combination. This Policy can be used as  a base rule or can be linked to a policy in the Inventory Planning table. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| SimulationPolicyValue1 | double | NonNegative |  | SimulationPolicy = (s,S) OR (Min,Max) OR (R,Q) |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The first argument of the Policy. To Optimize policies do not require parameters. (T,S) will not use this parameter, but the periodic review information will be taken from the Review Period First Time / Duration fields directly | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| SimulationPolicyValue1UOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight, DaysOfSupply] |  | SimulationPolicyParameter1 |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight, DaysOfSupply] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure by which the reorder points specified will be evaluated by | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| SimulationPolicyValue2 | double | NonNegative |  | SimulationPolicy = (s,S) OR (Min,Max) OR (R,Q) OR (T,S) |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The second argument of the Policy. To Optimize policies do not require parameters. | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| SimulationPolicyValue2UOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight, DaysOfSupply] |  | SimulationPolicyParameter2 |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight, DaysOfSupply] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure by which the order up to / order quantities will be evaluated by | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| StockingSite | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | If True, the Facility can hold inventory of the specified Product. Otherwise, no units of the Product can be held in inventory at the Facility. Non-inventory handling costs can be defined in the Warehousing Policies table. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ReviewPeriodFirstTime | Date/Time |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The first Date/Time at which to review inventory of the Product at the specified Facility. Inventory levels will be evaluated using the criteria defined by the Policy and Policy Parameter fields. After this Date/Time, the review period length will dictate when inventory reviews occur. If NULL, Review Period First Time will be the start of the simulation. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ReviewPeriod | Cost Time Expression | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | ReviewSchedules |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The time between inventory reviews. After Review Period First Time, an inventory review will occur each time Review Period has elapsed. For example, if Review Period First Time is 1/1/2022 12:00:00 AM and Review Period is six hours, we will conduct reviews on 1/1/2022 at 12:00:00 AM, 6:00:00 AM, 12:00:00 PM, and 6:00:00 PM, continuing similarly until the simulation ends. A Review Schedule defined in the Review Schedules table can be entered by Review Schedule Name in this field as well. If NULL, continuous review is used. | [Time] | [] | ReviewScheduleName |
| ReviewPeriodUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | ReviewPeriod |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines Review Period. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| ScheduledReceipt | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The amount of inventory that is set to arrive at the beginning of the specified period | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| ScheduledReceiptUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | ScheduledReceipt |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines the scheduled receipt amount. Quantity, Volume, and Weight units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| UnitValue | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | Unit Value represents the value of one unit of the Product being held at the Facility. If NULL, this field will be equal to the Unit Value specified in the Products table. The holding cost for this Facility/Product combination will be calculated as Unit Value * Carrying Cost Percentage. | [Cost] | [] |  |
| UnitValueUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | UnitValue |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines the Unit Value. Quantity, Volume, and Weight units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| CarryingCostPercentage | double | Percentage |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | {InventoryCarryingCostPercentage} |  | The percentage of the Product's value (Unit Value) that will be incurred as a per unit inventory holding cost. This percentage is to be entered as an annual percentage of the value, and inventory holding costs will be calculated as Average Inventory Value * Carrying Cost Percentage * (Period Length in days) / 365) | [Percentage] | [] |  |
| UnitStorageCost | Cost Time Expression | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE StepCosts.StepCostBehavior = [All Item, Incremental, Stepwise] |  |  |  | StepCosts |  | [NEO] |  |  | 0 | Currently supports doubles and named step costs that are defined in the StepCosts table where StepCostBehavior = All Item, Incremental or Stepwise or a combination of Stepwise and All Item | The cost associated with storing one unit of the Product in inventory at the Facility. This cost is incurred in addition to inventory holding costs and is applied against the calculated Average Inventory for the Facility / Product combination. This field can support a single numeric value, a step cost lookup (defined in the Step Costs table), or a custom cost function. | [Cost] | [] | StepCostName |
| UnitStorageCostUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | UnitStorageCost |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines Unit Storage Cost. Quantity, Volume, and Weight units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| MinDwellTime | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The Min Dwell Time will determine the length of time that a product must remain at a Facility before it can flow out or be consumed. When being used in Optimization, Dwell Time will be evaluated against the period lengths to determine which period a product will become available. For example, if the Dwell Time is 45 Days and a model has monthly periods, the product will become available in Period 2. | [Time] | [] |  |
| MinDwellTimeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | MinDwellTime |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines the length of the Min Dwell Time | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| MaxDwellTime | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The Max Dwell Time will determine the maximum length of time that a product can remain at a Facility before it must flow out or be consumed. When being used in Optimization, Dwell Time will be evaluated against the period lengths to determine which period a product will become available. For example, if the Dwell Time is 45 Days and a model has monthly periods, the product must leave the Facility by Period 2. | [Time] | [] |  |
| MaxDwellTimeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | MaxDwellTime |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines the length of the Max Dwell Time | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| SurplusLevel | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The inventory level above which surplus inventory will be calculated. For any inventory that is identified as surplus, any push policies will be evaluated from the Replenishment Policies table to identify which destinations the surplus inventory might be sent out to. In the event that ALL unclaimed inventory should be pushed, set the Surplus Level to 0. | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| SurplusLevelUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight, DaysOfSupply] |  | SurplusLevel |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight, DaysOfSupply] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines Surplus Level.  Quantity, Volume, and Weight and DOS units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| SurplusReviewPeriodFirstTime | Date/Time |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The first Date/Time at which to evaluate whether surplus inventory is present for the Facility/Product combination. If NULL, Surplus Review Period First Time will be the start of the simulation. NOTE this review period will only apply to Push policies where identified surplus is pushed out of the facility. In instances where Pull policies are evaluating surplus, a surplus review will happen at the time the pull request lands at the Facility. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| SurplusReviewPeriod | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | 30 |  | The time between Surplus reviews. After Surplus Review Period First Time, a Surplus review will occur each time Surplus Review Period has elapsed. If NULL, Surplus Review Period Duration will be 30 days. NOTE this review period will only apply to Push policies where identified surplus is pushed out of the facility. In instances where Pull policies are evaluating surplus, a surplus review will happen at the time the pull request lands at the Facility. | [Time] | [] |  |
| SurplusReviewPeriodUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | SurplusReviewPeriod |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines Surplus Review Period. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| DOSWindow | double | PositiveNumeric |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The number of days' worth of throughput that will be tracked for the Facility/Product combination when determining the Days of Supply (DOS) level. Throughput will be summed over this window then divided by the length of the window to obtain a single-day DOS estimate. This DOS estimate can be used as the unit of measure in inventory constraints. | [Time] | [] |  |
| DOSWindowUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | DOSWindow |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines the length of the DOS Window. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| DOSLeadTime | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The length of time to offset the DOS Window by when determining the DOS value for the Facility/Product combination. | [Time] | [] |  |
| DOSLeadTimeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | DOSLeadTime |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines DOS Lead Time. If DOS Lead Time = 5 and DOS Lead Time UOM Symbol = Day, the end point of the DOS Window will be five days prior to the current simulation time. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| DOSReviewPeriodFirstTime | Date/Time |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The first Date/Time at which to calculate DOS for the Facility/Product combination. If NULL, DOS Review Period First Time will be the start of the simulation. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| DOSReviewPeriod | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The time between DOS reviews. After DOS Review Period First Time, a DOS review will occur each time DOS Review Period has elapsed. If NULL, DOS Review Period will be 30 days. | [Time] | [] |  |

| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| SourceName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Facilities, Suppliers |  | [HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | The Facility or Supplier that the demand will be served from. | [NaN] | [] | [Facilities.FacilityName, Suppliers.SupplierName] |  |
| CustomerName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Customers |  | [HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | The name of the Customer receiving the demand |  |  | CustomerName |  |

### シート: Lookups

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| LookupName | Name |  |  |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The name assigned to this lookup. This name can be referenced in other input tables, at which point the lookup will attempt to pull in the Source Value based on the mappings defined in the Source Column and Destination Column fields. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Source | Name |  |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The location where SourceTable can be found. If the data exists in another database, put the database name here. If blank, SourceTable must be part of this Frog Model. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| SourceTable | Name |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The table that will serve as the data source for the lookup. Values will be pulled from the column listed in the Source Value field based on the mappings specified in the Source Column and Destination Column Mapping fields | [NaN] | [] |  |
| DestinationTable | Name |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The table in the ANURA database that the lookup will be used in. |  | [] |  |
| SourceColumnMapping | Name |  |  |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The Source Column Mapping and Destination Column Mapping fields will specify the columns in the listed Source Table and the Input Table where the lookup is referenced that a join will be defined over. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| DestinationColumnMapping | Name |  |  |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The Source Column Mapping and Destination Column Mapping fields will specify the columns in the listed Source Table and the Input Table where the lookup is referenced that a join will be defined over. The Destination Column will support the use of a dot operator to reference columns in the associated master table, if applicable. For example, the Destination Column could be listed as ProductName.UnitWeight and this would be referencing the Unit Weight entry in the Products table for whatever product the lookup is being evaluated over. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| IsRangeLookup | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The Is Range Lookup field will define whether or not the mapping for the Source / Destination Column mapping fields should be an exact match (Range Lookup = FALSE) or a ranged lookup (Range Lookup = True). If a value of True is entered, the value from the Source column will be evaluated against those in the Destination Column where the Destination values will serve as lower bounds for the different bands. |  | [] |  |
| SourceValue | Name |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The column from the Source Table that is the be populated into the input filed where the Lookup is called. |  | [] |  |
| DefaultValue | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | If a lookup fails to return a value for a record, the Default Value will be inserted instead. | [NaN] | [] |  |

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Option | Name |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, HOPPER] |  |  |  | Validation rules, allowable values to be specified in hash table | Models run using either Optimization or Simulation will have additional run options that can be adjusted. The name of those options will be listed in this field, with the option setting, description, and impacted technology in fields to follow | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, HOPPER] |  |  | Exclude |  | The Status of the Model Run Options, all will be set to Exclude by default which will allow for the solver-specified defaults to be used. If users wish to use their own settings for runs, they can adjust the status to be Include and set the appropriate Value | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Value | String |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, HOPPER] |  |  |  | Validation rules, allowable values to be specified in hash table | This field will specify the value that the solver option is taking | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Technology | String |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, HOPPER] |  |  |  | Validation rules, allowable values to be specified in hash table | This field will note which technology the solver option will apply for. This can be quickly filtered against to only show options that are relevant to the technology which you are currently running | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Description | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, HOPPER] |  |  |  | Validation rules, allowable values to be specified in hash table | This field will offer a description as to the functionality of the run option. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| DataType | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | For use in Cosmic Frog, this passes over the Data Type to show which options to render in the UI | [NaN] | [] |  |
| UIDisplayName | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | For use in Cosmic Frog, this passes over the parameter's display name to be shown in the run options splash screen. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| UIDisplayCategory | [Basic, Advanced, None] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | For use in Cosmic Frog, this is a toggle to show which section of the splash screen, if any, the option should be displayed in. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| UIDisplaySubCategory | [Configurations, Termination, Network, Output Reporting, Cost To Serve, Troubleshooting, Infeasibility] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, HOPPER, DENDRO, TRIAD] |  |  |  |  | For use in Cosmic Frog, this is a toggle to show which sub-section of the splash screen, if any, the option should be displayed in. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| UIDisplayOrder | int |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | For use in Cosmic Frog, this will control the ordering in which options are displayed. | [NaN] | [] |  |

If the Warmup End Date falls before the Model Start Date, then there will be no warmup period in the simulation. | [NaN] | [THROG] |  |

If no value is specified, CO2 will not be restricted at a network-wide level. Specific CO2 constraints can be modelled through Flow or Production Constraints where CO2 is the selected UOM. | [Cost] | [] |  |

| AllowProductMix | [True, False] |  |  |  |  | Products |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | True |  | If Allow Product Mix is set to be True, orders can be made up of a mixture between the ordered and substitute products. If Allow Product Mix is set to False, in the even that a substitute product is used it must fulfill the entire requirement for the ordered product. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| SubstituteProduct | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  | Yes | Products |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The name of the substitute product | [NaN] | [] | ProductName |
| SubstituteProductPriority | int |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The priority for this substitute product | [Integer] | [] |  |

### シート: OrderFulfillmentPolicies

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| FacilityName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Facilities | Yes | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The Facility that the policy record applies to. | [NaN] | [] | FacilityName |
| AllowPartialFillOrders | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | True |  | If True, orders fulfilled by the Facility may be partially filled. Otherwise, orders must be filled in full. A value of False overrides a value of True in the Allow Partial Fill Lines field. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| AllowPartialFillLineItems | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | True |  | If True, line items within orders fulfilled by the Facility may be partially filled. Otherwise, line items must be filled in full. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ReviewPeriodFirstTime | Date/Time |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The first Date/Time at which to review order queues at the specified Facility. Order queues will be evaluated using the logic specified in Order Assignment Policies. After this Date/Time, the review period length will dictate when order queue reviews occur. If NULL, Review Period First Time will be the start of the simulation. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ReviewPeriod | Cost Time Expression | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | ReviewSchedules |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The time between order queue reviews. After Review Period First Time, an order queue review will occur each time Review Period has elapsed. For example, if Review Period First Time is 1/1/2022 12:00:00 AM and Review Period is six hours, we will conduct reviews on 1/1/2022 at 12:00:00 AM, 6:00:00 AM, 12:00:00 PM, and 6:00:00 PM, continuing similarly until the simulation ends. A Review Schedule defined in the Review Schedules table can be entered by Review Schedule Name in this field as well. If NULL, continuous review is used. | [Time] | [] | ReviewScheduleName |
| ReviewPeriodUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines Review Period length. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| QueueingPriorityLogic | [FIFO, ByDueDateAndLocation, ByDayQueuedAndLocation] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | FIFO |  | The logic used at the Facility both when deciding how to arrange orders in the order queue and when evaluating the order queue to determine if an order should be repositioned. ByDueDateAndLocation will fulfill the orders that are due the soonest first, and in the case of ties will prioritize orders based on the destination priority set in either the Customers, Facilities, or the Advanced Queueing Priorities table. ByDayQueuedAndLocation will place the items that have been in the queue the longest first, and apply the same prioritization logic as described for ByDueDateAndLocation | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the policy exists in the model. If Status is set to Exclude, the policy record is ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| TimeInQueueCost | double |  |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The cost associated with an item sitting in a queue. The cost expressed in this field will be charged each time that items sit in a queue for the length of time specified in the Time In Queue Cost UOM field. | [Cost] | [] |  |
| TimeInQueueCostUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  | TimeInQueueCost |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure that time is expressed in terms of. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |

### シート: OrderProfileProductAffinity

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| OrderID | String |  |  |  | YES |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The Order ID as specified in any of the Order Profile tables for which the secondary product selection likelihoods will apply. If left empty, the product affinity details will apply over all order profiles. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| PrimaryProduct | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  | YES |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The Primary Product drawn for multi line order profiles, the selection percentages of the primary product are specified in the Order Profile Product Selection table. | [NaN] | [] | ProductName |
| SecondaryProduct | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  | YES |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | Given the selected Primary Product, Secondary Products pairings will specify which additional products would be potentially ordered along on the additional line items. If no secondary products are specified, only the primary product will be ordered. | [NaN] | [] | ProductName |
| SecondaryProductSelectionPercentage | double | Percentage |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The likelihood that the secondary product is selected as an additional line item to be ordered with the primary product. If selected, the quantity listed for this product in the Order Profile Product Selection table will be used to determine the line item quantity. | [Percentage] | [] |  |

### シート: OrderProfileProductSelection

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| OrderID | String |  |  |  | YES |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The Order ID as specified in any of the Order Profile tables for which the product selection likelihoods will apply. If left empty, the product selection details will apply over all order profiles. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| PrimaryProduct | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  | YES | Products |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The product name that will be selected as the primary product. For Order Profiles that contain multiple lines, the primary product will be selected first and then draws will occur to fill the products of the remaining line items based on the likelihood of selection as defined in the OrderProfileProductAffinity table. | [NaN] | [] | ProductName |
| SelectionPercentage | double | Percentage |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The percentage chance that the listed product will be selected as the primary product for the order. When selecting a primary product, the SIM will generate a probability distribution for all possible primary products and will draw one product to be used as the primary product of the order. | [Percentage] | [] |  |
| OrderQuantity | double | PositiveNumeric |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The quantity of product to be ordered. If the Order Quantity was filled out in the Order Profile record, this quantity entry will be ignored. | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| OrderQuantityUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines Quantity. Quantity, Volume, and Weight units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| OrderProportion | double |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | If the Order Quantity was filled out in the Order Profile table, the Order Proportion field will be used to determine how the Order Profile Quantity will be divided amongst the products that were selected to make up the line items. | [Percentage] | [] |  |

| InScope | [True, False] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  | True | Defines whether or not we want the outputs related to the members of this organization to be included in the objective function | If True, costs associated with the Organization are included in the model's objective function (MIP) and cost calculations (SIM). ### シート: OutputFactors

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| OutputFactorName | Name |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The name of the Output Factor; Output Factors allow a user to build a utility curve for a selection of simulation metrics which will be used in a genetic algorithm (GA). The GA will iteratively run the simulation and modify certain input parameters (defined in the Input Factors table) to improve the model outcomes according to the Utility Level field of the Output Factors table. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| TableName | Name | Valid Table Name | Yes |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The name of the output table that contains the target Output Factor. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ColumnName | Name | Valid Column Name | Yes |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The column of the specified table that contains the simulation metric that the genetic algorithm will seek to improve. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Filter | String | Valid Filter |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The filter to apply to the specified column and table (optional). | [NaN] | [] |  |
| UtilityCurve | String |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | A series of pairings that will build the utility curve for the specified Output Factor. These pairs should be entered as [ Level 1 ｜ Utility Point 1 ][ Level 2 ｜ Utility Point 2 ]â€¦ A piecewise linear utility function of the specified Output Factor will be constructed using the given levels and points; this function will be used to evaluate performance of the genetic algorithm as it makes changes to Input Factors. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| OutputFactorWeight | double |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | 1 |  | The weight that is applied to the Utility Curve when calculating the listed Output Factor's impact on the overall utility function. Weight is used in the case of multiple output factors being specified so that the weight of each factor can be expressed relative to the other factors being considered. | [Percentage] | [] |  |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the Output Factor is considered when running the genetic algorithm. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |

### シート: Periods

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| PeriodName | Name |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The name of the Period. Periods allow for the model to be broken down into smaller sections of time, each with its own modeling inputs. Optimization will require periods to divide the model into smaller time buckets other than the model horizon whereas Simulation will have a rolling clock. Simulation can still interpret changes that are made through Multi Time Period records, however this will not be required for Simulation models. | [NaN] | [NEO, CYCLO] |  |
| StartDate | Date/Time |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | Periods are each defined based on their Start Date value. A Period extends from its Start Date to the soonest Start Date of the other defined Periods. NOTE that for optimization models that will contain cross-period flows (the transportation/production time exceeds that of the period length) then the periods are required to be of equal length. | [NaN] | [NEO, CYCLO] |  |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [NEO, CYCLO] |  |

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | Guru Table2 | Guru Column2 | Guru Table3 | Guru Column3 | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| ProcessName | Name |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The name of the Process; Processes are a single path of sequential processing steps that can be used to describe Production, Inbound/Outbound Warehousing, and Inbound/Outbound Transportation behavior. | InventoryProcessSteps | ProcessName | TransportationProcessSteps | ProcessName | [NaN] | [] |  |
| StepName | Name |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The name of the Process Step being defined; each Step can have its own unit cost, processing time, component consumption, and byproduct production details. | InventoryProcessSteps | ProcessStepName | TransportationProcessSteps | ProcessStepName | [NaN] | [] |  |
| StepNumber | int | NonNegative | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The position of the specified Step in the Process sequence. |  |  |  |  | [Integer] | [] |  |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the Step exists in the Process. If Status is set to Exclude, the Step will be ignored during the model run. | InventoryProcessSteps | InventoryProcessStepsStatus | TransportationProcessSteps | TransportationProcessStepsStatus | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ComponentName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | Products |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The name of the Component (optional) consumed at the specified Process Step; at most one Component can be associated with each Step. If a BOM is associated with the specified Process via a production policy but consumption of a BOM Component is not specified in a Process Step, the Component will be consumed during the first Process Step. A Component may be consumed during a Process Step without explicitly being required by a BOM. |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] | ProductName |
| ComponentQuantity | double | PositiveNumeric |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The amount of the Component consumed during the Process Step. If a BOM is associated with the Process via a production policy and the Component is a member of the BOM, this amount will be compared to the Component's required amount in the BOM. In the event that the total amount of the Component consumed across all Steps of the Process is less than the BOM requirement, the difference will be consumed at the first Step of the Process. Otherwise, the Component Quantity specified at each Step will be consumed accordingly. |  |  |  |  | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| ComponentQuantityUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | ComponentQuantity |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines Component Quantity. Quantity, Volume, and Weight units of measure are supported. |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| ByproductName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | Products |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The name of the Byproduct (optional) produced at the specified Process Step; at most one Byproduct can be associated with each Step. If a BOM is associated with the specified Process via a production policy but production of a Byproduct is not specified in a Process Step, the Byproduct will be produced during the final Process Step. A Byproduct may be produced during a Process Step without explicitly being specified in a BOM. |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] | ProductName |
| ByproductQuantity | double | PositiveNumeric |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The amount of the Byproduct produced during the Process Step. If a BOM is associated with the Process via a production policy and the Byproduct is a member of the BOM, this amount will be compared to the Byproduct's amount in the BOM. In the event that the total amount of the Byproduct produced across all Steps of the Process is less than the BOM requirement, the difference will be produced at the final Step of the Process. Otherwise, the Byproduct Quantity specified at each Step will be consumed accordingly. |  |  |  |  | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| ByproductQuantityUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | ByproductQuantity |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines Byproduct Quantity. Quantity, Volume, and Weight units of measure are supported. |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| EndProductName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | [Products, CategoricalDistributions] |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The name of the End Product that will be introduced as the result of the Process Step. |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] | [Products.ProductName, CategoricalDistributions.DistributionName] |
| EndProductQuantity | double | PositiveNumeric |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The amount of the End Product that is introduced as the result of the specified Process Step. |  |  |  |  | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| EndProductQuantityUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | EndProductQuantity |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines End Product Quantity. Quantity, Volume, and Weight units of measure are supported. |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| WorkCenterName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | WorkCenters |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | None | Optimization: Currently supports single WorkCenter entries, WorkCenter groups are not supported  Simulation: Currently supports single WorkCenter entries, WorkCenter groups are not supported | The Work Center assigned to the Process Step; the processing time and capacity consumed by the Process Step will count toward the capacity of the Work Center. Work Center Groups are not supported. | InventoryProcessSteps | WorkCenterName | TransportationProcessSteps | WorkCenterName | [NaN] | [] | WorkCenterName |
| WorkResourceName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | WorkResources |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | None |  | The Work Resource assigned to the Process Step; the processing time and capacity consumed by the Process Step will count toward the capacity of the Work Resource. If a Work Resource Group is entered into this field, the Process will be created for each group member (i.e., the group behavior will be Enumerate). | InventoryProcessSteps | WorkResourceName | TransportationProcessSteps | WorkResourceName | [NaN] | [] | WorkResourceName |
| ProcessingRate | double | PositiveNumeric |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The production rate for the given Process Step. It will be defined as Quantity/Time as is specified in the Quantity/Time UOM fields. If there are is a production rate defined in the Production Policies table where the Process is called, the information in the Process table will override any information that exists in the Production Policies table | InventoryProcessSteps | UnitTime | TransportationProcessSteps | UnitTime | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| RateQuantityUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | ProcessingRate |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The UOM that defines the number of units that the Production Rate is expressed in. This field can support a single numeric value, a step cost lookup (defined in Step Costs), or a custom cost function. | InventoryProcessSteps | UnitCost | TransportationProcessSteps | UnitCost | [Cost] | [] | StepCostName |
| UnitCostUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | UnitCost |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines Unit Cost. Quantity, Volume, and Weight units of measure are supported. |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| FixedTime | Cost Time Expression | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  | Optimization: Currently supports doubles  Simulation: Currently supports doubles | The fixed time that is applied if the Process Step is used. This is how NEO will apply the concept of Changeover Time | InventoryProcessSteps | AvgChangeoverTime | TransportationProcessSteps | AvgChangeoverTime | [Time] | [] |  |
| FixedTimeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | FixedTime |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines the Fixed Time |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |

| FixedCostRule | [Prorate, Treat As Full] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Prorate |  | The rule by which Fixed Cost is applied to the estimated number of lots. If the selected rule is Treat as Full, the estimated number of lots will be rounded up and the fixed cost will be applied accordingly. |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |
