### シート: ProcessesMultiTimePeriod

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| PeriodName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Periods | Yes | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The period name in which a change is being made. Groups of periods are also supported. Changes that are made in the Multi Time Period table will be applied as overrides for the records of the base table for the specified periods only. Additionally, only the fields that contain information needing to be updated are required to be entered, in the absence of data in the Multi Time Period table the data from the standard table will be persisted | [NaN] | [] | PeriodName |
| ProcessName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Processes | Yes | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The name of the Process; Processes are a single path of sequential processing steps that can be used to describe Production, Inbound/Outbound Warehousing, and Inbound/Outbound Transportation behavior. | [NaN] | [] | ProcessName |
| StepName | Name |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The name of the Process Step being defined; each Step can have its own unit cost, processing time, component consumption, and byproduct production details. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the multi-time period record exists in the model. If Status is set to Exclude, the record is ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ProcessStepStatus | [Include, Exclude] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | Process Step Status dictates whether or not the policy exists in the model for the specified period(s). If Process Step Status is set to Exclude, the Process Step will be  ignored during the model run for the specified period(s). | [NaN] | [] |  |
| StepNumber | int | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The position of the specified Step in the Process sequence. | [Integer] | [] |  |
| ComponentName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | Products |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The name of the Component (optional) consumed at the specified Process Step; at most one Component can be associated with each Step. If a BOM is associated with the specified Routing via a production policy but consumption of a BOM Component is not specified in a Process Step, the Component will be consumed during the first Process Step. A Component may be consumed during a Process Step without explicitly being required by a BOM. | [NaN] | [] | ProductName |
| ComponentQuantity | double | PositiveNumeric |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The amount of the Component consumed during the Process Step. If a BOM is associated with the Process via a production policy and the Component is a member of the BOM, this amount will be compared to the Component's required amount in the BOM. In the event that the total amount of the Component consumed across all Steps of the Process is less than the BOM requirement, the difference will be consumed at the first Step of the Process. Otherwise, the Component Quantity specified at each Step will be consumed accordingly. | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| ComponentQuantityUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | ComponentQuantity |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines Component Quantity. Quantity, Volume, and Weight units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| ByproductName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | Products |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The name of the Byproduct (optional) produced at the specified Process Step; at most one Byproduct can be associated with each Step. If a BOM is associated with the specified Process via a production policy but production of a Byproduct is not specified in a Process Step, the Byproduct will be produced during the final Process Step. A Byproduct may be produced during a Process Step without explicitly being specified in a BOM. | [NaN] | [] | ProductName |
| ByproductQuantity | double | PositiveNumeric |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The amount of the Byproduct produced during the Process Step. If a BOM is associated with the Process via a production policy and the Byproduct is a member of the BOM, this amount will be compared to the Byproduct's amount in the BOM. In the event that the total amount of the Byproduct produced across all Steps of the Process is less than the BOM requirement, the difference will be produced at the final Step of the Process. Otherwise, the Byproduct Quantity specified at each Step will be consumed accordingly. | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| ByproductQuantityUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | ByproductQuantity |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines Byproduct Quantity. Quantity, Volume, and Weight units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| EndProductName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | Products |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The name of the End Product that will be introduced as the result of the Process Step. | [NaN] | [] | ProductName |
| EndProductQuantity | double | PositiveNumeric |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The amount of the End Product that is introduced as the result of the specified Process Step. | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| EndProductQuantityUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | EndProductQuantity |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines End Product Quantity. Quantity, Volume, and Weight units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| WorkCenterName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | WorkCenters |  | [NEO] |  |  | None | Optimization: Currently supports single WorkCenter entries, WorkCenter groups are not supported | The Work Center assigned to the Process Step; the processing time and capacity consumed by the Process Step will count toward the capacity of the Work Center. Work Center Groups are not supported. | [NaN] | [] | WorkCenterName |
| WorkResourceName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | WorkResources |  | [] |  |  | None |  | The Work Resource assigned to the Process Step; the processing time and capacity consumed by the Process Step will count toward the capacity of the Work Resource. If a Work Resource Group is entered into this field, the Process will be created for each group member (i.e., the group behavior will be Enumerate). | [NaN] | [] | WorkResourceName |
| ProcessingRate | double | PositiveNumeric |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The production rate for the given Process Step. It will be defined as Quantity/Time as is specified in the Quantity/Time UOM fields. If there are is a production rate defined in the Production Policies table where the Routing is called, the information in the Process table will override any information that exists in the Production Policies table | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| RateQuantityUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | ProcessingRate |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The UOM that defines the number of units that the Production Rate is expressed in. This can be any UOM of Type = Quantity, Weight, Volume. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| RateTimeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | ProcessingRate |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The UOM that defines the amount of time that the Production Rate is expressed in. This can be any UOM of Type = Time. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| UnitCost | Cost Time Expression | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE StepCosts.StepCostBehavior = [All Item, Incremental, Stepwise] |  |  |  | StepCosts |  | [NEO] |  |  |  | Optimization: Currently supports doubles and named step costs that are defined in the StepCosts table where StepCostBehavior = All Item, Incremental or Stepwise or a combination of Stepwise and All Item | The cost associated with each unit of flow that undergoes the specified Process Step. This field can support a single numeric value, a step cost lookup (defined in Step Costs), or a custom cost function. | [Cost] | [] | StepCostName |
| UnitCostUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | UnitCost |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines Unit Cost. Quantity, Volume, and Weight units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| FixedTime | Cost Time Expression | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  | Optimization: Currently supports doubles | The fixed time that is applied if the Process is used. This is how NEO will apply the concept of Changeover Time | [Time] | [] |  |
| FixedTimeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | FixedTime |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines the Fixed Time | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| FixedCost | Cost Time Expression |  |  |  |  | StepCosts |  | [NEO] |  |  |  | Optimization: Currently supports doubles and named step costs that are defined in the StepCosts table where StepCostBehavior = Stepwise | The fixed cost that is applied if the Process is used. This is how NEO will apply the concept of Changeover Costs | [Cost] | [] |  |
| FixedCostRule | [Prorate, Treat As Full] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Prorate |  | The rule by which Fixed Cost is applied to the estimated number of lots. The cost can be prorated in the event that the estimated number of lots is not an integer. If the selected rule is Treat as Full, the estimated number of lots will be rounded up and the fixed cost will be applied accordingly. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| LotSize | double | PositiveNumeric |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The Lot Size for the Process Step | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| LotSizeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | LotSize |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines the Lot Size. Quantity, Volume, and Weight units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| YieldPercentage | double | Percentage |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The yield percentage of the Process Step, if the field is left as null it will be assumed that the Yield is 100% and there is no loss | [Percentage] | [] |  |
| CO2EmissionRate | Cost Time Expression | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [] | [] |  |  |  | The rate of CO2 emissions for this process for the given period. | [Cost] | [] |  |
| CO2EmissionRateUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight, Time] |  | CO2EmissionRate |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  |  | [Quantity, Volume, Weight, Time] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Quantity, Volume, Weight, Time) that defines the CO2 Emission Rate. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |

### シート: ProcurementOrderProfiles

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| OrderID | String |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The unique identifier for each Order profile, as orders are generated based on their recurring properties they will be indexed off of the entered order number. All Orders will be placed as single line item orders, however multiple recurring orders can be set at the same Facility | [NaN] | [] |  |
| FacilityName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  |  | Facilities |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The name of the Facility placing the Order | [NaN] | [] | FacilityName |
| ProductName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  |  | Products |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The Product that the Order will be placed for | [NaN] | [] | ProductName |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the Order profile exists in the model. If Status is set to Exclude, this  record will be ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Quantity | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The amount of Product being ordered by the Facility. Both doubles, as well as distributions are accepted as entries. | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| QuantityUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  | Quantity |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines Quantity. Quantity, Volume, and Weight units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| NumberOfOrders | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | 1 |  | Based on the information entered in the Start Date, Time Between Orders and End Date fields, the Order Profile will generate orders based at different times throughout the simulation. When an order is generated, the Number Of Orders will be evaluated to determine how many orders are going to be placed at that specific time. Once the Number Of Orders is determined, all of the other information specified in the Product Name, Quantity, Number of Lines will be used to generate the details for each of the orders at that time. | [Integer] | [] |  |
| NumberOfLines | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | 1 |  | If the Product Name is left blank for the Order Profile, recurring orders can be generated with a varying number of line items in each order. An integer value, or a distribution can be entered in this field. In the event that the number of lines in an order is 2 or greater, the Order Profile Product Selection and Order Profile Product Affinity tables will be used to determine which products are to be ordered. | [Integer] | [] |  |
| ServiceLevel | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | Service Level will be used to calculate the Due Date for all of the generated recurring orders. Due Date will be set to the Order Date + Service Level. | [Time] | [] |  |
| ServiceLevelUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  | ServiceLevel |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) used to define Service Level. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| StartDate | Date/Time |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The Start Date will defined the date that the first order will drop. Orders will drop exactly at the date/time specified in Start Date, and then each following order's drop date will be determined by the Time Between Orders information. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| TimeBetweenOrders | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | Time Between Orders will specify how much time goes by from the previous order until the next order drops. Both doubles, as well as distribution entries are supported. | [Time] | [] |  |
| TimeBetweenOrdersUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  | TimeBetweenOrders |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) used to define Time Between Orders. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| EndDate | Date/Time |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The End Date will specify the last possible time that an order can drop. If the Time Between Orders causes the next order drop date to fall after the specified End Date, that last order will not be generated. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |

### シート: ProcurementOrders

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| OrderID | String - Fixed |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The unique identifier for each Order; an Order consists of one or more Line Items. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| LineItemID | String - Fixed |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The unique identifier for the single Line Item being defined; Orders with multiple Line Items will require multiple table records. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| FacilityName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  |  | Facilities |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The name of the Facility placing the Order. Facility Groups are supported; the Group behavior will be Enumerate. | [NaN] | [] | FacilityName |
| ProductName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  |  | Products |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The Product in the Line Item | [NaN] | [] | ProductName |
| Quantity | double | NonNegative | Yes |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The amount of Product requested by the Facility. | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| QuantityUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | Quantity |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines Quantity. Quantity, Volume, and Weight units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| OrderDate | Date/Time |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The date and time at which the Facility places the Order. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| DueDate | Date/Time |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The date and time at which the Order is due at the Facility. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the Line Item exists in the model. If Status is set to Exclude, this  record will be ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ServiceMode | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | TransportationModes |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | Optionally, a required Transportation Mode can be specified here. If multiple Modes are acceptable, a Mode Group may be used; the Group behavior will be Aggregate. If NULL, any Mode is assumed to be acceptable. | [NaN] | [] | ModeName |
| SourceName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | Suppliers |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | Optionally, a required Supplier for the Line Item can be specified here. If multiple Suppliers are acceptable, a Supplier Group may be used; the Group behavior will be Aggregate. If NULL, any Supplier of the Product is assumed to be acceptable. | [NaN] | [] | SupplierName |
| SingleSourceOrder | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | If True, all Line Items in the Order must be sourced from a single Supplier. If False, each Line Item within the Order may be sourced from a different Supplier. A value of True in this field overrides Single Source Line Items = False, enforcing that all Line Items in the order come from a single source. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| SingleSourceLineItems | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | If True, the entire Line Item must be sourced from a single Supplier. If False, the Line Item may be sourced from different sites. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| AllowPartialFillOrders | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | If True, the Order may be partially filled. Otherwise, it must be filled in full. If backordering is allowed, the remaining quantity may be satisfied in the future with additional shipments; if the Backorder Time Limit is reached on a partially filled order, the remaining quantity will be cancelled. If Partial Fill Orders is False, Partial Fill Line Items will also be forced to False. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| AllowPartialFillLineItems | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | If True, the specified Line Item may be partially filled. Otherwise, the Line Item must be filled in full. If backordering is allowed, the remaining quantity of partially filled Line Items may be satisfied in the future with additional shipments; if the Backorder Time Limit is reached on a partially filled Line Item, the remaining quantity will be cancelled. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| AllowBackorders | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | If True, the Facility will accept a backorder in the event that the Order cannot be fulfilled before its Due Date. If False and if the Order is not fulfilled by its Due Date, it will be cancelled on that date. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| BackorderTimeLimit | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | If Allow Backorders = True, this value is the amount of time after which the backorder will be cancelled by the Facility. A value of NULL will be interpreted as an infinite time limit, while a value of zero is equivalent to setting Allow Backorders = False. | [Time] | [] |  |
| BackorderTimeLimitUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | BackorderTimeLimit |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) used to define Backorder Time Limit. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |

### シート: ProcurementPolicies

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| FacilityName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Facilities | Yes | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The Facility that the policy record applies to. | [NaN] | [THROG] | FacilityName |
| ProductName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Products | Yes | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The Product that the policy record applies to. | [NaN] | [THROG] | ProductName |
| SourceName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Suppliers | Yes | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The Supplier that the policy record applies to. | [NaN] | [THROG] | SupplierName |
| OptimizationPolicy | [To Optimize, By Ratio (Auto Scale), By Ratio (No Scale), Single Source] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | To Optimize |  | The logic that procurement flows for the Facility, Product, and Source combination(s) must follow. To Optimize selects the lowest cost option. By Ratio (Auto Scale) will enforce a flow split between the specified sources, the defined ratios will apply to flow into the Facility from the listed sources only. By Ratio (No Scale) will enforce a flow split between the specified sources and the defined ratios will be in terms of the total inbound flow into the Facility. Single Source policies force the Facility to receive the Product from one Source. Note that if a group is used in the Facility Name, Product Name, or Source Name field that the groups will be enumerated and the policy logic will be applied over the enumerated entries. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| OptimizationPolicyValue | double | PositiveNumeric |  | OptimizationPolicy = By Ratio (Auto Scale) OR By Ratio (No Scale) |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | By Ratio (Auto Scale): The Policy Value will define the percentage of flow that must be satisfied from the listed source. The percentage will be calculated based on the total inbound flow for the Facility/Product combination from sources with a policy of By Ratio (Auto Scale).  By Ratio (No Scale): The Policy Value will define the percentage of flow that must be satisfied from the listed source. The percentage will be calculated based on the total inbound flow for the Facility/Product combination across ALL sources. | [Percentage] | [] |  |
| SimulationPolicy | [By Preference, Single Source, Allocation, By Distance] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | By Preference |  | The logic that procurement flows for the Facility, Product, and Source combination(s) must follow. By Preference will pick the sources pending product availability in the specified order of preference. Single Source policies force the Facility to receive the Product from one Source. Allocation will source as much as possible from the sources based on availability in the specified order of preference - this is similar to By Preference but the Allocation policy will not attempt to keep orders together from a single source. By Distance will select the closest source using straight-line distances. | [NaN] | [THROG] |  |
| SimulationPolicyValue | double | PositiveNumeric |  | SimulationPolicy = By Preference |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | By Preference: Policy Value defines the order of preference in which a source will be selected pending product availability, with the lower numbers representing a higher selection preference. Allocation: Policy value defines the order in which sources will be selected. | [Percentage] | [THROG] |  |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the policy exists in the model. If Status is set to Exclude, the policy record is ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [THROG] |  |
| UnitCost | Cost Time Expression | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE StepCosts.StepCostBehavior = [All Item, Incremental, Stepwise] |  |  |  | StepCosts |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | 0 | Optimization: Currently supports doubles and named step costs that are defined in the StepCosts table where StepCostBehavior = All Item, Incremental or Stepwise or a combination of Stepwise and All Item  Simulation: Currently supports doubles | The unit cost associated with flow for the specified Supplier/Facility/Product combination. This field can support a single numeric value, a step cost (either a direct entry or a lookup), or a custom cost function. | [Cost] | [] | StepCostName |
| UnitCostUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | UnitCost |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure used to define flow for the specified Supplier/Facility/Product combination. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| MinimumOrderQuantity | double | PositiveNumeric |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The minimum quantity that must move between source and destination in a period for a flow to occur | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| MinimumOrderQuantityUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | MinimumOrderQuantity |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines the MOQ for the Supplier/Facility/Product combination. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| LotSize | double | PositiveNumeric |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | Orders placed from the Customer to the specified sources must be filled in multiples of the specified lot size. | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| LotSizeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | LotSize |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines the Lot Size for the Source/Customer/Product combination. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| MaxSourcingRange | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The maximum allowable distance between the Facility and Source for the Product(s) specified. This field is intended for use in models with group policies to eliminate excessively long lanes from consideration. If all sources exceed the max sourcing range for a Facility-Product pairing, the closest source will be kept by default - this behavior can be overwritten through the Keep Closest Lane For Max Sourcing Distance run option. | [Distance] | [] |  |
| MaxSourcingRangeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Distance] |  | MaxSourcingRange |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Distance] |  | {PrimaryDistanceUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Distance) that defines Max Delivery Range. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [THROG] |  |

### シート: ProcurementPoliciesMultiTimePe0

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| PeriodName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Periods | Yes | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The period name in which a change is being made. Groups of periods are also supported. Changes that are made in the Multi Time Period table will be applied as overrides for the records of the base table for the specified periods only. Additionally, only the fields that contain information needing to be updated are required to be entered, in the absence of data in the Multi Time Period table the data from the standard table will be persisted | [NaN] | [] | PeriodName |
| FacilityName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Facilities | Yes | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The Facility that the policy record applies to. | [NaN] | [] | FacilityName |
| ProductName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Products | Yes | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The Product that the policy record applies to. | [NaN] | [] | ProductName |
| SourceName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Suppliers | Yes | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The Supplier that the policy record applies to. | [NaN] | [] | SupplierName |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the multi-time period record exists in the model. If Status is set to Exclude, the record is ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| PolicyStatus | [Include, Exclude] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | Policy Status dictates whether or not the policy exists in the model for the specified period(s). If Policy Status is set to Exclude, the policy record is ignored during the model run for the specified periods. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| OptimizationPolicy | [To Optimize, By Ratio (Auto Scale), By Ratio (No Scale), Single Source] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The logic that procurement flows for the Facility, Product, and Source combination(s) must follow. To Optimize selects the lowest cost option. By Ratio (Auto Scale) will enforce a flow split between the specified sources, the defined ratios will apply to flow into the Facility from the listed sources only. By Ratio (No Scale) will enforce a flow split between the specified sources and the defined ratios will be in terms of the total inbound flow into the Facility. Single Source policies force the Facility to receive the Product from one Source. Note that if a group is used in the Period Name, Facility Name, Product Name, or Source Name field that the groups will be enumerated and the policy logic will be applied over the enumerated entries. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| OptimizationPolicyValue | double | PositiveNumeric |  | OptimizationPolicy = By Ratio (Auto Scale) OR By Ratio (No Scale) |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | By Ratio (Auto Scale): The Policy Value will define the percentage of flow that must be satisfied from the listed source. The percentage will be calculated based on the total inbound flow for the Facility/Product combination from sources with a policy of By Ratio (Auto Scale).  By Ratio (No Scale): The Policy Value will define the percentage of flow that must be satisfied from the listed source. The percentage will be calculated based on the total inbound flow for the Facility/Product combination across ALL sources. | [Percentage] | [] |  |
| SimulationPolicy | [By Preference, Single Source, Allocation, By Distance] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The logic that procurement flows for the Facility, Product, and Source combination(s) must follow. By Preference will pick the sources pending product availability in the specified order of preference. Single Source policies force the Facility to receive the Product from one Source. Allocation will source as much as possible from the sources based on availability in the specified order of preference - this is similar to By Preference but the Allocation policy will not attempt to keep orders together from a single source. By Distance will select the closest source using straight-line distances. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| SimulationPolicyValue | double | PositiveNumeric |  | SimulationPolicy = By Preference |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | By Preference: Policy Value defines the order of preference in which a source will be selected pending product availability, with the lower numbers representing a higher selection preference. Allocation: Policy value defines the order in which sources will be selected. | [Percentage] | [] |  |
| UnitCost | Cost Time Expression | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE StepCosts.StepCostBehavior = [All Item, Incremental, Stepwise] |  |  |  | StepCosts |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  | Optimization: Currently supports doubles and named step costs that are defined in the StepCosts table where StepCostBehavior = All Item, Incremental or Stepwise or a combination of Stepwise and All Item | The unit cost associated with flow for the specified Supplier/Facility/Product combination. This field can support a single numeric value, a step cost (either a direct entry or a lookup), or a custom cost function. | [Cost] | [] | StepCostName |
| UnitCostUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | UnitCost |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure used to define flow for the specified Supplier/Facility/Product combination. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| MinimumOrderQuantity | double | PositiveNumeric |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The minimum quantity that must move between source and destination in a period for a flow to occur | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| MinimumOrderQuantityUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | MinimumOrderQuantity |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines the MOQ for the Supplier/Facility/Product combination. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| LotSize | double | PositiveNumeric |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | Orders placed from the Facility to the specified sources must be filled in multiples of the specified lot size. | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| LotSizeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | LotSize |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines the Lot Size for the Source/Facility/Product combination. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| MaxSourcingRange | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The maximum allowable distance between the Facility and Source for the Product(s) specified. This field is intended for use in models with group policies to eliminate excessively long lanes from consideration. If all sources exceed the max sourcing range for a Facility-Product pairing, the closest source will be kept by default - this behavior can be overwritten through the Keep Closest Lane For Max Sourcing Distance run option. | [Distance] | [] |  |
| MaxSourcingRangeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Distance] |  | MaxSourcingRange |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Distance] |  | {PrimaryDistanceUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Distance) that defines Max Delivery Range. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |

### シート: ProductionConstraints

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| FacilityName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Facilities | Yes | [NEO] |  |  | ALL |  | The Facility or Facilities at which we are constraining production. Groups are supported. | [NaN] | [] | FacilityName |
| FacilityNameGroupBehavior | [Enumerate, Aggregate] |  |  |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Aggregate |  | If Facility Name is a Group, use this field to specify the Group's behavior. If Enumerate is selected, an instance of this constraint will be created for each member of the Group. If Aggregate is selected, the flow in the constraint will be summed over the members of the Group. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ProductName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Products | Yes | [NEO] |  |  | ALL |  | The Product(s) for which we are constraining production. Groups are supported. | [NaN] | [] | ProductName |
| ProductNameGroupBehavior | [Enumerate, Aggregate] |  |  |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Aggregate |  | If Product Name is a Group, use this field to specify the Group's behavior. If Enumerate is selected, an instance of this constraint will be created for each member of the Group. If Aggregate is selected, the flow in the constraint will be summed over the members of the Group. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| PeriodName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Periods | Yes | [NEO] |  |  | ALL |  | The Period(s) in which we are constraining production. Groups are supported. | [NaN] | [] | PeriodName |
| PeriodNameGroupBehavior | [Enumerate, Aggregate] |  |  |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Aggregate |  | If Period Name is a Group, use this field to specify the Group's behavior. If Enumerate is selected, an instance of this constraint will be created for each member of the Group. If Aggregate is selected, the flow in the constraint will be summed over the members of the Group. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| BOMName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  | Yes | BillsOfMaterials | Yes | [NEO] |  |  | ALL |  | The Bill(s) of Materials (BOMs) associated with the production we are constraining (optional). If a BOM is not specified in the Production Policies table for this Facility/Product combination, the constraint will be ignored and the user will be alerted. If left empty, the production constraint will be applied for productions that use a BOM as well as those which do not. To specifically constrain against productions that do not use a BOM, enter a value of 'None'. | [NaN] | [] | BOMName |
| BOMNameGroupBehavior | [Enumerate, Aggregate] |  |  |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Aggregate |  | If Bill of Material Name is a Group, use this field to specify the Group's behavior. If Enumerate is selected, an instance of this constraint will be created for each member of the Group. If Aggregate is selected, the flow in the constraint will be summed over the members of the Group. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ProcessName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  | Yes | Processes | Yes | [NEO] |  |  | ALL |  | The Process(es) associated with the production we are constraining (optional). If a Process is not specified in the Production Policies table for this Facility/Product combination, the constraint will be ignored and the user will be alerted.If left empty, the production constraint will be applied for productions that use a Process as well as those which do not. To specifically constrain against productions that do not use a Process, enter a value of 'None'. | [NaN] | [] | ProcessName |
| ProcessNameGroupBehavior | [Enumerate, Aggregate] |  |  |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Aggregate |  | If Process Name is a Group, use this field to specify the Group's behavior. If Enumerate is selected, an instance of this constraint will be created for each member of the Group. If Aggregate is selected, the flow in the constraint will be summed over the members of the Group. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ConstraintType | [Min, Max, Fixed, Fixed With Tolerance, Conditional Min] |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The type of constraint imposed on production for the specified Facility/Product combination.   Min: the number of units produced must be greater than or equal to the Constraint Value. Max: the number of units produced must be less than or equal to the Constraint Value. Fixed: the number of units produced must be equal to the Constraint Value. Fixed With Tolerance: The number of units produced must be equal to the Constraint Value * (1 +/- Relative Constraint Tolerance). Relative Constraint Tolerance is specified in the Model Run Options. Conditional Min: if the number of units produced is nonzero, it must be greater than or equal to the Constraint Value. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ConstraintValue | double | NonNegative | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The numeric value that the specified production level will be evaluated against. | [Constraint] | [] |  |
| ConstraintValueUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | ConstraintValue | Yes | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines Constraint Value. Quantity, Volume, and Weight units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the constraint exists in the model. If Status is set to Exclude, this constraint (or constraints in the case of Enumerated Groups) will be ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |

### シート: ProductionOrderProfiles

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| OrderID | String |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The unique identifier for each Order profile, as orders are generated based on their recurring properties they will be indexed off of the entered order number. All Orders will be placed as single line item orders, however multiple recurring orders can be set at the same Facility | [NaN] | [] |  |
| FacilityName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  |  | Facilities |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The name of the Facility at which the Production Order will be placed | [NaN] | [] | FacilityName |
| ProductName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  |  | Products |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The Product that the Order will be placed for | [NaN] | [] | ProductName |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the Order profile exists in the model. If Status is set to Exclude, this  record will be ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Quantity | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The amount of Product being scheduled for production at the Facility. Both doubles, as well as distributions are accepted as entries. | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| QuantityUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  | Quantity |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines Quantity. Quantity, Volume, and Weight units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| NumberOfOrders | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | 1 |  | Based on the information entered in the Start Date, Time Between Orders and End Date fields, the Order Profile will generate orders based at different times throughout the simulation. When an order is generated, the Number Of Orders will be evaluated to determine how many orders are going to be placed at that specific time. Once the Number Of Orders is determined, all of the other information specified in the Product Name, Quantity, Number of Lines will be used to generate the details for each of the orders at that time. | [Integer] | [] |  |
| NumberOfLines | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | 1 |  | If the Product Name is left blank for the Order Profile, recurring orders can be generated with a varying number of line items in each order. An integer value, or a distribution can be entered in this field. In the event that the number of lines in an order is 2 or greater, the Order Profile Product Selection and Order Profile Product Affinity tables will be used to determine which products are to be ordered. | [Integer] | [] |  |
| ServiceLevel | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | Service Level will be used to calculate the Due Date for all of the generated recurring orders. Due Date will be set to the Order Date + Service Level. | [Time] | [] |  |
| ServiceLevelUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  | ServiceLevel |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) used to define Service Level. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| StartDate | Date/Time |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The Start Date will defined the date that the first order will drop. Orders will drop exactly at the date/time specified in Start Date, and then each following order's drop date will be determined by the Time Between Orders information. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| TimeBetweenOrders | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | Time Between Orders will specify how much time goes by from the previous order until the next order drops. Both doubles, as well as distribution entries are supported. | [Time] | [] |  |
| TimeBetweenOrdersUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  | TimeBetweenOrders |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) used to define Time Between Orders. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| EndDate | Date/Time |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The End Date will specify the last possible time that an order can drop. If the Time Between Orders causes the next order drop date to fall after the specified End Date, that last order will not be generated. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |

| LineItemID | String - Fixed |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The unique identifier for the single Line Item being defined; Orders with multiple Line Items will require multiple table records. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| FacilityName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  |  | Facilities |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The Facility at which the production order is placed. | [NaN] | [] | FacilityName |
| ProductName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  |  | Products |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The Product in the Line Item. Product Groups are supported. | [NaN] | [] | ProductName |
| Quantity | double | NonNegative | Yes |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The amount of the Product being ordered. This field will support probability distributions in the future. | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| QuantityUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | Quantity |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines Quantity. Quantity, Volume, and Weight units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| OrderDate | Date/Time |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The date and time at which the Order is placed. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| DueDate | Date/Time |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The date and time at which the Order must be completed by the Facility. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the Line Item exists in the model. If Status is set to Exclude, this  record will be ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ProcessName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | Processes |  | [] |  |  |  |  | Process required for the Line Item according to the Facility/Product combination can be specified here. In the event that the Facility does not have a production policy in place for this Product/Process combination, the Process will not be applied. This discrepancy will be reported to the user. | [NaN] | [] | ProcessName |
| AllowPartialFillOrders | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | True |  | If True, the Order may be partially filled. Otherwise, it must be filled in full. If backordering is allowed, the remaining quantity may be satisfied in the future; if the Backorder Time Limit is reached on a partially filled order, the remaining quantity will be cancelled. If Partial Fill Orders is False, Partial Fill Line Items will also be forced to False. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| AllowPartialFillLineItems | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | True |  | If True, the specified Line Item may be partially filled. Otherwise, the Line Item must be filled in full. If backordering is allowed, the remaining quantity of partially filled Line Items may be satisfied in the future with additional shipments; if the Backorder Time Limit is reached on a partially filled Line Item, the remaining quantity will be cancelled. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| AllowBackorders | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | True |  | If True, the Facility will accept a backorder in the event that the entirety of the Order cannot be produced before its Due Date. If False and if the Order is not produced by its Due Date, it will be cancelled on that date. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| BackorderTimeLimit | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | If Allow Backorders = True, this value is the amount of time after which the backorder will be cancelled by the Facility. A value of NULL will be interpreted as an infinite time limit, while a value of zero is equivalent to setting Allow Backorders = False. | [Time] | [] |  |
| BackorderTimeLimitUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | BackorderTimeLimit |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) used to define Backorder Time Limit. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |

### シート: ProductionPolicies

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| FacilityName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Facilities | Yes | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The Facility at which the production policy is being defined | [NaN] | [THROG, DENDRO, NEO] | FacilityName |
| ProductName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Products | Yes | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The Product for which the production policy is being defined | [NaN] | [THROG, DENDRO, NEO] | ProductName |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  | Include |  | The Status of the policy record, if this is set to Exclude the policy record is ignored during the model run | [NaN] | [THROG, DENDRO, NEO] |  |
| SimulationPolicy | [Make By Demand, Make By Schedule, Make By Demand And Schedule, Continuous Production] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | Make By Demand | Simulation: Currently supports policies of [Make By Demand, Make By Schedule, Continuous Production] | The policy that will dictate the production logic for the Facility/Product combination. Make By Demand will trigger production events based on the Production Orders which are triggered by the Inventory Policy for the Facility/Product combination. Make By Schedule will trigger production events based off of Production Orders specified in the Production Orders / Recurring Production Orders tables. Make By Demand And Schedule will trigger production orders based on both the Inventory Policy and the orders specified in the Production Orders / Recurring Production Orders tables. Continuous Production will continuously trigger production orders of the amount specified in the SIM Policy Lot Size field as soon as the production of the previous lot is completed. | [NaN] | [THROG] |  |
| UnitCost | Cost Time Expression | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE StepCosts.StepCostBehavior = [All Item, Incremental, Stepwise] |  |  |  | StepCosts |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | 0 | Optimization: Currently supports doubles and named step costs that are defined in the StepCosts table where StepCostBehavior = All Item, Incremental or Stepwise or a combination of Stepwise and All Item  Simulation: Currently supports doubles | The unit cost of a production flow at the Facility/Product combination. This field can support either a single numeric value, a lookup into a defined step cost, or a custom cost function entered directly into the field. If there are Processes defined, the information in the Processes table will override any information that exists in the Production Policies table | [Cost] | [THROG, DENDRO, NEO] | StepCostName |
| UnitCostUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | UnitCost |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The UOM that specifies what unit the Unit Cost will be applied over. This can be any UOM of Type = Quantity, Weight, Volume. If there are Processes defined, the information in the Processes table will override any information that exists in the Production Policies table | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| ProductionRate | double |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The production rate for the given Facility/Product combination. It will be defined as Quantity/Time as is specified in the Quantity/Time UOM fields. If there are Processes defined, the information in the Processes table will override any information that exists in the Production Policies table | [Quantity] | [THROG] |  |
| RateQuantityUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | ProductionRate |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The UOM that defines the number of units that are able to be produced on this Production Policy. This can be any UOM of Type = Quantity, Weight, Volume. If there are Processes defined, the information in the Processes table will override any information that exists in the Production Policies table | [NaN] | [THROG] | Symbol |
| RateTimeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | ProductionRate |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The UOM that defines the amount of time that the Production Rate is expressed in. This can be any UOM of Type = Time. If there are Processes defined, the information in the Processes table will override any information that exists in the Production Policies table | [NaN] | [THROG] | Symbol |
| WorkCenterName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  | Yes | WorkCenters |  | [NEO] |  |  | None | Optimization: Currently supports single WorkCenter entries, WorkCenter groups are not supported | The Work Center assigned to the Production Policy; the production time and capacity consumed by the production at the listed Facility / Product will count toward the capacity of the Work Center. If a Process is also specified for this Production Policy, then this Work Center field is ignored and the value from the Processes table will take precedence.  WorkCenter groups are not supported. | [NaN] | [] | WorkCenterName |
| BOMName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  | Yes | BillsOfMaterials | Yes | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | None | Optimization: Currently supports single Bill Of Material entries, Bill Of Material groups are not supported  Simulation: Currently supports single Bill Of Material entries, Bill Of Material groups are not supported | The Bill of Material record that will be associated with any productions that occur at the Facility/Product combination. If a BOM is specified, in order to produce the product for this policy record the production location must also be able to source all required components as entered in the BOM  BOM Groups are not currently supported. | [NaN] | [] | BOMName |
| ProcessName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  | Yes | Processes | Yes | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | None | Optimization: Currently supports single Process entries, Process Groups are not supported  Simulation: Currently supports single Process entries, Process Groups are not supported | The Process associated with the production behavior for the specified Facility/Product combination. Processes offer a more detailed step by step production behavior that can specify the work time per unit, which Work Centers or Work Resources must be used, and at which points a component is required in the production flow.  Process Groups are not currently supported. | [NaN] | [] | ProcessName |
| LotSize | double | PositiveNumeric |  | SimulationPolicy = Continuous Production |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | For SIM Policy = Continuous Production, the SIM Policy Lot Size will determine the size of each production order that is triggered for the Facility / Product combination. | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| LotSizeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  |  |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The UOM that specifies what unit the SIM Policy Lot Size will be defined in. This can be any UOM of Type = Quantity, Weight, Volume. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| TimeBetweenProductions | double | PositiveNumeric |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The amount of time between productions for this Facility / Product combination. Given the time between productions, the number of production runs will be calculated for the length of the period. Then, total production will be divided by the number of production runs to determine the Production Estimated Inventory. In the event that the Time Between Productions is longer than the length of the Period, the Production Estimated Inventory will be calculated based off of the proportion of the calculated production window that falls within the period. | [Time] | [] |  |
| TimeBetweenProductionsUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  | TimeBetweenProductions |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines the Time Between Productions. Time units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| CO2EmissionRate | Cost Time Expression | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] | [] |  | 0 |  | The rate of CO2 emissions for this production policy. | [Cost] | [] |  |
| CO2EmissionRateUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight, Time] |  | CO2EmissionRate |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight, Time] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Quantity, Volume, Weight, Time) that defines the CO2 Emission Rate. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| ReviewPeriodFirstTime | Date/Time |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The first Date/Time at which to review how much to produce of the Product at the specified Facility. This only applies to the Make to Forecast production policy | [NaN] | [THROG] |  |
| ReviewPeriod | Cost Time Expression | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | ReviewSchedules |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The time between production reviews. After Review Period First Time, a production review will occur each time Review Period has elapsed. For example, if Review Period First Time is 1/1/2022 12:00:00 AM and Review Period is six hours, we will conduct reviews on 1/1/2022 at 12:00:00 AM, 6:00:00 AM, 12:00:00 PM, and 6:00:00 PM, continuing similarly until the simulation ends. A Review Schedule defined in the Review Schedules table can be entered by Review Schedule Name in this field as well. | [Time] | [THROG] | ReviewScheduleName |
| ReviewPeriodUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | ReviewPeriod |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines Review Period. | [NaN] | [THROG] | Symbol |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [THROG, DENDRO, NEO] |  |

### シート: ProductionQueueDetails

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| FacilityName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Facilities |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The facility that the queue logic is applied for | [NaN] | [] | FacilityName |
| ReviewPeriodFirstTime | Date/Time |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The first Date/Time at which to review the queue for the specified production queue. Production queues will be evaluated using the logic specified in the Priority Logic field. After this Date/Time, the review period length will dictate when production queue reviews occur. If NULL, Review Period First Time will be the start of the simulation. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ReviewPeriod | Cost Time Expression | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | ReviewSchedules |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The time between production queue reviews. After Review Period First Time, a production queue review will occur each time Review Period has elapsed. For example, if Review Period First Time is 1/1/2022 12:00:00 AM and Review Period is six hours, we will conduct reviews on 1/1/2022 at 12:00:00 AM, 6:00:00 AM, 12:00:00 PM, and 6:00:00 PM, continuing similarly until the simulation ends. A Review Schedule defined in the Review Schedules table can be entered by Review Schedule Name in this field as well. If NULL, continuous review is used. | [Time] | [] | ReviewScheduleName |
| ReviewPeriodUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines Review Period length. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| QueueingPriorityLogic | [FIFO, ByDueDateAndLocation, ByDayQueuedAndLocation, ProductionWheel] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | FIFO |  | The logic used for the production queue when deciding how to arrange orders in the production queue. If a value of By Date And Location is selected, the QueuePriority fields in the Customers, Facilities, and Suppliers will be leveraged. If the Advanced Queueing Details table is populated, the override priorities for the Origin, Destination, Product combination will be used | [NaN] | [] |  |
| QueueingPriorityLogicValue | String |  |  |  |  | ProductionWheelDetails |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The value associated with the given queueing priority logic.  For ProductionWheel this will contain the name of the wheel | [NaN] | [] | ProductionWheelName |
| SecondaryQueueingPriorityLogic | [MinimizeChangeover] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | MinimizeChangeover |  | The logic used to break ties within the production queue | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the policy exists in the model. If Status is set to Exclude, the policy record is ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| TimeInQueueCost | double |  |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The cost associated with an item sitting in a queue. The cost expressed in this field will be charged each time that items sit in a queue for the length of time specified in the Time In Queue Cost UOM field. | [Cost] | [] |  |
| TimeInQueueCostUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  | TimeInQueueCost |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure that time is expressed in terms of. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |

### シート: ProductionWheelDetails

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| ProductionWheelName | Name |  | Yes |  | Yes |  | No | [] |  |  |  |  | The name of the production wheel | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ProductName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Products | Yes | [] |  |  |  |  | The Product for which the entry in the production wheel is being defined | [NaN] | [] | ProductName |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the row of the Production Wheel is passed into the engine. If Status is set to Exclude, this record will be ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| WheelOrder | double |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The position in the wheel for the given product | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Duration | double | PositiveNumeric |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The duration of the production wheel element. If the duration is hit while in the middle of processing an order, that order will complete. If the duration is not met and all orders are finished processing, the logic used in the Duration Policy will determine the production activity. | [Time] | [] |  |
| DurationUoM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  |  |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measurement for the duration. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| DurationPolicy | [Advance, Fill, Idle] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The policy to be used when the existing orders will not fill the entire duration. | [Time] | [] |  |
| MinProductionQuantity | double | PositiveNumeric |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | Minimum value to be produced for the defined production wheel slot | [Time] | [] |  |
| MaxProductionQuantity | double | PositiveNumeric |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | Maximum value to be produced for the defined production wheel slot | [Time] | [] |  |
| ProductionQuantityUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  |  |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The UOM that defines the Min / Max Production Quantities. Quantity, Volume and Weight UOM entries are supported. | [Time] | [] | Symbol |
| MinProductionPolicy | [Advance, Produce Min] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The policy to be used when the existing collection of orders for the product does not meet the Min Production Quantity. | [NaN] | [] |  |

| ProductGroupName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Groups |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The name of the product group for which to specify intra-product-group production requirements. | [NaN] | [] | GroupName |
| ProductName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Products |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The products comprising the product group. | [NaN] | [] | ProductName |
| ProductOrder | int |  |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The sequence of products within a product group which occupies a spoke on a production wheel. If no sequence is provided, a product sequence within the group's wheel spoke will not be enforced; production will occur according to the sequence in which production orders were generated. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| SupplementaryProductionTarget | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | For group-based production wheel structures that may result in supplementary production (Duration/Fill or Produce Min wheel types), this field dictates how supplementary production is allocated across products within each product group that occupies a spoke on the wheel. If no targets are provided, supplementary production will be uniformly allocated across products within groups. Targets provided for a product group will be scaled to sum to 1. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether a record is passed into the engine. If Status is set to Exclude, this record will be ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |

| UnitPriceUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | UnitPrice |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines the basis for Unit Price. For example, we could specify that the Unit Price applies to one case or one pound of the Product. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| UnitVolume | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, HOPPER, CYCLO] |  |  | 1 |  | The volume of the Product. | [Volume] | [THROG, DENDRO, NEO, HOPPER] |  |

| UnitWeight | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, HOPPER, CYCLO] |  |  | 1 |  | The weight of the Product. | [Weight] | [THROG, DENDRO, NEO, HOPPER] |  |
| UnitWeightUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Weight] |  | UnitWeight |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, HOPPER, CYCLO] | [Weight] |  | {PrimaryWeightUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Weight) used to define Unit Weight. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| UnitLength | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The length of the Product. | [Distance] | [] |  |
| UnitWidth | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The width of the Product. | [Distance] | [] |  |
| UnitHeight | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The height of the Product. | [Distance] | [] |  |
| DimensionUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Distance] |  | UnitLength, UnitWidth, UnitHeight |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [] | [Distance] |  | {PrimaryDistanceUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Length) used to define the Product's dimensions. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| ShelfLife | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG] |  |  |  |  | The Shelf Life will determine the length of time that a product will be valid before it expires. When being used in Optimization, Shelf Life will be evaluated against the period lengths to determine which period a product will expire in. For example, if the Shelf Life is 45 Days and a model has monthly periods, the product will expire in Period 2. | [Time] | [] |  |
| ShelfLifeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | ShelfLife |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO, THROG] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines the length of the Product's Shelf Life | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| MaturationTime | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The maturation time will determine the minimum length of time that a product should have before it reaches to the customer. When being used in Optimization, Maturation time will be evaluated against the period lengths to determine which period a product will be ready for the customer. For example, if the Maturation Time is 40 Days and a model has monthly periods, the product will be ready to use in 1 Period. | [Time] | [] |  |
| MaturationTimeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | MaturationTime |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines the length of the Product's Maturation Time | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| DisposalCost | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | 0 |  | The cost of disposal of the Product. | [Cost] | [THROG] |  |
| DisposalCostUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | DisposalCost |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines the basis for disposal cost. For example, we could set the Product's Disposal Cost as $5 per pound or $5 each. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| DisposalBehavior | [OnExpiry, EndOfHorizon] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | OnExpiry |  | The Disposal Behavior determines how the product is handled when it is expired. If set to Immediate, the product is removed from inventory and incurs the disposal cost. If set to KeepInInventory, the product remains in inventory without any consumption and a disposal cost applied at the end of horizon. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ProductBehavior | [Discrete, Continuous] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  | Discrete |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, TRIAD, HOPPER, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [THROG, DENDRO, NEO, HOPPER] |  |
