### シート: ReplenishmentOrderProfiles

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| OrderID | String |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The unique identifier for each Order profile, as orders are generated based on their recurring properties they will be indexed off of the entered order number. All Orders will be placed as single line item orders, however multiple recurring orders can be set at the same Facility | [NaN] | [] |  |
| FacilityName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  |  | Facilities |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The name of the Facility placing the Order | [NaN] | [] | FacilityName |
| ProductName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  |  | Products |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The Product that the Order will be placed for | [NaN] | [] | ProductName |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the Order profile exists in the model. If Status is set to Exclude, this  record will be ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Quantity | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The amount of Product being ordered by the Facility. Both doubles, as well as distributions are accepted as entries. | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| QuantityUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  | Quantity |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines Quantity. Quantity, Volume, and Weight units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| NumberOfOrders | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | 1 |  | Based on the information entered in the Start Date, Time Between Orders and End Date fields, the Order Profile will generate orders based at different times throughout the simulation. When an order is generated, the Number Of Orders will be evaluated to determine how many orders are going to be placed at that specific time. Once the Number Of Orders is determined, all of the other information specified in the Product Name, Quantity, Number of Lines will be used to generate the details for each of the orders at that time. | [Integer] | [] |  |
| NumberOfLines | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | 1 |  | If the Product Name is left blank for the Order Profile, recurring orders can be generated with a varying number of line items in each order. An integer value, or a distribution can be entered in this field. In the event that the number of lines in an order is 2 or greater, the Order Profile Product Selection and Order Profile Product Affinity tables will be used to determine which products are to be ordered. | [Integer] | [] |  |
| ServiceLevel | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | Service Level will be used to calculate the Due Date for all of the generated recurring orders. Due Date will be set to the Order Date + Service Level. | [Time] | [] |  |
| ServiceLevelUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  | ServiceLevel |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) used to define Service Level. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| StartDate | Date/Time |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The Start Date will defined the date that the first order will drop. Orders will drop exactly at the date/time specified in Start Date, and then each following order's drop date will be determined by the Time Between Orders information. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| TimeBetweenOrders | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | Time Between Orders will specify how much time goes by from the previous order until the next order drops. Both doubles, as well as distribution entries are supported. | [Time] | [] |  |
| TimeBetweenOrdersUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  | TimeBetweenOrders |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) used to define Time Between Orders. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| EndDate | Date/Time |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The End Date will specify the last possible time that an order can drop. If the Time Between Orders causes the next order drop date to fall after the specified End Date, that last order will not be generated. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |

### シート: ReplenishmentOrders

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| OrderID | String - Fixed |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The unique identifier for each Order; an Order consists of one or more Line Items. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| LineItemID | String - Fixed |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The unique identifier for the single Line Item being defined; Orders with multiple Line Items will require multiple table records. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| FacilityName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  |  | Facilities |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The name of the Facility placing the Order. Facility Groups are supported; the Group behavior will be Enumerate. | [NaN] | [] | FacilityName |
| ProductName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  |  | Products |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The Product in the Line Item. | [NaN] | [] | ProductName |
| Quantity | double | NonNegative | Yes |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The amount of Product requested by the Facility. | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| QuantityUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | Quantity |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines Quantity. Quantity, Volume, and Weight units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| OrderDate | Date/Time |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The date and time at which the Facility places the Order. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| DueDate | Date/Time |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The date and time at which the Order is due at the Facility. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the Line Item exists in the model. If Status is set to Exclude, this  record will be ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ServiceMode | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | TransportationModes |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | Optionally, a required Transportation Mode can be specified here. If multiple Modes are acceptable, a Mode Group may be used; the Group behavior will be Aggregate. If NULL, any Mode is assumed to be acceptable. | [NaN] | [] | ModeName |
| SourceName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | Facilities |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | Optionally, a required Source for the Line Item can be specified here. If multiple Sources are acceptable, a Source (Facility or Supplier) Group may be used; the Group behavior will be Aggregate. If NULL, any Source is assumed to be acceptable. | [NaN] | [] | FacilityName |
| SingleSourceOrder | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | If True, all Line Items in the Order must be sourced from a single Facility. If False, each Line Item within the Order may be sourced from a different Facility. A value of True in this field overrides Single Source Line Items = False, enforcing that all Line Items in the order come from a single source. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| SingleSourceLineItems | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | If True, the entire Line Item must be sourced from a single site (Facility or Supplier). If False, the Line Item may be sourced from different sites. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| AllowPartialFillOrders | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | If True, the Order may be partially filled. Otherwise, it must be filled in full. If backordering is allowed, the remaining quantity may be satisfied in the future with additional shipments; if the Backorder Time Limit is reached on a partially filled order, the remaining quantity will be cancelled. If Partial Fill Orders is False, Partial Fill Line Items will also be forced to False. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| AllowPartialFillLineItems | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | If True, the specified Line Item may be partially filled. Otherwise, the Line Item must be filled in full. If backordering is allowed, the remaining quantity of partially filled Line Items may be satisfied in the future with additional shipments; if the Backorder Time Limit is reached on a partially filled Line Item, the remaining quantity will be cancelled. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| AllowBackorders | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | If True, the Facility will accept a backorder in the event that the Order cannot be fulfilled before its Due Date. If False and if the Order is not fulfilled by its Due Date, it will be cancelled on that date. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| BackorderTimeLimit | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | If Allow Backorders = True, this value is the amount of time after which the backorder will be cancelled by the Facility. A value of NULL will be interpreted as an infinite time limit, while a value of zero is equivalent to setting Allow Backorders = False. | [Time] | [] |  |
| BackorderTimeLimitUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | BackorderTimeLimit |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) used to define Backorder Time Limit. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |

### シート: ReplenishmentPolicies

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| FacilityName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Facilities | Yes | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The Facility that the policy record applies to. If Procurement Policies are also defined for the Facility, Replenishment Policies will take priority. | [NaN] | [THROG] | FacilityName |
| ProductName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Products | Yes | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The Product that the policy record applies to. | [NaN] | [THROG] | ProductName |
| SourceName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Facilities | Yes | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The Facility from which the Product will be sourced under the policy record. | [NaN] | [THROG] | FacilityName |
| OptimizationPolicy | [To Optimize, By Ratio (Auto Scale), By Ratio (No Scale), Single Source] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO, CYCLO] |  |  | To Optimize |  | The logic that replenishment flows for the Facility, Product, and Source combination(s) must follow. To Optimize selects the lowest cost option. By Ratio (Auto Scale) will enforce a flow split between the specified sources, the defined ratios will apply to flow into the Facility from the listed sources only. By Ratio (No Scale) will enforce a flow split between the specified sources and the defined ratios will be in terms of the total inbound flow into the Facility. Single Source policies force the Facility to receive the Product from one Source. Note that if a group is used in the Facility Name, Product Name, or Source Name field that the groups will be enumerated and the policy logic will be applied over the enumerated entries. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| OptimizationPolicyValue | double | PositiveNumeric |  | OptimizationPolicy = By Ratio (Auto Scale) OR By Ratio (No Scale) |  |  |  | [NEO, CYCLO] |  |  |  | 0 | By Ratio (Auto Scale): The Policy Value will define the percentage of flow that must be satisfied from the listed source. The percentage will be calculated based on the total inbound flow for the Facility/Product combination from sources with a policy of By Ratio (Auto Scale).  By Ratio (No Scale): The Policy Value will define the percentage of flow that must be satisfied from the listed source. The percentage will be calculated based on the total inbound flow for the Facility/Product combination across ALL sources. | [Percentage] | [] |  |
| SimulationPolicy | [By Preference, Single Source, Allocation, By Distance] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | By Preference |  | The logic that replenishment flows for the Facility, Product, and Source combination(s) must follow. By Preference will pick the sources pending product availability in the specified order of preference. Single Source policies force the Facility to receive the Product from one Source. Allocation will source as much as possible from the sources based on availability in the specified order of preference - this is similar to By Preference but the Allocation policy will not attempt to keep orders together from a single source. By Distance will select the closest source using straight-line distances. | [NaN] | [THROG] |  |
| SimulationPolicyValue | double | PositiveNumeric |  | SimulationPolicy = By Preference |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | By Preference: Policy Value defines the order of preference in which a source will be selected pending product availability, with the lower numbers representing a higher selection preference. Allocation: Policy value defines the order in which sources will be selected. | [Percentage] | [THROG] |  |
| SimulationPolicyLogic | [Push, Pull] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | Pull |  | Simulation Policy Logic will specify whether the sourcing behavior specified for the Replenishment Policy is a Pull action (Facility selecting a Source) or a Push action (Source selecting a Facility to send surplus inventory). Surplus Level definitions, and a Surplus Review Period will be set in the Inventory Policies table. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the policy exists in the model. If Status is set to Exclude, the policy record is ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [THROG] |  |
| UnitCost | Cost Time Expression | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE StepCosts.StepCostBehavior = [All Item, Incremental, Stepwise] |  |  |  | StepCosts |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | 0 | Optimization: Currently supports doubles and named step costs that are defined in the StepCosts table where StepCostBehavior = All Item, Incremental or Stepwise or a combination of Stepwise and All Item  Simulation: Currently supports doubles | The unit cost associated with flow for the specified Source/Facility/Product combination. This field can support a single numeric value, a step cost (either a direct entry or a lookup), or a custom cost function. | [Cost] | [] | StepCostName |
| UnitCostUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | UnitCost |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure used to define flow for the specified Source/Facility/Product combination. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| MinimumOrderQuantity | double | PositiveNumeric |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The minimum quantity that must move between source and destination in a period for a flow to occur | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| MinimumOrderQuantityUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | MinimumOrderQuantity |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines the MOQ for the Source/Facility/Product combination. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| LotSize | double | PositiveNumeric |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | Orders placed from the Facility to the specified sources must be filled in multiples of the specified lot size. | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| LotSizeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | LotSize |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines the Lot Size for the Source/Facility/Product combination. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| MaxSourcingRange | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The maximum allowable distance between Customer and Source for the Product(s) specified. This field is intended for use in models with group policies to eliminate excessively long lanes from consideration. If all sources exceed the max sourcing range for a Facility-Product pairing, the closest source will be kept by default - this behavior can be overwritten through the Keep Closest Lane For Max Sourcing Distance run option. | [Distance] | [] |  |
| MaxSourcingRangeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Distance] |  | MaxSourcingRange |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Distance] |  | {PrimaryDistanceUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Distance) that defines Max Delivery Range. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| OnlySourceFromSurplus | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | 0 |  | If True, when selecting a source for the Facility / Product combination only sources that have available surplus can be selected to fulfill the order. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [THROG] |  |

### シート: ReturnPolicies

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| SiteName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Customers | Yes | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The Customer that the policy record applies to. | [NaN] | [] | CustomerName |
| ReturnProductName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Products | Yes | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The Product (or Product group) that the policy record applies to. | [NaN] | [] | ProductName |
| DestinationName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Facilities | Yes | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The destination Facility (or Facility group) that the policy record applies to. | [NaN] | [] | FacilityName |
| OptimizationPolicy | [To Optimize, Return to Sender, By Ratio (Auto Scale), By Ratio (No Scale), Single Destination] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | To Optimize |  | The logic that returns for the site, Product, and Destination combination(s) must follow. To Optimize selects the lowest cost option. By Ratio (Auto Scale) will enforce a flow split between the specified destinations, the defined ratios will apply to flow from the site into the listed destinations only. By Ratio (No Scale) will enforce a flow split between the specified destinations and the defined ratios will be in terms of the total outbound flow from the site. Single Destination policies force the site to return the Product to one Destination. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| OptimizationPolicyValue | double | PositiveNumeric |  | OptimizationPolicy = By Ratio (Auto Scale) OR By Ratio (No Scale) |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | By Ratio (Auto Scale): The Policy Parameter will define the percentage of flow that must be satisfied from the listed destination. The percentage will be calculated based on the total outbound flow for the site/product combination and enforced proportionally across the destinations with a policy of By Ratio (Auto Scale).  By Ratio (No Scale): The Policy Parameter will define the percentage of flow that must be satisfied from the listed destination. The percentage will be calculated based on the total outbound flow for the site/product combination  and enforced over the listed destinations. If the Policy Parameters add up to less than 100, the remainder of unaccounted flow can be delivered through any other viable lane. If the Policy Parameters add up to more than 100, they will be scaled to 100 and enforced the same as Auto Scale. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the policy exists in the model. If Status is set to Exclude, the policy record is ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| UnitCost | Cost Time Expression | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE ThroughputLevelUOM = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  |  |  | StepCosts |  | [NEO] |  |  | 0 |  | The cost incurred per unit of flow for the specified Source/Customer/Product combination. | [NaN] | [] | StepCostName |
| UnitCostUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  | UnitCost |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines flow for the Source/Customer/Product combination. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| MaxDeliveryRange | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The maximum allowable distance between Destination and Customer for the Product(s) specified. This field is intended for use in models with group policies to eliminate excessively long lanes from consideration. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| MaxDeliveryRangeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  | MaxDeliveryRange |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Distance] |  | {PrimaryDistanceUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Distance) that defines Max Delivery Range. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |

| BandName | Name |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [DART, NEO, TRIAD, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The name of the Risk Band. Risk Bands are referenced by name in all of the Risk Configuration tables. Bands are defined based on a series of levels and associated Risk Scores and when the DART engine is run the corresponding scores will be assigned to all places where the Risk Band is called in the configuration tables. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| BandLowerValue | double |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [DART, NEO, TRIAD, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The lower bound of the step that the Risk Score will be applied over. | [Risk] | [] |  |
| RiskScore | double | RiskScore | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [DART, NEO, TRIAD, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The Risk Score that will be selected based on the corresponding band's lower value. | [Risk] | [] |  |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [DART, NEO, TRIAD, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |

Risk Ratings that are included will be run, and a default Opti Risk rating will always be run as well. | [NaN] | [] |  |

| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [DART, NEO, TRIAD, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the Risk Rating will be run in the model. If Status is set to Exclude, the Risk Rating is ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [] |  |

### シート: SequentialObjectives

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| ObjectiveType | [TotalSupplyChainCost, TotalRevenue, TotalProfit, TotalTransportationCost, TotalShipmentCost, TotalInTransitHoldingCost, TotalDutyCost, TotalTariffCost, TotalProductionCost, TotalPrebuildHoldingCost, TotalTurnEstimatedHoldingCost, TotalStorageCost, TotalSourcingCost, TotalFixedOperatingCost, TotalFixedStartupCost, TotalFixedClosingCost, TotalInboundHandlingCost, TotalOutboundHandlingCost, TotalProcessCost, TotalCO2Cost, TotalCO2Emissions, TotalFuelSurchargeCost, TotalMultiStopRouteCost, TotalUnservedDemandPenaltyCost, TotalReturnCost, TotalUserDefinedCost, UserDefinedCost, UserDefinedVariable, UserDefinedConstraint, TotalWeightedFlowDistance, TotalWeightedFlowTime, GeographicRisk, ConcentrationRisk, SourceCountRisk, TransportTimeRisk, ScenarioArcsDeviation-CustomerFulfillment, ScenarioArcsDeviation-Procurement, ScenarioArcsDeviation, FlowConstraintsDeviation, ProductionConstraintsDeviation, InventoryConstraintsDeviation, FacilityThroughputCapacityDeviation, WorkCenterThroughputCapacityDeviation, TotalFacilityThroughputQuantity, TotalFacilityThroughputVolume, TotalFacilityThroughputWeight, TotalWorkCenterThroughputQuantity, TotalWorkCenterThroughputVolume, TotalWorkCenterThroughputWeight, TotalWorkCenterThroughputTime, TotalInfeasibilityDeviation] |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The objective that optimization will solve for. Objectives will correspond to the cost and revenue columns from the Optimization Network Summary table, but specific User Defined Variables and User Defined Costs will also be supported. If the Objective Type = UserDefinedVariable or UserDefinedCost, the specific variable or cost can be specified in the UserDefinedObjectiveName column. Sequential Solves will solve for the objectives marked as Priority = 1 first and use this solution as a base for any subsequent solves for lower Priority values. The solution from Priority 1 can then be relaxed within the specified tolerance for each listed Priority 1 objective to find a solution targeting the Priority 2 objectives. This process will be repeated until the lowest priority objective has been solved for. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| UserDefinedObjectiveName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  | ObjectiveType = UserDefinedCost OR UserDefinedVariable OR UserDefinedConstraint OR ScenarioArcsDeviation OR ScenarioArcsDeviation-CustomerFulfillment OR ScenarioArcsDeviation-Procurement | Yes | UserDefinedCosts, UserDefinedVariables, UserDefinedConstraints, Scenarios |  | [NEO] |  |  | ALL |  | If the Objective Type is set as User Defined Variable, User Defined Cost or User Defined Constraint, the specific User Defined Cost, Variable or Constraint can be referenced here. If nothing is specified, All Costs/Variables/Constraints will be considered. If the Objective Type is set as any of the Scenario Arc deviation options, enter the Scenario Name from which flow deviations are being measured. | [NaN] | [] | [UserDefinedCosts.CostName, UserDefinedVariables.VariableName, UserDefinedConstraints.ConstraintName, Scenarios.ScenarioName] |
| Priority | int | NonNegative | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | 1 |  | The numeric value indicating the Objective priority. | [Integer] | [] |  |
| Tolerance | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | 0.1 |  | The Tolerance value of each objective will specify how much that Objective can be relaxed to solve for the Objective(s) in the next Priority. A value of 10 would mean that the solution could be relaxed by 10 percent. | [Percentage] | [] |  |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Include |  | Status will dictate whether or not the Objective will be considered for the Sequential Solve. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |

| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| ShipmentID | String - Fixed |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [HOPPER, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | A unique identifier for the Shipment; a Shipment consists of one or more Line Items. | [NaN] | [HOPPER] |  |
| LineItemID | String - Fixed |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | A unique identifier for the Line Item being defined; Shipments with multiple Line Items will require multiple table records. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| SourceName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  |  | Facilities, Suppliers, Customers |  | [HOPPER, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The Facility or Supplier that the shipment originates from. | [NaN] | [HOPPER] | [Facilities.FacilityName, Suppliers.SupplierName, Customers.CustomerName] |
| DestinationName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  |  | Customers, Facilities |  | [HOPPER, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The Facility or Customer that the shipment will be delivered to. | [NaN] | [HOPPER] | [Customers.CustomerName, Facilities.FacilityName] |
| ProductName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  |  | Products |  | [HOPPER, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The Product in the Line Item. | [NaN] | [HOPPER] | ProductName |
| Quantity | double | NonNegative | Yes |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | 0 |  | The amount of the Product being shipped. | [Quantity] | [HOPPER] |  |
| QuantityUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | Quantity |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [HOPPER, THROG, DENDRO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines Quantity. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| Volume | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | The volume of the Product being shipped. If empty, the solver will use the unitVolume of the Product if it is defined, or 0 otherwise. | [Weight] | [HOPPER] |  |
| VolumeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Volume] |  | Volume |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [HOPPER] | [Volume] |  | {PrimaryVolumeUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines Volume. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| Weight | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | The weight of the Product being shipped. If empty, the solver will use the unitVolume of the Product if it is defined, or 0 otherwise. | [Weight] | [HOPPER] |  |
| WeightUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Weight] |  | Weight |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [HOPPER] | [Weight] |  | {PrimaryWeightUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines Weight. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| OrderDate | Date/Time |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The date and time at which the Shipment is requested. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| DueDate | Date/Time |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The date and time at which the Shipment must be delivered. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| EarliestPickupDate | Date/Time |  |  |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | The date and time of the earliest possible shipment pickup. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| LatestPickupDate | Date/Time |  |  |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | The date and time of the latest possible shipment pickup. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| EarliestDeliveryDate | Date/Time |  |  |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | The date and time of the earliest possible shipment delivery. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| LatestDeliveryDate | Date/Time |  |  |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | The date and time of the latest possible shipment delivery. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the Line Item exists in the model. If Status is set to Exclude, this record will be ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [HOPPER] |  |
| ServiceMode | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | TransportationModes |  | [] |  |  |  |  | Optionally, a required Transportation Mode can be specified here. If multiple Modes are acceptable, a Mode Group may be used; the Group behavior will be Aggregate. If NULL, any Mode is assumed to be acceptable. | [NaN] | [] | ModeName |
| AllowPartialFillShipment | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  | True |  | If True, the specified Line Item may be partially filled. Otherwise, the Line Item must be filled in full. The remaining quantity of partially filled Line Items may be satisfied in the future with additional shipments. If Partial Fill Shipment is False, Partial Fill Line Items will also be forced to False. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| AllowPartialFillLineItems | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  | True |  | If True, individual Line Items can be partially filled. If Allow Partial Fill Shipment = False and Allow Partial Fill Line Item = True, the Line Item Partial Fill behavior will override the Shipment Partial Fill behavior. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| FixedPickupTime | Cost Time Expression | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | The fixed time required at the pickup location of the shipment. This will be additive to the FixedPickupTime specified in the Facilities, Customers and Transportation Assets tables. | [Time] | [] |  |
| FixedPickupTimeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | FixedPickupTime |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [HOPPER] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines Fixed Pickup Time. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| FixedDeliveryTime | Cost Time Expression | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | The fixed time required at the delivery location of the shipment. This will be additive to the FixedDeliveryTime specified in the Facilities, Customers and Transportation Assets tables. | [Time] | [] |  |
| FixedDeliveryTimeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | FixedDeliveryTime |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [HOPPER] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines Fixed Delivery Time. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| UnitPickupTime | Cost Time Expression | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | The time required per unit to be loaded onto an Asset at the pickup location. This will be additive to any UnitPickupTime that is specified in the Facilities, Customers and Transportation Assets tables. | [Time] | [] |  |
| UnitUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | UnitPickupTime OR UnitDeliveryTime |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [HOPPER] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines the basis on which UnitPickupTime and UnitDeliveryTime will be applied for the Shipment. Quantity, Volume, and Weight units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| UnitPickupTimeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | UnitPickupTime |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [HOPPER] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines Unit Pickup Time. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| UnitDeliveryTime | Cost Time Expression | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | The time required per unit to be delivered at the destination location. This will be additive to any UnitDeliveryTime that is specified in the Transportation Assets table. | [Time] | [] |  |
| UnitDeliveryTimeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | UnitDeliveryTime |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [HOPPER] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines Unit Delivery Time. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| DirectCost | Cost Time Expression | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE StepCosts.StepCostBehavior = [All Item, Incremental, Stepwise] |  |  |  | StepCosts |  | [HOPPER] |  |  |  | Currently supports doubles and named step costs that are defined in the StepCosts table where StepCostBehavior = All Item, Incremental or Stepwise or a combination of Stepwise and All Item | The cost to send the shipment directly to the destination. | [Cost] | [] |  |
| TemplateRouteName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | TemplateRoutes |  | [HOPPER] |  |  | None |  | If the Shipment is assigned to a Template Route, the Template Route Name is specified here. | [NaN] | [] | TemplateRouteName |
| TemplateRouteStopSequence | int | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | The Stop sequence for the delivery stop of the shipment on the defined Template Route. | [Integer] | [] |  |
| TemplateRoutePickupStopSequence | int | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | The Stop sequence for the pickup stop of the shipment on the defined Template Route. | [Integer] | [] |  |
| TemplateTerritoryName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | TemplateTerritories |  | [HOPPER] |  |  | None |  | If the Shipment is assigned to a Template Territory, the Template Territory Name is specified here. | [NaN] | [] | TemplateTerritoryName |
| DecompositionName | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | Shipments with the same Decomposition Name will be run together as a subproblem in the Hopper solve. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| AllowedAssetName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | TransportationAssets |  | [HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | The Transportation Asset(s) that can be used to route this shipment. Blank means that all assets are allowed. Single asset name, groups and named filters can be used. | [NaN] | [] | AssetName |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [HOPPER] |  |

### シート: StepCosts

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| StepCostName | Name |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO, HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | The name given to the Step Cost. This name may be called in other input tables where Step Cost lookups can be used. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| StepCostBehavior | [Stepwise, Incremental, All Item] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO, HOPPER] |  |  | All Item |  | The behavior of the Step Cost. Most fields support All Item, Incremental and Stepwise as well as the combination of Stepwise and All Item. The only exceptions are: Fixed Startup, Fixed Operating, and Fixed Closing Costs fields will only support a Step Cost Behavior of Stepwise; the corresponding Cost value will be returned as a one-time cost. Incremental behavior will apply Costs to throughput levels individually. All Item behavior will apply the relevant Cost to the total throughput. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ThroughputLevel | double | NonNegative | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO, HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | The minimum throughput level at which the relevant Cost is to be applied. All step cost definitions should start with a throughput level of zero; otherwise, the lowest throughput interval will be enforced as a minimum requirement. | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| ThroughputLevelUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight, Time, Distance] |  | ThroughputLevel |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO, HOPPER] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight, Time, Distance] |  | EA |  | The unit of measure that defines Throughput Level. Quantity, Volume, Weight, Time and Distance units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| Cost | Cost Time Expression |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO, HOPPER] |  |  |  | Optimization: Currently supports doubles | The cost value to incur for the specified Throughput Level. | [Cost] | [] |  |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO, HOPPER] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the table record exists. If Status is set to Exclude, this record will not be considered when constructing Step Costs. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, HOPPER] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |

| GeographicRiskWeight | double | NonNegative | Yes |  |  |  |  | [DART, NEO, TRIAD, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The weight applied to the Geographic Risk when calculating the Supplier Risk Score. Detailed Geographic Risks configurations on geographic risk components are entered in the Geographic Risk Configurations table. | [Risk] | [] |  |
| UserDefinedRiskWeight | double | NonNegative | Yes |  |  |  |  | [DART, NEO, TRIAD, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The weight applied to the User Defined Risk when calculating the Supplier Risk Score. | [Risk] | [] |  |
| UtilizationRiskWeight | double | NonNegative | Yes |  |  |  |  | [DART, NEO, TRIAD, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The weight applied to the Utilization Risk when calculating the Supplier Risk Score. Unlike Facilities, Suppliers will only be impacted by a single capacity factor and therefore there is no need for more detailed Utilization Risk configurations. | [Risk] | [] |  |
| ConcentrationRiskWeight | double | NonNegative | Yes |  |  |  |  | [DART, NEO, TRIAD, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The weight applied to the Concentration Risk when calculating the Supplier Risk Score. | [Risk] | [] |  |
| ConcentrationRiskBand | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  |  | RiskBandDefinitions |  | [DART, NEO, TRIAD, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The Risk Band that is used to evaluate the Concentration Risk at the Supplier. Risk Bands are defined in the Risk Band Definitions table. | [NaN] | [] | BandName |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [DART, NEO, TRIAD, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |

If left empty for some records, the User Defined Risk will not be considered for those specific Suppliers. | PathDestinationProperty | [Name, Address, City, PostalCode, Region, Country] |  |  |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Country |  | The property of the Destination (Customer or Facility) that the Rate cost structure has been created for. For example, a Postal Code to Postal Code rate system could be defined; both the Source and Destination Properties would be set to Postal Code and the Source/Destination values would include all allowable Postal Code pairings. | [NaN] | [] |  |  |
| PathDestinationValue | String |  |  |  | Yes | Customers, Facilities, Destinations |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The value to look up in the Destination table (either Customers or Facilities) Destination Property column. | [NaN] | [] | Facilities.FacilityName, Facilities.Address, Facilities.City, Facilities.PostalCode, Facilities.Region, Facilities.Country, Customers.CustomerName, Customers.Address, Customers.City, Customers.PostalCode, Customers.Region, Customers.Country |  |

| HSCode | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |  |
| DutyRate | double |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | 0 |  | The Duty Rate will be applied as a percentage of the product's value that is moving along the path starts at the Origin and ends in the Destination. For a Duty Rate of 10%, enter a value of 10 into the Duty Rate field. | [Percentage] | [] |  |  |
| UnitCost | Cost Time Expression |  |  |  |  | StepCosts |  | [NEO] |  |  | 0 |  | The duty cost per product unit | [Cost] | [] |  |  |
| UnitCostUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  |  |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines Cost. UOM Types of Quantity, Volume, Weight are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol | Per-Intra |
| ProductUnitValueOverride | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The unit value of the product that should be used for the tariff calculation. If blank, the unit value from the Products table will be used. | [Cost] | [] |  |  |
| ProductUnitValueOverrideUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | UnitValue |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines the basis for Unit Value. For example, we could set the Product's Unit Value as $5 per pound or $5 each. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |  |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the TariffRate exists in the model. If Status is set to Exclude, the Rate will be ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [] |  |  |

Different distances and times can be specified for individual Transportation Assets between the same Origin / Destination pairing. If nothing is entered, the Distance / Time will be applied over all Transportation Assets. | [NaN] | [] | AssetName |

| TransportTime | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER, NEO, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The travel time of the transportation arc for the given Origin / Destination / Asset combination. | [Time] | [] |  |
| TransportTimeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | TransportTime |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [HOPPER, NEO, CYCLO] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines Transport Time. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |

| IsSymmetric | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER, NEO, CYCLO] |  |  | True |  | If True, the same distance and time will be applied for movements between the Origin and Destination in both directions. | [NaN] | [] |  |

### シート: TransportationAssetConstraints

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | UOMConversion |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| AssetName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  | Yes | TransportationAssets | Yes | [HOPPER] |  |  | ALL |  | The relevant Transportation Asset. Groups are supported. |  |
| AssetNameGroupBehavior | [Enumerate, Aggregate] |  |  |  | Yes |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  | Aggregate |  | If AssetName is a Group, use this field to specify the Group's behavior. If Enumerate is selected, an instance of this Variable will be created for each member of the Group. If Aggregate is selected, the activity in the variable will be summed over the members of the Group. If you are using multiple terms for a variable, a group behavior entry of enumerate is only supported for one term. Having multiple terms with enumerations is not currently supported. |  |
| SiteName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  | Yes | Facilities, Customers | Yes | [HOPPER] |  |  | ALL |  | The site(s) (Facilities or Customers). Groups are supported. |  |
| SiteType | [Pickup, Delivery, Any] |  |  |  | Yes |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  | Any |  | The activity that is being tracked at the location specified in the SiteName field. This can be one of Pickup, Delivery, or Any. |  |
| SiteNameGroupBehavior | [Enumerate, Aggregate] |  |  |  | Yes |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  | Aggregate |  | If SiteName is a Group, use this field to specify the Group's behavior. If Enumerate is selected, an instance of this Variable will be created for each member of the Group. If Aggregate is selected, the activity in the variable will be summed over the members of the Group. If you are using multiple terms for a variable, a group behavior entry of enumerate is only supported for one term. Having multiple terms with enumerations is not currently supported. |  |
| ShipmentID | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  | Yes | Shipments | Yes | [HOPPER] |  |  | ALL |  | The relevant Shipment(s). Groups are supported. |  |
| ShipmentIDGroupBehavior | [Enumerate, Aggregate] |  |  |  | Yes |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  | Aggregate |  | If ShipmentID is a Group, use this field to specify the Group's behavior. If Enumerate is selected, an instance of this Variable will be created for each member of the Group. If Aggregate is selected, the activity in the variable will be summed over the members of the Group. If you are using multiple terms for a variable, a group behavior entry of enumerate is only supported for one term. Having multiple terms with enumerations is not currently supported. |  |
| ProductName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  | Yes | Products | Yes | [HOPPER] |  |  | ALL |  | The relevant Product(s). Groups are supported. |  |
| ProductNameGroupBehavior | [Enumerate, Aggregate] |  |  |  | Yes |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  | Aggregate |  | If Product Name is a Group, use this field to specify the Group's behavior. If Enumerate is selected, an instance of this Variable will be created for each member of the Group. If Aggregate is selected, the activity in the variable will be summed over the members of the Group. If you are using multiple terms for a variable, a group behavior entry of enumerate is only supported for one term. Having multiple terms with enumerations is not currently supported. |  |
| ConstraintType | [Min, Max, Fixed] |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | The type of constraint imposed on the flow for the specified Source/Destination/Product/Mode/Period combination.   Min: the flow must be greater than or equal to the Constraint Value. Max: the flow must be less than or equal to the Constraint Value. Fixed: the flow must be equal to the Constraint Value. Fixed With Tolerance: The flow must be equal to the Constraint Value * (1 +/- Relative Constraint Tolerance). Relative Constraint Tolerance is specified in the Model Run Options. Conditional Min: if the flow is nonzero, it must be greater than or equal to the Constraint Value. |  |
| ConstraintValue | double | NonNegative | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | The numeric value that the specified flow will be evaluated against. |  |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the constraint exists in the model. If Status is set to Exclude, this constraint (or constraints in the case of Enumerated Groups) will be ignored during the model run. |  |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  |  |  |  |  |

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn | UOMConversion |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| AssetName | Name |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [HOPPER, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The name of the Transportation Asset. This value can be referenced in other tables involving Transportation Assets. | [NaN] | [HOPPER] |  |  |
| AvailableUnits | int | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The units of this Asset that are available at the specified Base Location. If left blank, this is assumed to be an infinite amount of available assets | [Integer] | [HOPPER] |  |  |
| DomicileLocation | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Facilities | Yes | [HOPPER, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | ALL |  | The Facility at which the Asset is based. Units of this Asset will be located at the Base Location at the beginning of the model run. | [NaN] | [HOPPER] | FacilityName |  |
| MaxDistanceToFirstPickup | Cost Time Expression | NonNegative | Yes |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  | 0 |  | The max distance a vehicle can travel empty to its first pickup location. This useful to allow vehicles to start from a depot separate from a distribution center | [Distance] | [] |  |  |
| MaxDistanceToFirstPickupUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Distance] |  | MaxDistanceToFirstPickup |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [HOPPER] | [Distance] |  | {PrimaryDistanceUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Distance) that defines the max distance to first pickup. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |  |
| OperatingSchedule | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | BusinessHours |  | [HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | The schedule (defined in the Business Hours table) dictating when the Transportation Asset will be available. The vehicle must start and finish its route within one continuous business hours window. If no schedule is specified, the asset can be used all day. | [NaN] | [] | ScheduleName |  |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the Asset exists in the model. If Status is set to Exclude, the Asset is ignored in all tables. The Asset will be dropped from policies that reference it. | [NaN] | [HOPPER] |  |  |
| TransportationRateName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | TransportationRates |  | [HOPPER, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The name of the Transportation Rate. This value can be referenced in other tables involving Transportation Rates. | [NaN] | [HOPPER] | TransportationRateName |  |
| TransportationRateType | [LocationIndependent, LastDestination, FurthestDestination] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | LocationIndependent |  | The type of the rate defined in the TransportationRates table. For LastDestination and FurthestDestination, Hopper will look up the correct rate using the RouteDestinationName column in the TransportationRates table. LastDestination will use the site with the last delivery in the route while FurthestDestination will use the furthest customer from the DC in the route. | [NaN] | [] |  |  |
| TransportationDriverRateName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  | Yes | TransportationDriverRates |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The name of the Transportation Driver Rate. | [NaN] | [] | TransportationDriverRateName |  |
| TransportationStopRateName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  | Yes | TransportationStopRates |  | [HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | The name of the Transportation Stop Rate. | [NaN] | [] | TransportationStopRateName |  |
| FixedCost | Cost Time Expression | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE StepCosts.StepCostBehavior = [All Item, Incremental] |  |  |  | StepCosts |  | [HOPPER] |  |  | 0 |  | The one-time fixed cost incurred per unit of the Asset used in the model run. | [Cost] | [HOPPER] | StepCostName |  |
| QuantityCapacity | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The capacity (quantity) of the Asset. The most restrictive capacity given in this record (among quantity, volume, and weight) will be applied to the Asset. | [Quantity] | [HOPPER] |  |  |
| QuantityCapacityUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity] |  | QuantityCapacity |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [HOPPER, THROG, DENDRO] | [Quantity] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Quantity) that defines Quantity Capacity. | [NaN] | [HOPPER] | Symbol |  |
| QuantityFillLevel | double | PositiveNumeric |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The fill level of the Asset (quantity). Quantity Fill Level is the minimum quantity of items required for the Asset to depart. | [Quantity] | [] |  |  |

| IsLastInFirstOut | [True, False] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  | False |  | If True, shipments will always be delivered in reverse order of their pickup as if they were loaded and unloaded from the back of the vehicle. This is particularly useful for Interleaved and Backhaul models and won't impact standard outbound models. | [NaN] | [] |  |  |

This means that the end of one route will use the same clocks as the beginning of the next route. Otherwise, each route will have independent clocks. |  | [] |  |  |

| AssetWeightUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Weight] |  | AssetWeight |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [HOPPER] | [Weight] |  | {PrimaryWeightUOM} |  | The unit of measure of Type = Weight that the asset weight is defined in terms of. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |  |

| CO2EmissionRateUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Distance, Quantity-Distance, Volume-Distance, Weight-Distance] |  | CO2EmissionRate |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [HOPPER] | [Distance, Quantity-Distance, Volume-Distance, Weight-Distance] |  | {PrimaryDistanceUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Distance, Quantity-Distance, Volume-Distance, Weight-Distance) that defines the CO2 Emission Rate. If a UOM of Type = Distance is used, the total distance of the route will be used to calculate CO2 emissions. For composite UOMs, we calculate the CO2 emission for each segment, as segment distance times the load on that segment. For quantity and volume, the load is based on carried shipments only. For weight, we also consider the asset weight. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |  |
| DefaultDistanceCost | Cost Time Expression | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE StepCosts.StepCostBehavior = [All Item, Incremental] |  |  |  | StepCosts |  | [HOPPER] |  |  | 0 |  | If provided, this default distance cost will be used whenever a transportation rates cannot be found for a given route. This usually happens when a specific rate combination (eg. last destination) is missing. If not provided, hopper will treat any route without a valid transportation rate as invalid/infeasible. | [Cost] | [] | StepCostName |  |

| PeriodName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes |  | Yes | [] |  |  |  |  | The period name in which a change is being made. Groups of periods are also supported. Changes that are made in the Multi Time Period table will be applied as overrides for the records of the base table for the specified periods only. Additionally, only the fields that contain information needing to be updated are required to be entered, in the absence of data in the Multi Time Period table the data from the standard table will be persisted | [NaN] | [] | PeriodName |
| AssetName | Name |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The name of the Transportation Asset. This value can be referenced in other tables involving Transportation Assets. | [NaN] | [] | AssetName |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the multi-time period record exists in the model. If Status is set to Exclude, the record is ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| AssetStatus | [Include, Exclude] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | Asset Status dictates whether or not the policy exists in the model for the specified period(s). If Asset Status is set to Exclude, the policy record is ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [] |  |

| VolumeFillLevelUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Volume] |  | VolumeFillLevel |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [] | [Volume] |  | {PrimaryVolumeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Volume) that defines Volume Fill Level. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| WeightCapacity | double | PositiveNumeric |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The capacity (weight) of the Asset. The most restrictive capacity given in this record (among quantity, volume, and weight) will be applied to the Asset. | [Weight] | [] |  |
| WeightCapacityUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Weight] |  | WeightCapacity |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [] | [Weight] |  | {PrimaryWeightUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Weight) that defines Weight Capacity. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| WeightFillLevel | double | PositiveNumeric |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The fill level of the Asset (weight). Weight Fill Level is the minimum volume of items required for the Asset to depart. | [Weight] | [] |  |
| WeightFillLevelUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Weight] |  | WeightFillLevel |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [] | [Weight] |  | {PrimaryWeightUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Weight) that defines Weight Fill Level. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| AssetWeight | Cost Time Expression | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [] | [Weight] |  |  |  | The weight of the transportation asset when empty. This weight is added to the weight of products being shipped by this asset and will be used for CO2 emission calculations. | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| AssetWeightUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Weight] |  | AssetWeight |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [] | [Weight] |  | {PrimaryWeightUOM} |  | The unit of measure of Type = Weight that the asset weight is defined in terms of. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| CO2EmissionRate | Cost Time Expression | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [] | [] |  |  |  | The rate of CO2 emissions for this transportation asset for the given period. | [Cost] | [] |  |
| CO2EmissionRateUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Distance, Quantity-Distance, Volume-Distance, Weight-Distance] |  | CO2EmissionRate |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  |  | [Distance, Quantity-Distance, Volume-Distance, Weight-Distance] |  | {PrimaryDistanceUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Distance, Quantity-Distance, Volume-Distance, Weight-Distance) that defines the CO2 Emission Rate. If a UOM of Type = Distance is used, the total distance of the route will be used to calculate CO2 emissions. If weight-distance is used, the product of the total route distance and the total delivered shipment weight will be used. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |

### シート: TransportationBandCosting

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| RateName | Name |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The name of the Transportation Rate. This value can be referenced in other tables involving Transportation Rates. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| OriginProperty | [Name, Address, City, PostalCode, Region, Country, Organization] |  |  |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Name | Optimization: Organization is not currently supported as an Origin Property. | The property of the Source (Supplier or Facility) that the Rate cost structure has been created for. For example, a Postal Code to Postal Code rate system could be defined; both the Source and Destination Properties would be set to Postal Code and the Source/Destination values would include all allowable Postal Code pairings. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| OriginValue | String |  |  |  | Yes | Facilities, Suppliers, Sources | Yes | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The value to look up in the Source table (either Facilities or Suppliers) Source Property column. | [NaN] | [] | Facilities.FacilityName, Facilities.Address, Facilities.City, Facilities.PostalCode, Facilities.Region, Facilities.Country, Facilities.Organization, Suppliers.SupplierName, Suppliers.Address, Suppliers.City, Suppliers.PostalCode, Suppliers.Region, Suppliers.Country |
| DestinationProperty | [Name, Address, City, PostalCode, Region, Country, Organization] |  |  |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Name | Optimization: Organization is not currently supported as a Destination Property. | The property of the Destination (Customer or Facility) that the Rate cost structure has been created for. For example, a Postal Code to Postal Code rate system could be defined; both the Source and Destination Properties would be set to Postal Code and the Source/Destination values would include all allowable Postal Code pairings. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| DestinationValue | String |  |  |  | Yes | Customers, Facilities, Destinations | Yes | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The value to look up in the Destination table (either Customers or Facilities) Destination Property column. | [NaN] | [] | Facilities.FacilityName, Facilities.Address, Facilities.City, Facilities.PostalCode, Facilities.Region, Facilities.Country, Facilities.Organization, Customers.CustomerName, Customers.Address, Customers.City, Customers.PostalCode, Customers.Region, Customers.Country |
| DistanceBand | double | NonNegative | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | 0 |  | Distance-dependent piecewise Rates can be modeled using this field; the appropriate Distance Band interval and corresponding Cost will be selected based on the Distance specified in Transportation Policies. | [Distance] | [] |  |
| DistanceBandUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Distance] |  | DistanceBand |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Distance] |  | {PrimaryDistanceUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Distance) used to define Distance Band. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| ShipmentSize | double | NonNegative |  |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | 0 |  | If populated, the shipment size of the transportation policy record where this rate is assigned will be evaluated against the shipment sizes listed in this table for the other matching keys. This will act as a range lookup, similar to how distance bands are evaluated. | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| ShipmentSizeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | ShipmentSize |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that the shipment size is defined in terms of. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| Cost | Cost Time Expression | NonNegative | Yes |  |  | StepCosts |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The cost that is to be applied when the Rate is referenced in the Transportation Policies table. | [Cost] | [] | StepCostName |
| CostUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight, Time, Distance, Quantity-Distance, Volume-Distance, Weight-Distance, Quantity-Time, Volume-Time, Weight-Time] |  |  |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight, Time, Distance, Quantity-Distance, Volume-Distance, Weight-Distance, Quantity-Time, Volume-Time, Weight-Time] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines Cost. If the Unit Cost UOM field in the Transportation Policies table is populated for records that the listed Transportation Rate is assigned, the value in the Transportation Policies table will be used. If not, the Cost UOM specified in this table will be used along with the cost. UOM Types of Quantity, Volume, Weight, Distance, Time, Quantity-Distance, Quantity-Time, Volume-Distance, Volume-Time, Weight-Distance, and Weight-Time are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the Rate exists in the model. If Status is set to Exclude, the Rate will be ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |

### シート: TransportationDriverRates

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| TransportationDriverRateName | Name |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The name of the Transportation Driver Rate. This value can be referenced in the TransportationAssets table. These costs will be applied across multiple routes assigned to the same driver | [NaN] | [] |  |
| CostResetInterval | [Daily, Weekly, Monthly] |  |  |  | Yes |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | If blank, the costs are calculated across all routes assigned to the driver during the model horizon. This field can be used to apply cost over the specified time bucket instead. This is particularly useful in combination with step costs, eg. to model overtime cost after 40h each week. The costs will reset each day at midnight for Daily, each Monday for Weekly and on the first day of each month for Monthly. | [NaN] | [] | [Customers.CustomerName, Facilities.FacilityName] |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not this rate exists in the model. If Status is set to Exclude, this rate is ignored in all tables. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| DistanceCost | Cost Time Expression | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE StepCosts.StepCostBehavior = [All Item, Incremental] |  |  |  | StepCosts |  | [] |  |  | 0 |  | The cost per distance travelled by this driver. | [Cost] | [] | StepCostName |
| DistanceCostUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Distance] |  | DistanceCost |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [] | [Distance] |  | {PrimaryDistanceUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Distance) that defines how the distance costs will be applied. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| TimeCost | Cost Time Expression | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE StepCosts.StepCostBehavior = [All Item, Incremental] |  |  |  | StepCosts |  | [] |  |  | 0 |  | The cost per unit of time across all routes assigned to this driver. | [Cost] | [] | StepCostName |

| WaitTimeCost | Cost Time Expression | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE StepCosts.StepCostBehavior = [All Item, Incremental] |  |  |  | StepCosts |  | [] |  |  | 0 |  | The cost per unit of time that the driver is waiting. | [Cost] | [] | StepCostName |
| WaitTimeCostUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | WaitTimeCost |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines how the time costs will be applied. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| RestTimeCost | Cost Time Expression | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE StepCosts.StepCostBehavior = [All Item, Incremental] |  |  |  | StepCosts |  | [HOPPER] |  |  | 0 |  | The cost per unit of time during a rest between shifts. | [Cost] | [] | StepCostName |
| RestTimeCostUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | RestTimeCost |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [HOPPER] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines how the time costs will be applied. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| BreakTimeCost | Cost Time Expression | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE StepCosts.StepCostBehavior = [All Item, Incremental] |  |  |  | StepCosts |  | [HOPPER] |  |  | 0 |  | The cost per unit of time during a break after the maximum drive or duty time before short break is reached. | [Cost] | [] | StepCostName |
| BreakTimeCostUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | BreakTimeCost |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [HOPPER] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines how the time costs will be applied. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| DutyTimeCost | Cost Time Expression | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE StepCosts.StepCostBehavior = [All Item, Incremental] |  |  |  | StepCosts |  | [HOPPER] |  |  | 0 |  | The cost per unit of time a driver is on duty. This is additive to any other time costs specified for this driver. | [Cost] | [] | StepCostName |
| DutyTimeCostUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | DutyTimeCost |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [HOPPER] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines how the time costs will be applied. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| StopCost | Cost Time Expression | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE StepCosts.StepCostBehavior = [All Item, Incremental] |  |  |  | StepCosts |  | [] |  |  | 0 |  | The cost incurred by the Driver at each stop. | [Cost] | [] | StepCostName |
| UnitCost | Cost Time Expression | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE StepCosts.StepCostBehavior = [All Item, Incremental] |  |  |  | StepCosts |  | [] |  |  | 0 |  | The unit cost associated with shipments transported. This field can support a single numeric value, a step cost lookup (defined in the Step Costs table), or a custom cost function. | [Cost] | [] | StepCostName |
| UnitCostUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | UnitCost |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines the basis on which UnitCost will be applied for the Asset. Quantity, Volume, and Weight units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| FuelSurcharge | double |  |  |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  | 0 |  | The Fuel surcharge is an additional type of per distance cost. | [Cost] | [] |  |
| FuelSurchargeBasis | [Percentage, Per Distance] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  | Percentage |  | The Basis for the fuel surcharge. If percentage is selected, surcharge is calculated as DistanceCost * (FuelSurcharge / 100). For example, to apply a 5% surcharge you would enter a value of FuelSurcharge = 5, FuelSurchargeBasis = Percentage.   If Per Distance is selected then surcharge is calculated as TotalDistance * FuelSurcharge. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |

### シート: TransportationLaneModeQueueDet0

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| OriginName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Facilities, Suppliers, Sources | Yes | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The origin Facility or Supplier of the lane that the queue details apply to | [NaN] | [] | [Facilities.FacilityName, Suppliers.SupplierName] |
| DestinationName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Customers, Facilities, Destinations | Yes | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The destination Customer or Facility of the lane that the queue details apply to | [NaN] | [] | [Customers.CustomerName, Facilities.FacilityName] |
| ProductName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  | Yes | Products | Yes | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The Product of the lane that the queue details apply to | [NaN] | [] | ProductName |
| ModeName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | TransportationModes | Yes | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The Transportation Mode that the policy record applies to. | [NaN] | [] | ModeName |
| ReviewPeriodFirstTime | Date/Time |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The first Date/Time at which to review the queue for the specified Transportation Lane Mode combination. Lane Mode queues will be evaluated using the logic specified in the Priority Logic field. After this Date/Time, the review period length will dictate when transportation lane queue reviews occur. If NULL, Review Period First Time will be the start of the simulation. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ReviewPeriod | Cost Time Expression | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | ReviewSchedules |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The time between lane mode queue reviews. After Review Period First Time, a lane mode queue review will occur each time Review Period has elapsed. For example, if Review Period First Time is 1/1/2022 12:00:00 AM and Review Period is six hours, we will conduct reviews on 1/1/2022 at 12:00:00 AM, 6:00:00 AM, 12:00:00 PM, and 6:00:00 PM, continuing similarly until the simulation ends. A Review Schedule defined in the Review Schedules table can be entered by Review Schedule Name in this field as well. If NULL, continuous review is used. | [Time] | [] | ReviewScheduleName |
| ReviewPeriodUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines Review Period length. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| QueueingPriorityLogic | [FIFO, ByDueDateAndLocation, ByDayQueuedAndLocation] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | FIFO |  | The logic used for the Transportation Lane Mode combination when deciding how to arrange orders in the shipment queue. If a value of By Date And Location is selected, the QueuePriority fields in the Customers, Facilities, and Suppliers will be leveraged. If the Advanced Queueing Details table is populated, the override priorities for the Origin, Destination, Product combination will be used | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the policy exists in the model. If Status is set to Exclude, the policy record is ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| TimeInQueueCost | double |  |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The cost associated with an item sitting in a queue. The cost expressed in this field will be charged each time that items sit in a queue for the length of time specified in the Time In Queue Cost UOM field. | [Cost] | [] |  |
| TimeInQueueCostUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  | TimeInQueueCost |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure that time is expressed in terms of. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |

This field can support a single numeric value, a step cost lookup (defined in the Step Costs table), transportation rate, or a custom cost function. The cost can be prorated in the event that the estimated number of shipments is not an integer. If the selected rule is Treat as Full, the estimated number of shipments will be rounded up and the fixed cost will be applied accordingly. | [NaN] | [] |  |
