### シート: TransportationPolicies

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| OriginName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Facilities, Suppliers, Customers, Sources | Yes | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The source Facility or Supplier that the policy record applies to. | [NaN] | [THROG, DENDRO, NEO] | [Facilities.FacilityName, Suppliers.SupplierName, Customers.CustomerName] |
| DestinationName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Customers, Facilities, Destinations | Yes | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The Customer or destination Facility that the policy record applies to. | [NaN] | [THROG, DENDRO, NEO] | [Customers.CustomerName, Facilities.FacilityName] |
| ProductName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Products | Yes | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  | ALL |  | The Product that the policy record applies to. If NULL, the policy will be applied to all products in the model. | [NaN] | [NEO] | ProductName |
| ModeName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  | Yes | TransportationModes | Yes | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  | None |  | The Transportation Mode that the policy record applies to. | [NaN] | [THROG] | ModeName |
| OptimizationPolicy | [To Optimize, By Ratio (Auto Scale), By Ratio (No Scale)] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | To Optimize |  | The logic that transportation flows for the Origin, Destination, and Product combination(s) must follow when selecting a Mode. To Optimize selects the lowest cost option. By Ratio (Auto Scale) will enforce a flow split between the specified Modes, the defined ratios will apply to flow into the Facility from the listed Modes only. By Ratio (No Scale) will enforce a flow split between the specified Modes and the defined ratios will be in terms of the total flow between the Origin/Destination pair for the specified product. Note that the Product Name Group Behavior field will be used when applying the policy logic - if a group is entered in the Product Name field and a Group Behavior of Aggregate is selected, the policy logic will apply over the group of products collectively. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| OptimizationPolicyValue | double | PositiveNumeric |  | OptimizationPolicy = By Ratio (Auto Scale) OR By Ratio (No Scale) |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | By Ratio (Auto Scale): The Policy Value will define the percentage of flow that must be satisfied by the listed Mode. The percentage will be calculated based on the total flow between the Origin/Destination pair for the specified product. If the Policy Values do not add up to 100, the ratios will be automatically scaled and enforced.  By Ratio (No Scale): The Policy Value will define the percentage of flow that must be satisfied by the listed Mode. The percentage will be calculated based on the total flow between the Origin/Destination pair for the specified product. If the Policy Values add up to less than 100, the remaining flow between the Origin/Destination pair for the specified product can be satisfied using any other available Modes. | [Percentage] | [] |  |
| SimulationPolicy | [By Preference, By Due Date, On Quantity, On Volume, On Weight, By Cut Off Time, By Cut Off Time Default Last] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | By Preference |  | The logic applied for Mode Selection for specified Source/Destination/Product combinations. By Preference will select Modes, pending availability, in the order of preference specified in the SIM Policy Value field. By Due Date will select the highest priority mode that is available which can reach the destination by the due date of the shipment moving on the lane. On Quantity, On Volume, and On Weight will send shipments on the mode once the value specified in SIM Policy Value is met for the number of items being sent on that shipment. By Cut off Time finds the next mode in the list with a cut off time after the current time.  If no cut off time is after current time then it wraps back to the first time. By Cut Off Time Default Last works the same as By Cut Off Time, but the last policy will be treated as the default rather than wrapping around to the earliest. | [NaN] | [THROG] |  |
| SimulationPolicyValue | double | NonNegative |  | SimulationPolicy = By Preference OR On Quantity OR On Volume OR On Weight |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The values used to define policies. For By Preference and By Due Date, the lower the value the higher the preference. For On Quantity, On Volume, and On Weight the value will reference the shipment amount at which a shipment is sent. These will be expressed in units of EA, CFT, and LB respectively. | [Percentage] | [THROG] |  |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the policy exists in the model. If Status is set to Exclude, the policy record is ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [THROG, DENDRO, NEO] |  |
| UnitCost | Cost Time Expression | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE StepCosts.StepCostBehavior = [All Item, Incremental, Stepwise] |  |  |  | StepCosts, TransportationBandCosting |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | 0 | Optimization: Currently supports doubles and named step costs that are defined in the StepCosts table where StepCostBehavior = All Item, Incremental, Stepwise or a combination of Stepwise and All Item  Simulation: Currently supports doubles | The cost associated with one unit of flow for the Facilities/Product/Mode combination. This field can support a single numeric value, a step cost lookup (defined in the Step Costs table), transportation rate, or a custom cost function. | [Cost] | [THROG, DENDRO, NEO] | [StepCosts.StepCostName, TransportationBandCosting.RateName] |
| UnitCostUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight, Time, Distance, Quantity-Distance, Volume-Distance, Weight-Distance, Quantity-Time, Volume-Time, Weight-Time] |  | UnitCost |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight, Time, Distance, Quantity-Distance, Volume-Distance, Weight-Distance, Quantity-Time, Volume-Time, Weight-Time] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines Unit Cost. UOM Types of Quantity, Volume, Weight, Distance, Time, Quantity-Distance, Quantity-Time, Volume-Distance, Volume-Time, Weight-Distance, and Weight-Time are supported. | [NaN] | [THROG, DENDRO, NEO] | Symbol |
| FixedCost | Cost Time Expression | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE StepCosts.StepCostBehavior = [All Item, Incremental, Stepwise] |  |  |  | StepCosts, TransportationBandCosting |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | 0 | Optimization: Currently supports doubles and named step costs that are defined in the StepCosts table where StepCostBehavior = All Item, Incremental, Stepwise or a combination of Stepwise and All Item  Simulation: Currently supports doubles | The cost associated with a shipment made under the specified Origin/Destination/Product/Mode combination. The Average Shipment Size will be used to estimate the number of shipments that occurred; the Fixed Cost will be applied to the estimated number of shipments according to the Fixed Cost Rule. If a step cost is used, the steps will be evaluated in terms of the number of shipments that are sent over the Origin/Destination/Product/Mode combination. | [Cost] | [THROG] | [StepCosts.StepCostName, TransportationBandCosting.RateName] |
| FixedCostRule | [Prorate, Treat As Full] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Prorate |  | The rule by which Fixed Cost is applied to the estimated number of shipments. The cost can be prorated in the event that the estimated number of shipments is not an integer. If the selected rule is Treat as Full, the estimated number of shipments will be rounded up and the fixed cost will be applied accordingly. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| AverageShipmentSize | double | PositiveNumeric |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | 1 |  | The average shipment size associated with this Origin/Destination/Product/Mode combination. This figure will be used to estimate the fixed cost of shipments and will also be used in combination with any Time or Distance Unit Costs. If NULL, a default value of one unit will be assumed. | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| AverageShipmentSizeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | AverageShipmentSize |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines Average Shipment Size. Quantity, Volume, and Weight units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| ProductNameGroupBehavior | [Enumerate, Aggregate] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Enumerate |  | When determining the number of shipments to apply the Fixed Cost over, Product Name Group Behavior can describe how records for grouped products should be considered. If Enumerate is selected, Fixed Costs will be applied over each product in the group individually. If Aggregate is selected, the total flow of all products in the group will be used to determine how many shipments were sent. The same logic will apply for calculating what level of Step Costs to apply for a Unit Cost | [NaN] | [] |  |
| DutyRate | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | 0 |  | The Duty Rate will be applied as a percentage of the product's value that is moving along this Origin / Destination / Mode combination. For a Duty Rate of 10%, enter a value of 10 into the Duty Rate field | [Percentage] | [] |  |
| FuelSurcharge | double |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | 0 |  | The Fuel surcharge applied to this policy. | [Cost] | [] |  |
| FuelSurchargeBasis | [Percentage, Per Unit, Per Distance] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Percentage |  | The Basis for the fuel surcharge applied to this policy. If percentage is selected, surcharge is calculated as FlowCost * (1 / FuelSurcharge). For example, to apply a 5% surcharge you would enter a value of FuelSurcharge = 5, FuelSurchargeBasis = Percentage.   If Per Unit is selected then surcharge is calculated as FlowQuantity * FuelSurcharge. | [Cost] | [] |  |
| MinProductAge | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The minimum product age will determine the minimum age a product can have while moving along this Origin / Destination / Mode combination. | [Time] | [] |  |
| MinProductAgeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | MinProductAge |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines the length of the Product's Max Age | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| MaxProductAge | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The maximum product age will determine the maximum age a product can have while moving along this Origin / Destination / Mode combination. | [Time] | [] |  |
| MaxProductAgeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | MaxProductAge |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines the length of the Product's Max Age | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| LaneCapacity | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The capacity that applies over the Origin, Destination, Product combination. Capacities can be expressed in terms of flow quantity, volume, weight, or in terms of the number of shipments that are sent along the lane. This capacity will be measured over the time period specified in the Lane Capacity Period field. | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| LaneCapacityUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight, Shipment] |  | LaneCapacity |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight, Shipment] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines the Lane Capacity. UOM Types of Quantity, Volume, Weight, and Shipment are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| LaneCapacityPeriod | double | PositiveNumeric |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The time period over which the Lane Capacity is evaluated. If a Lane Capacity was set at 50 shipments per week, Lane Capacity = 50, Lane Capacity UOM = SHIPMENT, Lane Capacity Period = 1, Lane Capacity Period UOM = WK.  The first period will be evaluated at the Simulation Start Time, and capacity will be evaluated over the specified period length. Capacity is evaluated over individual capacity periods, a rolling capacity is not considered. | [Time] | [] |  |
| LaneCapacityPeriodUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | LaneCapacityPeriod |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines the Lane Capacity Period. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| LaneModeCapacity | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The capacity that applies over the Origin, Destination, Product, Mode combination. Capacities can be expressed in terms of flow quantity, volume, weight, or in terms of the number of shipments that are sent along the lane/mode combination. This capacity will be measured over the time period specified in the Lane Capacity Period field. | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| LaneModeCapacityUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight, Shipment] |  | LaneModeCapacity |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight, Shipment] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines the Lane Mode Capacity. UOM Types of Quantity, Volume, Weight, and Shipment are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| LaneModeCapacityPeriod | double | PositiveNumeric |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The time period over which the Lane Mode Capacity is evaluated. If a Lane Mode Capacity was set at 50 shipments per week, Lane Mode Capacity = 50, Lane Mode Capacity UOM = SHIPMENT, Lane Mode Capacity Period = 1, Lane Mode Capacity Period UOM = WK. The first period will be evaluated at the Simulation Start Time, and capacity will be evaluated over the specified period length. Capacity is evaluated over individual capacity periods, a rolling capacity is not considered. | [Time] | [] |  |
| LaneModeCapacityPeriodUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | LaneModeCapacityPeriod |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines the Lane Mode Capacity Period. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| LoadProcessName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | Processes |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The Processes table record that can be used to add detail to the initialization of the transportation policy at the Source under the specified Origin/Destination/Product/Mode combination. This field allows us to associate processing times, costs, and resource consumption with the beginning of the transportation process. | [NaN] | [] | ProcessName |
| UnloadProcessName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | Processes |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The Processes table record that can be used to add detail to the conclusion of the transportation policy at the Destination under the specified Origin/Destination/Product/Mode combination. This field allows us to associate processing times, costs, and resource consumption with the end of the transportation process. | [NaN] | [] | ProcessName |
| InventoryOwnership | [Origin, Destination] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | This field will determine whether the in-transit inventory costs will be assigned to the origin or the destination for products flowing on this transportation lane. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| InventoryCarryingCostPercentage | double | Percentage |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, CYCLO] |  |  | {InventoryCarryingCostPercentage} |  | The percentage of the product value that the in-transit inventory cost will consider. The in-transit inventory cost will be calculated as an annual cost, with the cost calculation being Product Value * Cost Percentage * (Transport Time in DAY / 365) | [Percentage] | [] |  |
| TimeBetweenDeliveries | double | PositiveNumeric |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The amount of time between delivers for this lane. Given the time between deliveries, the number of deliveries will be calculated for the length of the period. Then, total flow of for the  lane will be divided by the number of deliveries to determine the Transportation Estimated Inventory. In the event that the Time Between Deliveries is longer than the length of the Period, the Transportation Estimated Inventory will be calculated based off of the proportion of the calculated delivery window that falls within the period. | [Time] | [] |  |
| TimeBetweenDeliveriesUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  | TimeBetweenDeliveries |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines the Time Between Deliveries. Time units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| TransportDistance | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The distance of the transportation lane associated with the Origin/Destination/Product/Mode combination. | [Distance] | [NEO] |  |
| TransportDistanceUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Distance] |  | TransportDistance |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] | [Distance] |  | {PrimaryDistanceUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Distance) that defines Transport Distance. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| TransportTime | Cost Time Expression | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  |  | Optimization: Currently supports doubles  Simulation: Currently supports doubles | The time required to travel from Origin to Destination under the Product/Mode combination. | [Time] | [THROG] |  |
| TransportTimeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | TransportTime |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines Transport Time. | [NaN] | [THROG] | Symbol |
| CO2EmissionRate | Cost Time Expression | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] | [] |  | 0 |  | The rate of CO2 emissions for this transportation policy. | [Cost] | [] |  |
| CO2EmissionRateUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight, Distance, Time, Quantity-Distance, Volume-Distance, Weight-Distance, Quantity-Time, Volume-Time, Weight-Time] |  | CO2EmissionRate |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight, Distance, Time, Quantity-Distance, Volume-Distance, Weight-Distance, Quantity-Time, Volume-Time, Weight-Time] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Quantity, Volume, Weight, Distance, Time, Quantity-Distance, Volume-Distance, Weight-Distance, Quantity-Time, Volume-Time, Weight-Time) that defines the CO2 Emission Rate. If a UOM of Type = Distance is used, then the CO2 generated will be based off of the number of shipments - this would be calculated as the (Flow / Shipment Size) * Distance * CO2 Emission Rate. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| RoutePlannerName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | TransportationRoutePlanners |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The route planner name used by this transportation policy | [NaN] | [] | RoutePlannerName |
| MinimumServiceTime | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The minimum service time for the transportation policy. | [Time] | [] |  |
| MinimumServiceTimeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | MinimumServiceTime |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [CYCLO] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines Minimum Service Time. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| MaximumServiceTime | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The maximum service time for the transportation policy. | [Time] | [] |  |
| MaximumServiceTimeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | MaximumServiceTime |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [CYCLO] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines Maximum Service Time. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [NEO] |  |

### シート: TransportationPoliciesMultiTim0

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| PeriodName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Periods | Yes | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The period name in which a change is being made. Groups of periods are also supported. Changes that are made in the Multi Time Period table will be applied as overrides for the records of the base table for the specified periods only. Additionally, only the fields that contain information needing to be updated are required to be entered, in the absence of data in the Multi Time Period table the data from the standard table will be persisted | [NaN] | [] | PeriodName |
| OriginName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Facilities, Suppliers, Customers, Sources | Yes | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The source Facility or Supplier that the policy record applies to. | [NaN] | [] | [Facilities.FacilityName, Suppliers.SupplierName, Customers.CustomerName] |
| DestinationName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Customers, Facilities, Destinations | Yes | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The Customer or destination Facility that the policy record applies to. | [NaN] | [] | [Customers.CustomerName, Facilities.FacilityName] |
| ProductName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | Products | Yes | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The Product that the policy record applies to. If NULL, the policy will be applied to all products in the model. | [NaN] | [] | ProductName |
| ModeName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  | Yes | TransportationModes | Yes | [NEO] |  |  | None |  | The Transportation Mode that the policy record applies to. | [NaN] | [] | ModeName |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the multi-time period record exists in the model. If Status is set to Exclude, the record is ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| PolicyStatus | [Include, Exclude] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | Policy Status dictates whether or not the policy exists in the model for the specified period(s). If Policy Status is set to Exclude, the policy record is ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| OptimizationPolicy | [To Optimize, By Ratio (Auto Scale), By Ratio (No Scale), Single Source] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The logic that transportation flows for the Origin, Destination, and Product combination(s) must follow when selecting a Mode. To Optimize selects the lowest cost option. By Ratio (Auto Scale) will enforce a flow split between the specified Modes, the defined ratios will apply to flow into the Facility from the listed Modes only. By Ratio (No Scale) will enforce a flow split between the specified Modes and the defined ratios will be in terms of the total flow between the Origin/Destination pair for the specified product. Note that the Product Name Group Behavior field will be used when applying the policy logic - if a group is entered in the Product Name field and a Group Behavior of Aggregate is selected, the policy logic will apply over the group of products collectively. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| OptimizationPolicyValue | double | PositiveNumeric |  | OptimizationPolicy = By Ratio (Auto Scale) OR By Ratio (No Scale) |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | By Ratio (Auto Scale): The Policy Value will define the percentage of flow that must be satisfied by the listed Mode. The percentage will be calculated based on the total flow between the Origin/Destination pair for the specified product. If the Policy Values do not add up to 100, the ratios will be automatically scaled and enforced.  By Ratio (No Scale): The Policy Value will define the percentage of flow that must be satisfied by the listed Mode. The percentage will be calculated based on the total flow between the Origin/Destination pair for the specified product. If the Policy Values add up to less than 100, the remaining flow between the Origin/Destination pair for the specified product can be satisfied using any other available Modes. | [Percentage] | [] |  |
| SimulationPolicy | [By Preference, By Due Date, On Quantity, On Volume, On Weight, By Cut Off Time, By Cut Off Time Default Last] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The logic applied for Mode Selection for specified Source/Destination/Product combinations. By Preference will select Modes, pending availability, in the order of preference specified in the SIM Policy Value field. By Due Date will select the highest priority mode that is available which can reach the destination by the due date of the shipment moving on the lane. On Quantity, On Volume, and On Weight will send shipments on the mode once the value specified in SIM Policy Value is met for the number of items being sent on that shipment. By Cut off Time finds the next mode in the list with a cut off time after the current time.  If no cut off time is after current time then it wraps back to the first time. By Cut Off Time Default Last works the same as By Cut Off Time, but the last policy will be treated as the default rather than wrapping around to the earliest. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| SimulationPolicyValue | double | NonNegative |  | SimulationPolicy = By Preference OR On Quantity OR On Volume OR On Weight |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The values used to define policies. For By Preference and By Due Date, the lower the value the higher the preference. For On Quantity, On Volume, and On Weight the value will reference the shipment amount at which a shipment is sent. These will be expressed in units of EA, CFT, and LB respectively. | [Percentage] | [] |  |
| UnitCost | Cost Time Expression | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE StepCosts.StepCostBehavior = [All Item, Incremental, Stepwise] |  |  |  | StepCosts, TransportationBandCosting |  | [NEO] |  |  |  | Optimization: Currently supports doubles and named step costs that are defined in the StepCosts table where StepCostBehavior = All Item, Incremental or Stepwise or a combination of Stepwise and All Item | The cost associated with one unit of flow for the Facilities/Product/Mode combination. This field can support a single numeric value, a step cost lookup (defined in the Step Costs table), transportation rate, or a custom cost function. | [Cost] | [] | [StepCosts.StepCostName, TransportationBandCosting.RateName] |
| UnitCostUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight, Time, Distance, Quantity-Distance, Volume-Distance, Weight-Distance, Quantity-Time, Volume-Time, Weight-Time] |  | UnitCost |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight, Time, Distance, Quantity-Distance, Volume-Distance, Weight-Distance, Quantity-Time, Volume-Time, Weight-Time] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines Unit Cost. UOM Types of Quantity, Volume, Weight, Distance, Time, Quantity-Distance, Quantity-Time, Volume-Distance, Volume-Time, Weight-Distance, and Weight-Time are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| FixedCost | Cost Time Expression | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE StepCosts.StepCostBehavior = [All Item, Incremental, Stepwise] |  |  |  | StepCosts, TransportationBandCosting |  | [NEO] |  |  |  | Optimization: Currently supports doubles and named step costs that are defined in the StepCosts table where StepCostBehavior = All Item, Incremental or Stepwise or a combination of Stepwise and All Item | The cost associated with a shipment made under the specified Origin/Destination/Product/Mode combination. The Average Shipment Size will be used to estimate the number of shipments that occurred; the Fixed Cost will be applied to the estimated number of shipments according to the Fixed Cost Rule. If a step cost is used, the steps will be evaluated in terms of the number of shipments that are sent over the Origin/Destination/Product/Mode combination. | [Cost] | [] | [StepCosts.StepCostName, TransportationBandCosting.RateName] |
| FixedCostRule | [Prorate, Treat As Full] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The rule by which Fixed Cost is applied to the estimated number of shipments. The cost can be prorated in the event that the estimated number of shipments is not an integer. If the selected rule is Treat as Full, the estimated number of shipments will be rounded up and the fixed cost will be applied accordingly. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| AverageShipmentSize | double | PositiveNumeric |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The average shipment size associated with this Origin/Destination/Product/Mode combination. This figure will be used to estimate the fixed cost of shipments and will also be used in combination with any Time or Distance Unit Costs. If NULL, a default value of one unit will be assumed. | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| AverageShipmentSizeUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | AverageShipmentSize |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines Average Shipment Size. Quantity, Volume, and Weight units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| ProductNameGroupBehavior | [Enumerate, Aggregate] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | When determining the number of shipments to apply the Fixed Cost over, Product Name Group Behavior can describe how records for grouped products should be considered. If Enumerate is selected, Fixed Costs will be applied over each product in the group individually. If Aggregate is selected, the total flow of all products in the group will be used to determine how many shipments were sent. The same logic will apply for calculating what level of Step Costs to apply for a Unit Cost | [NaN] | [] |  |
| DutyRate | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The Duty Rate will be applied as a percentage of the product's value that is moving along this Origin / Destination / Mode combination. For a Duty Rate of 10%, enter a value of 10 into the Duty Rate field | [Percentage] | [] |  |

If percentage is selected, surcharge per unit = UnitCost*(1+ FuelSurcharge). If Per Unit is selected then surcharge per unit = FuelSurcharge | [Cost] | [] |  |
| LaneCapacity | double | NonNegative |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The capacity that applies over the Origin, Destination, Product combination. | Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |

| TransportationRateName | Name |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [HOPPER, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The name of the Transportation Rate. This value can be referenced in other tables involving Transportation Rates. | [NaN] | [HOPPER] |  |
| RouteOriginName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  | Yes | Customers, Facilities |  | [HOPPER, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | ALL |  | The location of the origin of the route. Will dictate which rate record will be used to calculate the cost of that route. Should be empty for rate type LocationIndependent | [NaN] | [] | [Customers.CustomerName, Facilities.FacilityName] |

Will dictate which rate record will be used to calculate the cost of that route. Should be empty for rate type LocationIndependent | [NaN] | [] | [Customers.CustomerName, Facilities.FacilityName] |

This is additive to the PerDistanceCost and PerRepositionDistanceCost | [Cost] | [HOPPER] | StepCostName |

| BreakTimeCost | Cost Time Expression | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE StepCosts.StepCostBehavior = [All Item, Incremental] |  |  |  | StepCosts |  | [HOPPER] |  |  | 0 |  | The cost per unit of time during a break after the maximum drive or duty time before short break is reached. | [Cost] | [] | StepCostName |
| BreakTimeCostUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | BreakTimeCost |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [HOPPER] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines how the time costs will be applied. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| DutyTimeCost | Cost Time Expression | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE StepCosts.StepCostBehavior = [All Item, Incremental] |  |  |  | StepCosts |  | [HOPPER] |  |  | 0 |  | The cost per unit of time an asset is on duty. This is additive to any other time costs specified for this asset. | [Cost] | [] | StepCostName |
| DutyTimeCostUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  | DutyTimeCost |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [HOPPER] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines how the time costs will be applied. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| StopCost | Cost Time Expression | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE StepCosts.StepCostBehavior = [All Item, Incremental] |  |  |  | StepCosts |  | [HOPPER, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | 0 |  | The cost incurred by the Asset at each stop. | [Cost] | [] | StepCostName |
| UnitCost | Cost Time Expression | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE StepCosts.StepCostBehavior = [All Item, Incremental] |  |  |  | StepCosts |  | [HOPPER, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | 0 |  | The unit cost associated with flow transported using the specified Asset. This field can support a single numeric value, a step cost lookup (defined in the Step Costs table), or a custom cost function. | [Cost] | [] | StepCostName |
| UnitCostUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | UnitCost |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [HOPPER, THROG, DENDRO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines the basis on which UnitCost will be applied for the Asset. Quantity, Volume, and Weight units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| MinimumCharge | double |  |  |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | The minimum charge for the Asset. The route cost will be set to this value if the other costs add up to less than this minimum charge. When applied, the difference between the minimum charge and the total route cost before application of the minimum charge will be added to the route's fixed cost. | [Cost] | [] |  |
| FuelSurcharge | double |  |  |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  | 0 |  | The Fuel surcharge is an additional type of per distance cost. | [Cost] | [] |  |
| FuelSurchargeBasis | [Percentage, Per Distance] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  | Percentage |  | The Basis for the fuel surcharge. If percentage is selected, surcharge is calculated as DistanceCost * (FuelSurcharge / 100). For example, to apply a 5% surcharge you would enter a value of FuelSurcharge = 5, FuelSurchargeBasis = Percentage.   If Per Distance is selected then surcharge is calculated as RouteDistance * FuelSurcharge. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [HOPPER] |  |

### シート: TransportationRoutePlanners

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| RoutePlannerName | Name |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | Name of the Route Planner | [NaN] | [] |  |
| RoutePlanningMethod | [Fixed, Hopper] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | Hopper |  | The route planning method for this transportation route planner | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ReviewPeriodFirstTime | Date/Time |  |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The first Date/Time at which the Route Planner reviews available shipments.  After this Date/Time, the review period length will dictate when the Route Planner reviews. If NULL, Review Period First Time will be the start of the simulation. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ReviewPeriod | Cost Time Expression | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | ReviewSchedules |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  | The time between Route Planner reviews. After Review Period First Time, a Route Planner review will occur each time Review Period has elapsed. For example, if Review Period First Time is 1/1/2022 12:00:00 AM and Review Period is six hours, we will conduct reviews on 1/1/2022 at 12:00:00 AM, 6:00:00 AM, 12:00:00 PM, and 6:00:00 PM, continuing similarly until the simulation ends. A Review Schedule defined in the Review Schedules table can be entered by Review Schedule Name in this field as well. A Review Schedule defined in the Review Schedules table can be entered by Review Schedule Name in this field as well. If NULL, continuous review is used. | [Time] | [] | ReviewScheduleName |
| ReviewPeriodUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Time] |  |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] | [Time] |  | {PrimaryTimeUOM} |  | The unit of measure (Type = Time) that defines Review Period length. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the Route Planner exists in the model. If Status is set to Exclude, the record is ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |

### シート: TransportationUserDefinedVaria0

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| VariableName | Name |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | The name assigned to the Variable. Users may define variables in this table to capture behaviors which are not directly supported by other constraint tables. User-Defined Variables are  functions of Terms made of native model variables. User-Defined Variables allow a user to create a detailed expression which is then constrained in the User-Defined Constraints table and/or incurs a cost defined in the User-Defined Costs table. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| TermName | String - Fixed |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | The unique name assigned to the Variable Term being defined. The Terms associated with a Variable Name will be summed to give the final Variable value. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| VariableAggregationMethod | [Sum, Min, Max, Avg] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  | Sum |  | The aggregation method used to collect all the Terms of a Variable. For example, VariableAggregationMethod=Sum will sum the values of all the Variable's Terms to produce the value of the Variable. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the Term exists. If Status is set to Exclude, this Term will not be included in the Variable. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Coefficient | double |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  | 1 |  | The coefficient by which to scale the Term. The default value is one. | [Constraint] | [] |  |
| Type | [RouteCount, StopCount, ShipmentCount, ProductCount, Quantity, Weight, Volume, Distance, Time, Variable, IntegerVariable, Constant] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | The Type of behavior the user wishes to capture via the Term. Terms will be related to each other via their coefficients. If the TermName is referencing a variable which has already been defined (TermName = VariableName) select a type of Variable. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Scope | [Route, Stop, Shipment] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | The scope that the variable will be iterated over. For example, a Variable with Type=ShipmentCount and Scope=Route will count the number of shipments in a route. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| TermAggregationMethod | [Sum, Min, Max, Avg] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  | Sum |  | The aggregation method used to collect all the quantities of a Term. For example, TermAggregationMethod=Sum will sum the values of all of the term's quantities to produce the Term value. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| AssetName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | TransportationAssets | Yes | [HOPPER] |  |  | ALL |  | The relevant Transportation Asset. Groups are supported. | [NaN] | [] | AssetName |
| AssetNameGroupBehavior | [Enumerate, Aggregate] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  | Aggregate |  | If AssetName is a Group, use this field to specify the Group's behavior. If Enumerate is selected, an instance of this Variable will be created for each member of the Group. If Aggregate is selected, the activity in the variable will be summed over the members of the Group. If you are using multiple terms for a variable, a group behavior entry of enumerate is only supported for one term. Having multiple terms with enumerations is not currently supported. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| SiteName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | Facilities, Customers | Yes | [HOPPER] |  |  | ALL |  | The site(s) (Facilities or Customers). Groups are supported. | [NaN] | [] | [Facilities.FacilityName, Customers.CustomerName] |
| SiteType | [Pickup, Delivery, Any] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  | Any |  | The activity that is being tracked at the location specified in the SiteName field. This can be one of Pickup, Delivery, or Any. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| SiteNameGroupBehavior | [Enumerate, Aggregate] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  | Aggregate |  | If SiteName is a Group, use this field to specify the Group's behavior. If Enumerate is selected, an instance of this Variable will be created for each member of the Group. If Aggregate is selected, the activity in the variable will be summed over the members of the Group. If you are using multiple terms for a variable, a group behavior entry of enumerate is only supported for one term. Having multiple terms with enumerations is not currently supported. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ShipmentID | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | Shipments | Yes | [HOPPER] |  |  | ALL |  | The relevant Shipment(s). Groups are supported. | [NaN] | [] | ShipmentID |
| ShipmentIDGroupBehavior | [Enumerate, Aggregate] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  | Aggregate |  | If ShipmentID is a Group, use this field to specify the Group's behavior. If Enumerate is selected, an instance of this Variable will be created for each member of the Group. If Aggregate is selected, the activity in the variable will be summed over the members of the Group. If you are using multiple terms for a variable, a group behavior entry of enumerate is only supported for one term. Having multiple terms with enumerations is not currently supported. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ProductName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | Products | Yes | [HOPPER] |  |  | ALL |  | The relevant Product(s). Groups are supported. | [NaN] | [] | ProductName |
| ProductNameGroupBehavior | [Enumerate, Aggregate] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  | Aggregate |  | If Product Name is a Group, use this field to specify the Group's behavior. If Aggregate is selected, the activity in the variable will be summed over the members of the Group. If you are using multiple terms for a variable, a group behavior entry of enumerate is only supported for one term. Having multiple terms with enumerations is not currently supported. | [NaN] | [] |  |

| MaxSequencePosition | Int |  |  |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | The maximum position in the stop sequence. Use negative numbers to count from the end. -1 means last stop in the route | [NaN] | [] |  |
| SequencePositionType | [Pickup, Delivery, Any] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [HOPPER] |  |  | Any |  | If pickup is selected, the min/max positions will only be counted among the pickup stops. Same for Delivery. By default (any) the entire stop sequence is considered. | [NaN] | [] |  |

| UnitOfMeasureName | Name |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, HOPPER, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The name of the Unit of Measure (UOM). Models should be given a set of default UOMs upon creation. Units of Measure will allow for input data in various tables to be expressed in a variety of units, all of which will be able to be converted to a common unit so that the solvers will be able to interpret them. If units of measure aren't specified in input tables, defaults that have been set in the Model Settings table as Primary UOM will be used for the appropriate measurement type. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Type | [Quantity, Volume, Weight, Distance, Time, Area, DaysOfSupply, Shipment, ProcessLot] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, HOPPER, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The measurement type defining the UOM. UOM Type dictates where the UOM can be referenced in other tables. For example, we will not be able to use UOMs of Type = Weight in table fields requiring Volume UOMs. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Symbol | Name |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, HOPPER, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  | The symbol by which the UOM may be referenced in other tables. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Ratio | double | PositiveNumeric | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO, THROG, DENDRO, HOPPER, CYCLO] |  |  | 1 |  | Defines the ratio of the UOM to the specified Type's Primary Unit. For example, the default Primary Unit for Type = Quantity will be Each (EA). As the Primary Quantity Unit, EA will have a ratio of 1; if we define Dozen as another Quantity UOM, we should assign it a ratio of 12 (12 EA per Dozen). In the event that Primary Units are adjusted through the Model Settings table, the ratios will also be adjusted accordingly. If you have defined your own unit of measure, be sure to enter the ratio in alignment with the Primary Unit of the appropriate type that is specified in the Model Settings. | [Percentage] | [] |  |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, HOPPER, CYCLO] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |

### シート: UserDefinedConstraints

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| ConstraintName | Name |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO, HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | The name assigned to the Constraint. This value is purely descriptive; it can increase visibility into the constraints that are implemented into the model in the event that the Variable referenced includes an Enumerated Group. This Constraint Name will be automatically indexed to match the indexed (as a result of Enumeration) instances of the relevant Variable Name. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| VariableName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | UserDefinedVariables, TransportationUserDefinedVariables |  | [NEO, HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | The name of the User-Defined Variable to be constrained. | [NaN] | [] | [UserDefinedVariables.VariableName, TransportationUserDefinedVariables.VariableName] |
| ConstraintType | [Min, Max, Fixed, Fixed With Tolerance, Conditional Min] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO, HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | The type of constraint imposed on the specified Variable.  Min: the Variable must be greater than or equal to the Constraint Value. Max: the Variable must be less than or equal to the Constraint Value. Fixed: the Variable must be equal to the Constraint Value. Fixed With Tolerance: The Variable must be equal to the Constraint Value * (1 +/- Relative Constraint Tolerance). Relative Constraint Tolerance is specified in the Model Run Options. Conditional Min: if the Variable is nonzero, it must be greater than or equal to the Constraint Value. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ConstraintValue | double |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO, HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | The numeric value that the specified Variable will be evaluated against. | [Constraint] | [] |  |
| ConditionVariableName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | UserDefinedVariables, TransportationUserDefinedVariables |  | [NEO, HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | The name of the User-Defined Variable that acts as a Condition on this constraint. The constraint entered in fields VariableName, ConstraintType and ConstraintValue will be applied if and only if this ConditionVariable has a value between ConditionMinValue and ConditionMaxValue. | [NaN] | [] | VariableName |
| ConditionMinValue | double |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | The lower bound value of the range where the condition is satisfied. | [Constraint] | [] |  |
| ConditionMaxValue | double |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | The upper bound value of the range where the condition is satisfied. | [Constraint] | [] |  |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO, HOPPER] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the Constraint exists. If Status is set to Exclude, this Constraint will be ignored in the model solve. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO, HOPPER] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |

| VariableName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  | Yes | UserDefinedVariables, TransportationUserDefinedVariables |  | [NEO, HOPPER] |  |  |  |  | The name of the User-Defined Variable for whose value we wish to incur a cost. | [NaN] | [] | [UserDefinedVariables.VariableName, TransportationUserDefinedVariables.VariableName] |
| UnitCost | Cost Time Expression | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE StepCosts.StepCostBehavior = [All Item, Incremental, Stepwise] |  |  |  | StepCosts |  | [NEO, HOPPER] |  |  | 0 | Optimization: Currently supports doubles and named step costs that are defined in the StepCosts table where StepCostBehavior = All Item, Incremental or Stepwise or a combination of Stepwise and All Item | The cost associated with each unit of the specified Variable. This cost will be multiplied by the value of the Variable; thus, the unit basis for this cost depends on the units that describe the Variable. | [Cost] | [] | StepCostName |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO, HOPPER] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the Cost exists. If Status is set to Exclude, this Cost will be ignored in the model solve. | [NaN] | [] |  |

| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the Forecast will exist in the model run. If Status is set to Exclude, the Forecast is ignored during the model run. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| AggregationStrategy | [Day, Week, Month, Year] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [THROG, DENDRO] |  |  | Day | Simulation: Supports values of Day | Aggregation Strategy will define the level that the Forecast data has been entered in. Forecasts of Daily, Weekly, Monthly or Yearly aggregations will be supported. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| DisaggregationLevel | [5 Day Week, 7 Day Week] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  | 5 Day Week |  | Disaggregation Level will determine how the Forecast data will be spread back out into individual order points. Each of the forecasted buckets will be uniformly distributed across the number of days specified in the Disaggregation Level. | [NaN] | [] |  |

### シート: UserDefinedVariables

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| VariableName | Name |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The name assigned to the Variable. Users may define variables in this table to capture behaviors which are not directly supported by other constraint tables. User-Defined Variables are  functions of Terms made of native model variables. User-Defined Variables allow a user to create a detailed expression which is then constrained in the User-Defined Constraints table and/or incurs a cost defined in the User-Defined Costs table. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| TermName | String - Fixed |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The unique name assigned to the Variable Term being defined. The Terms associated with a Variable Name will be summed to give the final Variable value. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the Term exists. If Status is set to Exclude, this Term will not be included in the Variable. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Coefficient | double |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | 1 |  | The coefficient by which to scale the Term. The default value is one. | [Constraint] | [] |  |
| Type | [Flow, Production, Inventory, FlowCount, ProductionCount, InventoryCount, FacilityCount, WorkCenterCount, Variable, Constant, Definition] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | The Type of behavior the user wishes to capture via the Term. Terms will be related to each other via their coefficients. If the TermName is referencing a variable which has already been defined (TermName = VariableName) select a type of Variable. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| UOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight, DaysOfSupply, Shipment, Quantity-Distance, Volume-Distance, Weight-Distance, Quantity-Time, Volume-Time, Weight-Time] |  | Type = Flow OR Production OR Inventory |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [NEO] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight, DaysOfSupply, Shipment, Quantity-Distance, Volume-Distance, Weight-Distance, Quantity-Time, Volume-Time, Weight-Time] |  | {PrimaryQuantityUOM} |  | The unit of measure that defines Type if Flow, Production, or Inventory is selected. Quantity, Volume, Weight, Shipment, Quantity-Distance, Volume-Distance, Weight-Distance, Quantity-Time, Volume-Time, Weight-Time (if Type = Flow) or Days of Supply (if Type = Inventory) units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| FacilityName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  | Type = Production OR Inventory OR ProductionCount OR InventoryCount OR FacilityCount OR WorkCenterCount |  | Facilities | Yes | [NEO] |  |  | ALL |  | The relevant Facility or Facilities. Groups are supported. Type must be one of [Production, Inventory, ProductionCount, FacilityCount, InventoryCount, WorkCenterCount]. | [NaN] | [] | FacilityName |
| FacilityNameGroupBehavior | [Enumerate, Aggregate] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Aggregate |  | If Facility Name is a Group, use this field to specify the Group's behavior. If Enumerate is selected, an instance of this Variable will be created for each member of the Group. If Aggregate is selected, the flow in the constraint will be summed over the members of the Group. If you are using multiple terms for a variable, a group behavior entry of enumerate is only supported for one term. Having multiple terms with enumerations is not currently supported. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| OriginName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  | Type = Flow OR FlowCount |  | Facilities, Suppliers, Customers, Sources | Yes | [NEO] |  |  | ALL |  | The Origin(s) (Facilities and/or Suppliers and/or Customers) of flow. Groups are supported. Type must be one of [Flow, FlowCount]. | [NaN] | [] | [Facilities.FacilityName, Suppliers.SupplierName, Customers.CustomerName] |
| OriginNameGroupBehavior | [Enumerate, Aggregate] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Aggregate |  | If Origin Name is a Group, use this field to specify the Group's behavior. If Enumerate is selected, an instance of this Variable will be created for each member of the Group. If Aggregate is selected, the flow in the constraint will be summed over the members of the Group. If you are using multiple terms for a variable, a group behavior entry of enumerate is only supported for one term. Having multiple terms with enumerations is not currently supported. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| DestinationName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  | Type = Flow OR FlowCount |  | Customers, Facilities, Destinations | Yes | [NEO] |  |  | ALL |  | The Destination(s) (Facilities and/or Customers) of flow. Groups are supported. Type must be one of [Flow, FlowCount]. | [NaN] | [] | [Customers.CustomerName, Facilities.FacilityName] |
| DestinationNameGroupBehavior | [Enumerate, Aggregate] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Aggregate |  | If Destination Name is a Group, use this field to specify the Group's behavior. If Enumerate is selected, an instance of this Variable will be created for each member of the Group. If Aggregate is selected, the flow in the constraint will be summed over the members of the Group. If you are using multiple terms for a variable, a group behavior entry of enumerate is only supported for one term. Having multiple terms with enumerations is not currently supported. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ProductName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  | Type = Flow OR Production OR Inventory OR FlowCount OR ProductionCount OR InventoryCount |  | Products | Yes | [NEO] |  |  | ALL |  | The relevant Product(s). Groups are supported. Type must be one of [Flow, Production, Inventory, FlowCount, ProductionCount, InventoryCount]. | [NaN] | [] | ProductName |
| ProductNameGroupBehavior | [Enumerate, Aggregate] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Aggregate |  | If Product Name is a Group, use this field to specify the Group's behavior. If Enumerate is selected, an instance of this Variable will be created for each member of the Group. If Aggregate is selected, the flow in the constraint will be summed over the members of the Group. If you are using multiple terms for a variable, a group behavior entry of enumerate is only supported for one term. Having multiple terms with enumerations is not currently supported. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ModeName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  | Type = Flow OR FlowCount |  | TransportationModes | Yes | [NEO] |  |  | ALL |  | The relevant Transportation Mode(s). Groups are supported. Type must be one of [Flow, FlowCount]. If left empty, all flows will be considered regardless of if a Mode is used. To specifically constrain against flows that do not use a Mode, enter a value of 'None' | [NaN] | [] | ModeName |
| ModeNameGroupBehavior | [Enumerate, Aggregate] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Aggregate |  | If Mode Name is a Group, use this field to specify the Group's behavior. If Enumerate is selected, an instance of this Variable will be created for each member of the Group. If Aggregate is selected, the flow in the constraint will be summed over the members of the Group. If you are using multiple terms for a variable, a group behavior entry of enumerate is only supported for one term. Having multiple terms with enumerations is not currently supported. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| WorkCenterName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  | Type = WorkCenterCount |  | WorkCenters | Yes | [NEO] |  |  | ALL |  | The relevant Work Center. Groups are supported. Type must be one of [WorkCenterCount]. If left empty, all productions will be considered regardless of if a Work Center is used. To specifically constrain against productions that do not use a Work Center, enter a value of 'None' | [NaN] | [] | WorkCenterName |
| WorkCenterNameGroupBehavior | [Enumerate, Aggregate] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Aggregate |  | If Work Center Name is a Group, use this field to specify the Group's behavior. If you are using multiple terms for a variable, a group behavior entry of enumerate is only supported for one term. Having multiple terms with enumerations is not currently supported. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| BOMName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable |  |  |  | BillsOfMaterials | Yes | [NEO] |  |  | ALL |  | The relevant Bill(s) of Material. Groups are supported. Type must be one of [Production, ProductionCount]. If left empty, all productions will be considered regardless of if a BOM is used. To specifically constrain against productions that do not use a BOM, enter a value of 'None' | [NaN] | [] | BOMName |
| BOMNameGroupBehavior | [Enumerate, Aggregate] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Aggregate |  | If BOM Name is a Group, use this field to specify the Group's behavior. If you are using multiple terms for a variable, a group behavior entry of enumerate is only supported for one term. Having multiple terms with enumerations is not currently supported. | [NaN] | [] |  |

To specifically constrain against productions that do not use a Process, enter a value of 'None' | [NaN] | [] | ProcessName |
| ProcessNameGroupBehavior | [Enumerate, Aggregate] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Aggregate |  | If Process Name is a Group, use this field to specify the Group's behavior. If Enumerate is selected, an instance of this Variable will be created for each member of the Group. If Aggregate is selected, the flow in the constraint will be summed over the members of the Group. If you are using multiple terms for a variable, a group behavior entry of enumerate is only supported for one term. Having multiple terms with enumerations is not currently supported. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| PeriodName | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  |  | Periods | Yes | [NEO] |  |  | ALL |  | The relevant Period(s). Groups are supported. Type must be one of [Flow, Production, Inventory, FlowCount, ProductionCount, FacilityCount, InventoryCount, WorkCenterCount]. | [NaN] | [] | PeriodName |
| PeriodNameGroupBehavior | [Enumerate, Aggregate] |  |  |  |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Aggregate |  | If Period Name is a Group, use this field to specify the Group's behavior. If Enumerate is selected, an instance of this Variable will be created for each member of the Group. If Aggregate is selected, the flow in the constraint will be summed over the members of the Group. If you are using multiple terms for a variable, a group behavior entry of enumerate is only supported for one term. Having multiple terms with enumerations is not currently supported. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ConsideredInventory | [Prebuild Only, Cycle Only, Prebuild and Cycle] |  |  | Type = Inventory |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  | Prebuild Only |  | The type of inventory that is to be considered when applying the constraint. Prebuild inventory is restricted based on the Ending Prebuild Inventory, and Cycle Inventory is restricted based on the total of Average Turn Estimated Inventory, Average Transportation Estimated Inventory and Average Production Estimated Inventory. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| CountingRule | [Facilities, Origins, Destinations, Products, Modes, WorkCenters, BOMs, Processes, Periods, Origins-Destinations, Origins-Modes, Destinations-Modes, Products-Modes, Origins-Products, Destinations-Products] |  |  | Type = FlowCount OR InventoryCount OR ProductionCount OR FacilityCount OR WorkCenterCount |  |  |  | [NEO] |  |  |  |  | Facilities: Count the number of unique facilities for Inventory Count, Production Count, Facility Count Origins: Count the number of unique Origins for Flow Count Destinations: Count the number of unique Destinations for Flow Count Products: Count the number of unique products for Flow Count, Production Count, Inventory Count Modes: Count the number of unique modes for Flow Count WorkCenters: Count the number of unique Work Centers for Work Center Count BOMs: Count the number of unique BOMs for Production Count Processes: Count the number of unique Processes for Production Count WorkCenters: Count the number of unique work centers for WorkCenter Count Periods: Count the number of unique periods for Flow Count, Inventory Count, Production Count, Facility Count, Work Center Count Origins-Destinations: Count the number of unique Origin-Destination combinations on which flow occurs for the specified Mode/Product/Period combination. | [NaN] | [] |  |

### シート: UtilityCurves

| Column Name | Data Type | Validation Type | Required | RequiredIf | PK | Master Table | Expandable | Technology | AllowableUOMTypes | Relates to keys | Default value | Notes | Explanation | PrecisionCategory | BasicMode | MasterColumn |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| UtilityCurveName | Name |  | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The name given to the Utility Curve. This name may be called in other input tables where Utility Curve lookups can be used. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| UtilityCurveBehavior | [Stepwise, Incremental, All Item] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  | All Item |  | The behavior of the Utility Curve. Most fields support All Item, Incremental and Stepwise as well as the combination of Stepwise and All Item. The only exceptions are: Fixed Startup, Fixed Operating, and Fixed Closing Costs fields will only support a Utility Curve Behavior of Stepwise; the corresponding Cost value will be returned as a one-time cost. Incremental behavior will apply Costs to throughput levels individually. All Item behavior will apply the relevant Cost to the total throughput. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| ThroughputLevel | double | NonNegative | Yes |  | Yes |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  | The minimum throughput level at which the relevant Cost is to be applied. All Utility Curve definitions should start with a throughput level of zero; otherwise, the lowest throughput interval will be enforced as a minimum requirement. | [Quantity] | [] |  |
| ThroughputLevelUOM | Name | ExistsInMasterTable WHERE UnitsOfMeasure.Type = [Quantity, Volume, Weight, Time, Distance] |  | ThroughputLevel |  | UnitsOfMeasure |  | [] | [Quantity, Volume, Weight, Time, Distance] |  | EA |  | The unit of measure that defines Throughput Level. Quantity, Volume, Weight, Time and Distance units of measure are supported. | [NaN] | [] | Symbol |
| Value | double |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  |  | [] | [] |  |
| Status | [Include, Exclude] |  | Yes |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  | Include |  | Status dictates whether or not the table record exists. If Status is set to Exclude, this record will not be considered when constructing Utility Curves. | [NaN] | [] |  |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |

Risk Profiles are configured to run using specified source data in the Risk Rating Configurations table, and will take the risk weightings and bands associated with the Risk Profile from each of the configuration tables. | [NaN] | [] | RiskProfileName |
| ThroughputUtilizationRiskWeight | double | NonNegative | Yes |  |  |  |  | [DART, NEO, TRIAD] |  |  |  |  | The weight applied to the Throughput Utilization Risk when calculating the Utilization Risk Score for Facilities and Suppliers. | [Risk] | [] |  |

| StorageUtilizationRiskWeight | double | NonNegative | Yes |  |  |  |  | [DART, NEO] |  |  |  |  | The weight applied to the Storage Utilization Risk when calculating the Utilization Risk Score for Facilities. | [Risk] | [] |  |

| WorkCenterUtilizationRiskWeight | double | NonNegative | Yes |  |  |  |  | [DART, NEO] |  |  |  |  | The weight applied to the Work Center Utilization Risk when calculating the Utilization Risk Score for Facilities. | [Risk] | [] |  |
| WorkCenterUtilizationRiskBand | Name | ExistsInMasterTable | Yes |  |  | RiskBandDefinitions |  | [DART, NEO] |  |  |  |  | The Risk Band that is used to evaluate the Work Center Utilization Risk for Facilities. Risk Bands are defined in the Risk Band Definitions table. | [NaN] | [] | BandName |
| Notes | String |  |  |  |  |  |  | [DART, NEO, TRIAD] |  |  |  |  |  | [NaN] | [] |  |
